The monoknife has the following profile: (Melee; Damage +1; Critical 3; Range [Engaged]; Pierce 1). Characters upgrade the difficulty of checks to detect a concealed escape kit twice.
Concealed Recorder: A surreptitiously captured moment can be turned into evidence that wins wars, topples governments, or simply persuades a certain person to remain silent.
Concealing Robes: Many ancient Jedi Knights favored heavy concealing robes for their anonymity, and now Force users throughout the galaxy use them to avoid notice.
Concussion Grenade: Rather than relying on shrapnel or heat, concussion grenades depend on an explosive detonation's shock wave to cause damage.
Concussion Missile: Concussion missiles unleash a powerful explosion upon impact, killing targets nearby and incapacitating those further away with punishing shock waves.
Condenser Unit: Condensers use vaporator technology to provide both fresh water and a smokeless heat source in one portable package. [...] A condenser can collect enough water each day to sustain a single individual, and its power cell lasts roughly six months.
Consolidated Learning Systems Transliter: The vast majority of known galactic species speak or understand Basic. A transliterator removes two setback dice from any Charm, Coercion, or Negotiation checks that are added due to linguistic difficulties or misunderstandings.
Contained Energy Ax: Until activated, a contained energy ax is easily mistaken for a common vibro-ax. A contained energy ax can use appropriate Melee weapon attachments, but not lightsaber attachments.
Copora-Tech Thermal Cutter: Designed for cold-weather expeditions, the Copora-Tech Thermal Cutter is basically a scaled-down mining beamdrill. A thermal cutter's charge lasts for roughly five minutes of continuous use, and can remove one cubic meter of snow per minute, or dig through 10 centimeters of ice per minute. If plugged into a fusion lantern, it can run indefinitely, otherwise it requires a new battery. Nefarious individuals can also use a thermal cutter as a weapon with the following profile (Melee; Damage 4; Critical 4; Range [Engaged]; Burn 1, Inaccurate 2).
Corellian Arms CR-2 Heavy Blaster Pistol: Produced by Corellian Arms, the CR-2 heavy blaster pistol is a somewhat outdated weapon.
Corellian Arms CR-8 Takedown Rifle: The CR-8 is a high-performance blaster rifle designed for use by CorSec snipers and secret agents. When a CR-8 is properly broken-down and concealed, add two setback dice to a character's Perception checks made to find it on a person's body.
Corellian Compound Bow: A massive bow made of composite materials, the compound bow uses a system of cams and cables to lever the arms and reduce the draw weight when fully drawn, allowing for longer, more accurate aiming.
Corellian Cutlass: Traditionally granted to Corellian naval officers upon being promoted from ensign or sub-lieutenant to full lieutenant, the Corellian cutlass is a relatively short broad saber with a solid hand guard.
Corellian Whiskey: A popular alcoholic drink from Corellia, distilled anywhere from three to twenty times and aged for several years in wooden casks. Whyren's Reserve, an expensive label aged for up to two decades, is considered the finest Corellian whiskey available and often traded on the black market.
Coronet Arms HL-27 Light Blaster Pistol: The HL-27 is a sterling example of the superior craftsmanship of weapons produced by Coronet Arms.
Corrupted Crystal: [Dark side] Force users bend the kyber crystals in their lightsabers to their will, leading to the crimson hue of blades produced by these corrupted crystals.
Cortex Bomb: Cortex bombs are last-resort measures to keep an agent from breaking under interrogation. Cortex bombs detonate when the implanted individual speaks, hears, or sees a predetermined code word or signal, and they kill only the wearer. They deal very little discernible damage to the target, though, and no damage to the surrounding area.
Cortosis Forging: For most, dueling a Jedi is a fool's errand, and even more so in close combat, where lightsabers dictate the terms of battle.
Cortosis Shield: Those who deal with the remnants of the Jedi Order, such as the Inquisitors and the secret police units of the Galactic Empire, often have need of specialized equipment to reduce the effectiveness of suspected Jedi and their lightsabers.
Cortosis Staff (Refined): In most forms, cortosis ore resists and absorbs energy, allowing items to withstand blows from weapons that would otherwise shatter them. Furthermore, cortosis refined in a particular way can short out lightsaber blades on contact.
Cortosis Sword: The making and use of cortosis swords is, perhaps, as old as the making and use of lightsabers.
Cortosis Weave: Although cortosis weave is rare, and most combatant haven't even heard of it, some armorers shape cortosis ore into this energy-resistant mesh.
Coupling Belts: When activated, these paired belts are tethered by a power coupling. The wearers are drawn together by the coupling, adding a level of complexity to most physical tasks. They have been used in both formal gladiatorial tournaments as well as small town bar brawls. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Cracian Thumper: Exported from Craci in the Corporate Sector, Cracian thumpers have become one of the most widespread riding animals in the galaxy.
Cracked Crystal: These crystals may be flawed, cracked, or otherwise damaged. This item cannot be bought; it must be found. Base Modifiers: Installing this crystal changes a lightsaber's base damage to 7, its critical rating to 3, and the lightsaber gains the Breach 2, Sunder, and Vicious 1 item qualities. If a combat check made with this lightsaber results in a despair result, the GM may cause the crystal to shatter, after which the lightsaber loses these qualities and reverts to its previous base damage and critical rating. If the crystal is ever removed, the lightsaber loses these qualities and reverts to its previous base damage and critical rating. Modification Options: 1 Decrease the weapon's critical rating by 1 to a minimum of 1 Mod, 2 Item Quality (Vicious +1) Mods.
Cracked Jedha Crystal: When a terrible mining accident destroyed Jedha City, the galaxy lost one of the last known sources of kyber crystals. Base Modifiers: Installing this crystal changes a lightsaber's damage to 7 and critical rating to 3, and the lightsaber gains the Breach 1, Inaccurate 2, Sunder, and Vicious 1 qualities. If the crystal is ever removed, the lightsaber loses these qualities and reverts to its previous base damage and critical rating. Modification Options: 3 Item Quality (Vicious +1) Mods, 2 Damage +1 Mods, 1 Item Quality (Inaccurate –1) Mod. 1 Decrease the weapon's critical rating by 1 to a minimum of 1 Mod.
Crash Gear: Most sets of crash gear consist of an assortment of protective wear designed to allow a user to better survive the inevitable collisions and buffeting that are part of every race or mission.
Crash Survival Kit: Popular among pilots and vehicle crews, crash survival kits are a necessary precaution for those who regularly travel through inhospitable areas.
Credit Cleaner: While Imperial officials generally turn a blind eye to the various dealings of the Hutts and their associates, those who traffic in high-value illegal goods and services often find it beneficial to make sure that their income cannot be easily traced.
Creereen: Small, flight-capable mammals that dwell in tight-knit nesting groups of up to a dozen individuals, creereen survive the attention of predators through their mobility and alertness.
Cresh \"Luck\" Armor: Creshaldyne riot armor is a popular choice among police and paramilitary units across the galaxy. Cresh Luck armor adds an automatic advantage result to the wearer's Vigilance checks.
Creshaldyne EOD-Mk II Explosives Disposa: EOD-Mk II armor by Creshaldyne is one of the most popular explosives disposal suits in the galaxy. Due to its extremely thick padding and ponderous form, character wearing EOD-Mk II armor lose their free movement. EOD-Mk II Upgrade Options: EOD-MK II suits lack any standard modification options, but can be upgraded with two valuable attachments. These attachments are integrated load bearing gear and a data link for controlling EOD and demolitions remotes, such as the EOD-Mk IV. As the suits are designed for these upgrades, neither takes up any hard points for the armor, but each upgrade can only be taken once. Integrated load-bearing gear reduces the suit's encumbrance by 3, and costs an additional 700 credits with a rarity of 3. The data link allows the wearer to control a single EOD remote and costs an extra 300 credits with a rarity of 4.
Creshaldyne Mk. IV Modular Backpack: The convenient Mk. IV Modular Backpack is manufactured by Creshaldyne Industries and marketed almost exclusively to military and governmental forces.
Crossguard Lightsaber: Crossguard lightsabers were once common among Makashi Duelists, many of whom fought at the Great Scourge of Malachor. Whenever an enemy engaged with a character armed with a crossguard lightsaber makes a Melee or Lightsaber check, after the attack is resolved, the character may spend three threat results or a despair result to hook their opponent's blade and disarm them. Aside from losing the weapon, any other effects of being disarmed are left to the GM's discretion. This lightsaber is a crossguard lightsaber hilt containing an unmodded Ilum lightsaber crystal. This crystal occupies two of the weapon's hard points.
Crossguard Lightsaber Hilt: [A lightsaber hilt] requires a crystal to become a true weapon (otherwise they deal no damage, have no critical rating, and do not have many of their item qualities).
Crunchies: Produced by LaKiave Amalgamated Foodstuffs, Crunchies are a breakfast cereal popular among humans. Blue and brown in colour, these sugary pebbleshaped, spherical treats are usually served in bowls of blue milk. The mascot for Crunchies is CRUNCH-0, the cereal loving droid. Though during the Clone Wars, CRUNCH-0 was briefly retired in favour of the Clone Commander Crunch. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Crushgaunts: Crushgaunts, also called shuk'orok in Mando'a, are strength-enhancing gloves designed centuries ago by the Mandalorians.
CryoBan Projector: CryoBan projectors like TaggeCo.'s spew a mix of chemicals that create areas of intense cold. A character armed with a CryoBan projector adds a boost die to attempts to put out a fire. Assisted checks to extinguish flames add one additional boost die for each assisting character equipped with a CryoBan projector.
CryoBan Rifle: Loronar's CryoBan rifle is in the tradition of Ulban Arms and several other manufacturers who have weaponized CryoBan technology.
CryonCorp EnhanceScan Long-Range Terrain: The EnhanceScan Long-Range Terrain Scanner was a device that uses a low-power ground-following radar to measure and produce a topographical map of a planetary surface in very little time. It is often mounted to speeders to accelerate the mapping process.
CryonCorp Ground-Penetrating Sensor Pack: Another sensor suite produced by CryonCorp, the GPSP is a powerful ground-penetrating radar suite marketed to companies involved in gas and minerals exploration and chartered search and rescue.
CryonCorp Mk. I Cat's Eye Multipurpose S: CryonCorp is a relative newcomer to the galactic technology market, having been founded in the last decade of the Clone Wars.
Cultural Etiquette Manual: Diplomats rely on protocol droid to ensure not only fluent translation, but also prevent embarrassing cultural incidents. Models include: Baobab Archives Compendiums.
Curved Hilt: The curved hilt is preferred by duelists and practitioners of the second lightsaber form, Makashi. This attachment can only be applied to lightsabers. Base Modifiers: Adds an automatic advantage result to successful Lightsaber combat checks when the wielder is engaged with a single opponent. Modification Options: 1 Item Quality (Defensive +1) Mod.
Custom Fit: Although standard "off-the-shelf" armor can provide invaluable protection, it can also prove bulky or awkward, even after adjustments.
Custom Grip: A skilled tinkerer can tailor a weapon to perfectly fits its wielder's hand. This attachment can be applied to any weapon with a handle. Base Modifiers: Remove a setback die from all combat checks the weapon's owner makes using this weapon. Anyone other than the owner instead adds two setback dice to all combat checks made using it. Modification Options: 1 Item Quality (Accurate +1) Mod.
Custom Tailored Armored Jacket: Kamperdine Clothing Specialists is a boutique manufacturer of custom clothing. Kamperdine armored jackets are each tailored to a specific wearer. When worn by that wearer, they add an advantage result to any successful Charm, Deception, or Negotiation checks the character makes (the GM may decide that the bonus does not apply in certain situations, such as dealings over a comlink).
Custom Tool Kit: For every job there is a proper tool, and the bigger the job, the more tools a technician needs. Using a custom tool kit grants the user for whom it was built a boost die to any skill check made to maintain or repair an item, computer, weapon, vehicle, or starship. A character attempting to use custom tools made for someone else suffers a setback die to the same checks. Custom tools can be customized for another user with extensive, and often expensive, modification.
Cyanoxis D-570: Cyanoxis D-570 was originally developed by Toxico as an all-purpose rodenticide. When used as an assassination tool, D-570 is typically administered through food and drink. One dose of Cyanoxis D-570 requires a character to make a Hard difficulty Resilience check to overcome. On a failed check, the poison inflicts 2 wounds and 2 strain immediately, plus 1 wound and 1 strain per day for ten days or until the target succumbs to death. Each threat result generated inflicts 1 additional strain per day as internal blood loss takes its toll on the target's constitution. A despair result can be spent to increase the number of days the poison stays in effect by one.
Cyber Disguise: A cybernetic disguise implant is composed of a series of systems embedded beneath an individual's skin that allow their users to alter their appearance.
Cybernetic Arms (Mod V and Mod VI): Built by companies such as BioTech to fit nearly every sentient species in the galaxy, cybernetic arms are designed to increase an individual's strength or agility, depending on the model.
Cybernetic Brain Implant: These enhancements are miniaturized computer implants designed to augment the user's brainpower. A cybernetic brain implant provides +1 Intellect and includes a comlink and computer access link.
Cybernetic Cavity: Some smugglers go to great lengths to conceal valuable cargo, including hiding it in their own bodies. A cybernetic cavity provides 1 rank of Hidden Storage talent. A character with this cybernetic can use the Hidden Storage talent to conceal items within [their] own body.
Cybernetic Eyes: Cybernetic eyes were designed to restore sight to beings who have been blind since birth or have lost their sight due to disease or accident.
Cybernetic Legs (Mod II and Mod III): To assist those who have lost legs to accident or disease, many biotechnology companies produce replacement cyberlegs. Mod II cyberlegs provide +1 Brawn, and Mod III cyberlegs provide +1 Agility. Both legs must be replaced to gain these benefits. Both legs must be the same model, as they are designed to work in tandem. Modifiers from both legs do not stack.
Cybernetic Reflexes: A series of specialized implants designed to stimulate and help regulate the sympathetic nervous system of the user, cybernetic reflexes can greatly increase an individual's reaction time to outside stimuli and threats.
Cybernetic Respirator: For those organics seeking to escape fleshy frailties, cybernetic respirators offer an alternative to lungs. A cybernetic respirator permanently provides a character with the benefits of a breath mask and respirator and one rank of the Blooded talent.
Cybernetic Weapon Implant: Popular among bounty hunters, assassins, and clandestine operatives, cybernetic weapon implants guarantee that no matter the situation, an individual is never without a weapon.
Cyberscanner Limb: More than a simple cybernetic replacement, this cybernetic arm includes a basic scanner for medium-range detection. The Mod IV Scanner Limb incorporates a portable scanner (such as the general purpose scanner) and, unlike most cybernetics, is concealed by a synthflesh covering to disguise that there is anything out of the ordinary about the limb.
Cybot Galactica/KDY All-Terrain Explorat: The new All-Terrain Exploration Droid by Cybot Galactica is designed to assist in the exploration and surveying of newly discovered planets.
Cybot Galactica K-9 Series Hunting Droid: To aid hunters, Cybot Galactica sells the K-9 series hunting-and-tracking droid. Skills: Brawl 3, Perception 3, Stealth 3, Survival 3. Talents: Hunter 2 (adds two boost dice to all skill checks when interacting with wild beasts and animals, including combat checks. This character adds +20 to all Critical Injury rolls against animals). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink, and can survive indefinitely underwater or in vacuum. Immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: Teeth and claws (Brawl; Damage 5; Critical 5; Range Engaged; Pierce 1), Built-in Fabritech "Hound" sensor suite (allows the droid to see in dim and dark conditions and to hear sounds far above and below a human's range of hearing at great distances. Add a boost die to any Perception or Survival checks).
Cyclic Crystal Array: Lightsabers rely on kyber crystals attuned to the wielder to create energy blades of different types. This attachment can only be applied to lightsabers. Base Modifiers: Allows installation of a second lightsaber crystal at no additional hard point cost. Whenever the wielder ignites the lightsaber, they choose one of the crystals to be the active crystal that produces the blade; any other crystals installed have no effect. As a maneuver, while the lightsaber is not ignited, the wielder may switch any one lightsaber crystal in the hilt to be the active crystal that produces the blade. Modification Options: 1 Install a third lightsaber crystal at no additional hard point cost Mod.
Czerka \"Peacekeeper\" Stun Baton: Czerka Arms sells "Peacekeeper" stun batons to law enforcement organizations across the galaxy, but they also see substantial sales to private buyers.
Czerka Arms 411 Holdout Blaster: Developed for use by Imperial agents, the 411 holdout blaster's supercharged actuator module and tight muzzle emitter give it impressive range for a holdout blaster.
Czerka Arms KS-23 Hammer: The KS-23 is yet another popular slugthrowing design by Czerka Arms.
Czerka Arms Model 57 \"Homesteader\" Hun: With a reputation for sturdy, reliable, no-nonsesne products, Czerka Arms has produced slugthrowers for the civilian and military market for millennia.
Czerka BC-7 Environmental Cocoon: Primarily intended for hunters and survivalists, an environmental cocoon provides a secure space in an otherwise hostile locale.
Czerka CZ-28 Flamestrike: A direct and blatant copy of the Merr-Sonn CR-28 flame cannon, the CZ-28 Flamestrike heavy weapon is a powerful flame projector with an effective range double that of standard flame projectors.
D-24 \"Inferno\" Grenade: Merr-Sonn Munitions' D-24 "Inferno" grenade is an incendiary device that creates intense heat, easily igniting any and all flammable materials within its blast radius.
D-29 Repulsor Rifle: Repulsor weapons use ubiquitous repulsor engines, similar to those found on landspeeders and speeder bikes, tuned to create narrower, longer-range areas of effect..
D-5 Detonation Pack: The D-5 detonation pack is a self-contained explosive package produced by Merr-Sonn Munitions Inc. for use by industrial interests. It uses a pair of stabilised baradium half-charges, containing them in boxy structures on either side of a central signal receiver and detonator matrix. Due to its intended use as a demolitions charge, once active it produces a bright and flashing red light from the signal receiver unit. Despite this, the D-5 has been picked up widely for use by armed forces across the galaxy as a quick and easy anti-vehicle weapon. Due to its high yield, it has been made illegal for civilian ownership on most worlds. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
DC-12U Beam Rifle: BlasTech's DC-12U is an uncommon weapon designed for underwater operations. The under-barrel mount typically mounts a high-powered flashlight that removes a setback die due to darkness from any combat checks made with the weapon. When outside of an aquatic environment, the DC-12U has the Inaccurate 2 item quality.
DC-15A Blaster Carbine: BlasTech's DC-15A is the GAR's standard-issue long-arm. (the price is 100 credits more expensive than the printing in Collapse of the Republic)
DC-15LE Heavy Blaster Rifle: Iterating on BlasTech Industries’ DC-15, the LE model was intended to turn the already excellent—albeit cumbersome—rifle into the equivalent of a light repeater. With the LE signifying ‘light expansion’, this particular model had its plating reinforced. The resulting rifle was a thicker and almost ‘upscaled’ version of the DC-15. The additional build strength allowed for the powercell and spincycle to be overclocked for additional stopping power and fire rate. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
DC-15 Blaster Rifle: The DC-15 was, before the introduction of the DC-15A carbine, the standard issue longarm of the GAR.
DC-15s Side Arm Blaster: Created by BlasTech Industries for use by the Grand Army of the Republic, this light blaster pistol was exclusively used by clone commandos. Designed as an efficient backup weapon system which could recharge itself on the field, it was used for conserving ammunition or for particularly close quarters combat. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
DC-15x Sniper Rifle: When clones speak of using the DC-15x sniper rifle they don’t usually distinguish between what amounts to a DC-15 with an improved scope and sighting system, and the actual DC-15x manufactured for dedicated sniper units. BlasTech’s specially designed DC-15x heavily strips down the excess armour and cooling coils of the base rifle. This allows it to be more single-shot accurate, with a loss of sustained fire abilities. The telescopic sight installed on this rifle keeps snipers in a familiar environment as it is largely identical to that installed by non-dedicated sharpshooter units. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
DC-17 Hand Blaster: The DC-17 is the standard-issue sidearm of the GAR's special forces units. Game Masters may spend three threat results (in addition to a despair result) to make a DC-17 run out of ammo during combat.
DC-17m InterChangeable Weapon System: Specifically designed for use by the SO BDE, this BlasTech Industries proprietary design was marked as a great success by all who used it. Built with similar components to the carbine produced for the regular infantry, the base model is effectively a compact assault rifle. What makes this weapon special is that it serves as a platform for multiple systems. As a standard, the ICWS was shipped with an extended barrel sniper mode and a sort of underslung armour penetrating grenade launcher as an anti-tank mode. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
DC-19 \"Stealth\" Carbine: In need of a true stealth operations weapon, BlasTech Industries was contracted to provide the Republic with a weapon that would create virtually no noise or visual trace when fired. They used the DC-15a carbine as a starting point, adding on a proprietary sound suppressor unit. Then, in a technique which would be replicated by the Xerrol Corporation in their Nightstinger line of hunting rifles, BlasTech engineers developed a special mixture of tibanna gas. The mixture resulted in blaster bolts which emitted no light to the naked eye, making them virtually invisible. The carbine was given a optional stealth mode which would dispense an additive to the gas before it was used to fire. This made reloads for the weapon expensive, as they were only available directly from BlasTech; and the resulting shot emitted much more heat into the weapon.
DDC-MR6 Modular Rifle: A modular rifle that the Drearian Defense Conglomerate designed for the Confederacy of Independent Systems during the Clone Wars, the MR6 comes with an electromagnetic rail system that enables the user to reconfigure quickly for different situations.
DE-10 Blaster Pistol: The DE-10 is a stylish and reliable heavy blaster produced by BlasTech subsidiary Antrech Arms, specialists in boutique and niche products.
DF-D1 Duo-Flechette Rifle: Salus Corporation is a Rodia-based arms and armor manufacturer known for its specialty weaponry. Its DF-D1 duo-flechette rifle is inspired by Golan Arms' FC-1 flechette launcher.
DH-17 Blaster Carbine: BlasTech's popular DH-17 blaster pistol spawned an entire line of weapons after landing large contracts with the Empire and thousands of planetary security forces.
DH-X Heavy Blaster Rifle: One of BlasTech's heaviest rifles, the DH-X heavy blaster rifle is designed to punch smashball-sized holes through any target in sight.
DH77 Headcomm: Standard issue for Imperial Army platoon leaders and also installed inside stormtrooper helmets, SoroSuub's DH77 headcomm is an easy to use system with complex encryption subroutines.
DL-19C Blaster Pistol: While most weapons engineers strive for power, accuracy, capacity, and durability from blaster designs, BlasTech's DL-19C design team chose to elevate customization above all else.
DLS-12 Heavy Blaster Carbine: Designed for the Republic during the final months of the Clone Wars, BlasTech's DLS-12 is a carbine version of the DLT-19 heavy blaster rifle.
DLT-18 Blaster Rifle: The DLT-18 were manufactured by BlasTech industries, but never saw as wide a distribution as many of their other rifles due to their weight and length, which made them difficult to carry comfortably. Due to these drawbacks, they were commonly seen in use by combat droids, or more physically imposing sentient species, such as Houks or Trandoshans This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
DLT-19D Heavy Blaster Rifle: The DLT-19D is a weapon BlasTech made specifically for the Imperial Navy's death troopers. The integrated optics counts as a scope and reduces the difficulty of Ranged combat checks at long and extreme range once.
DN Bolt Caster: An evolution of a breaching tool developed by Drever Corp, the DN bolt caster used plasma disruptor technology to great effect against droid opponents. It was also capable of damaging organic targets, with less effectiveness. The weapon ended up being particularly expensive, but the Grand Army of the Republic quickly scooped up many of these weapons once the Clone Wars began. Due to their limited production, they were handed to special units for self defence, such as vehicle clone pilots who might see combat when their ride was disabled. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
DP-23 Scatter Blaster: The success of the close range and multi-target bolt caster had the Republic seeking similar weapons in a cheaper package. BlasTech Industries was quick to respond to this new demand, given how lucrative their partnership with the GAR had been thus far. The DP-23 was yet another weapon using the DC-15’s frame as a design basis. A stubby and simple redesign, the DP-23 concentrated the punch of a DC-15 into a tightly spread shot, increasing armour piercing capabilities while sacrificing range. At longer ranges, it could be used to sweep through groups of droids. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
DR-45 \"Dragoon\" Cavalry Blaster: The DR-45 "Dragoon" Cavalry Blaster has its origins in the popular Merr-Sonn Munitions DD6 blaster pistol. Converting the DR-45 from a pistol to a carbine takes one maneuver and changes the weapon's combat skill from Ranged (Light) to Ranged (Heavy). Similarly, it takes one maneuver to convert the weapon back, restoring its combat skill to Ranged (Light).
DSD1 Dwarf Spider Droid: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Skills: Gunnery 2, Perception 2, Vigilance 1. Talents: None. Abilities: All-Terrain Walker (the DSD1 ignores the penalties of difficult terrain; can walk along vertical and upside down surfaces), Droid (does not need to breathe, eat or drink, and can survive in vacuum or underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Silhouette 2. Equipment: Integrated heavy blaster cannon (Gunnery; Damage 15; Critical 2; Range [Long]; Auto-fire, Pierce 2, Vicious 1), built-in sensor & comms suite (adds a boost die to Perception & Vigilance checks).
DTS-series Dismantler Droid: Industrial Automaton's DTS-series dismantler droids are a common sight in the galaxy's ports and breaking yards. Skills: Computers 3, Mechanics 4, Perception 3, Ranged (Heavy) 1. Talents: Adversary 1 (upgrade the difficulty of all combat checks against this target once), Gearhead 1 (remove a setback die from all Mechanics check), Solid Repairs 2 (repair two additional points of hull trauma when repairing ships or vehicles). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Flyer, Silhouette 2.
DUM-Series Pit Droid: Produced in huge numbers by Serv-O-Droid, the ubiquitous DUM-series droids are a common sight in workshops, mechanics' garages, and racing pits all across the galaxy.
DZ-70 Fugitive Tracker Droid: The DZ-70 by Arakyd Industries is one of the most popular tracking droids on the market today. Skills: Ranged (Light) 2, Stealth 2, Survival 3, Vigilance 2. Talents: Adversary 1 (upgrade all combat checks targeting this character once), Expert Tracker 2 (Remove two setback dice from checks to find or follow tracks. Survival checks made to track targets take 50% less time than normal). Abilities: None. Equipment: Blaster Pistol (Ranged [Light]; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Medium]; Stun Setting), Ionization Blaster (Ranged [Light]; Damage 10; Critical 5; Range [Short]; Disorient 5, Stun Damage [Droid Only]).
Dai Bendu Holocron: At the surface level, Dai Bendu holocrons resemble those produced by the Jedi. [...] Each holocron contains six tracts, subdivided into four lessons apiece. [...] Completing each lesson in the tract requires an amount of study and meditation up to the GM's decision. At its conclusion, the player must roll a Discipline check of one higher difficulty than it took to access that lesson. For example, to complete the first lesson, a Mystic must roll an Average difficulty Discipline check. To complete the final lesson in a tract requires a Formidable difficulty Discipline check. When a Force-sensitive character completes a tract, the player selects one characteristic. Once per day, they may upgrade the ability dice pool of any single check related to that characteristic by two steps. The player has learned all they can from that tract, and must move on to another.
Dalgo: The imposing dalgo is a riding beast native to Onderon and has a long history as both a beast of burden and a powerful mount.
Dampener Aerosol: An expensive consumable produced by Rafkind Industrials, the dampener aerosol was sold in a can akin to the old smoke grenades. These containers were compartmentalised in such a way that activating the mistifier would only consume a third of the capacity. When activated, it would produce a thick blue mist which would conceal the user and was specifically designed to interact with blaster energy—absorbing bolts and rendering them considerably less powerful. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Damping Emitter: Before the Jedi's destruction, many Jedi elected to modify their hilts to reduce the amount of energy their blade projected.
Dantari Crystal: As their name suggests, Dantari crystals are found on the Outer Rim world of Dantooine. Base Modifiers: Installing this crystal changes a lightsaber's base damage to 7 and critical rating to 2, and the lightsaber gains the Breach 1 and Sunder weapon qualities. When making a Force power check as part of a combat check, a character using a lightsaber with a Dantari crystal may spend a Force point to recover 2 strain. If the crystal is ever removed, the lightsaber loses these qualities and abilities, and it reverts to its previous base damage and critical rating. Modification Options: 2 Damage +1 Mods, 1 Decrease the weapon's critical rating by 1 to a minimum of 1 Mod.
Dart Gun: This is a simple device that uses springs or compressed air to fire a tiny alloy dart. Base Modifiers: Enables the item to fire a single dart with the following profile: (Ranged [Light]; Damage 2; Critical 5; Range [Short]; Limited Ammo 1, Pierce 1). Modification Options: None.
Dart Launcher: While some martial artists have become experts at throwing darts, most users still prefer a spring-loaded, gas or magnetic propulsion dart launcher, which increases the range at which the dart flies. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Darth Vader's Armor: Completely encased in an ebony suit of custom life support armor, Darth Vader cuts a fearsome silhouette and generates an air of mystery; the strained, unnerving gasps of his respiratory implant the only insight into stoic Vader's mood.
Darth Vader's Lightsaber: Darth Vader's lightsaber is of a gleaming alloy construction with a black carbon composite grip and a red energy blade. Dramatic Entrance: Darth Vader's red lightsaber is naturally intimidating. Once per encounter when making a Coercion check (or using an ability that requires a Coercion check to activate), the wielder may ignite this lightsaber to upgrade the ability of the check once. Dual-Phase Modification: The wielder of Vader's lightsaber may change the length of the blade once per encounter as an incidental. The next attack after changing blade length ignores the target's melee defense. [...] Whenever Vader makes a combat check with his lightsaber and generates a Critical Injury, he may also, as an incidental, activate one Force power he possesses as if he had spent force points equal to the severity of the Critical Injury.
Data Dead Drop: A common and relatively cheap tool of embedded agents and informants, a data dead drop enables the covert exchange of a datachip or some other form of encoded message. Models include: Brennkeyes Syndicate Security Capsule, countless custom-made models.
Data Disk: Data disks are small, coin-sized coding devices. They are metallic stepped cone shapes that appear to be three disks stacked on top of one another. Commonly seen on the caps and belts of high ranking Imperial officers and contain coded identification information. Data disks are not limited to Imperial use however, and these small devices can be seen galaxy-wide, employed by trillions of beings in countless different scenarios. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Data Goggles: These goggles overlay the wearer's field of vision with additional information. At the beginning of an encounter, a character may select one Knowledge skill. For the remainder of the encounter, the character adds an automatic success result to all checks [they] make using that skill while wearing the data goggles. The GM may rule that a character needs to obtain specialized data files to upload into the data goggles before using them.
Datapad: A powerful combination of communications device, holo-messenger, handheld computer, and personal database, datapads combine a number of common electronics in one easy to use package.
Dataspike: Dataspikes, also known as computer spikes, are a common tool for bypassing computer security. Once per round, a character with a dataspike may expend it to attempt to disable a security program as a maneuver (rather than as an action). [...] Reduce the difficulty of subsequent Computers check to disable the program by 2, to a minimum of Simple (—). After one use, the dataspike burns out and becomes useless.
Death Sticks: Death sticks are an unpleasant creation derived from the balo mushroom of the planet of Balosar and distributed in a liquid form in distinctive small red and yellow rods. Restrictions: Death sticks are controlled substances on most worlds, and at the very least those under the age of majority cannot purchase them. Death sticks are completely illegal on many other worlds, especially those with high health standards.
DefenStar Ltd. Nyalsan II Planetary Shie: DefenStar Ltd. produces a number of planetary shield generators designed to protect everything from small, platoon-sized camps to huge forward garrisons that house thousands of troops, personnel, and weapons.
Defender Sporting Blaster Pistol: Producing a number of high-quality blaster weapons, the Drearian Defense Conglomerate is especially known for supplying the Corporate Sector Authority.
Degan Explorations Syntherope: Made from a mix of natural and synthetic chemicals and textiles, including the proteins found in the secretions of numerous arachnid species, Syntherope is thin, lightweight, and extremely strong, with a tensile strength ten times that of durasteel.
Dejarik Table: Dejarik, which uses a holographic display table to project playing pieces of real and mythical monsters, is a popular game of skill between two players.
Delvin Constructs Modular Base Structure: Delvin Constructs has made a name for itself designing and building cheap and affordable prefabricated buildings. They allow for a modular configuration of different structures to be easily transported by most freighters and deposited to suit the needs of the user. Environmental adaptation kits are also available for when the prefabs are used in hostile environments. Setting up a single module requires an Easy (1 difficulty) Athletics check and roughly 2 hours.
Demolitions Tool Kit: An essential part of any explosive ordnance disposal (EOD) trooper's gear, this kit is small (roughly thirteen centimeters long closed) and constructed of nonreactive, nonconductive materials.
Demon Mask: Every species and culture has scary stories of monsters with supernatural powers. A Force-sensitive character wearing a demon mask increases [their] wound threshold by 2 while [they] are a dark side Force user. Furthermore, character making Discipline checks to resist fear caused by a character wearing a demon mask add setback dice equal to that character's Force rating.
Dendriton Toxin: Dendriton toxion is an exceedingly lethal neurotoxin favored by assassins who prefer their victims to suffer. Any sized dose has a Daunting difficulty. If the target fails the check, the poison inflicts 1 strain at the end of [their] next turn, 3 strain at the end of [their] subsequent turn, and 5 strain at the end of the turn after that one (this ignores soak). In addition, the target suffers a setback die to any check [they] attempt while under the effects of the poison, and each threat result [they] generate on those checks inflicts 1 additional strain (this ignores soak) as the target is wracked with pain and violent muscle spasms. The GM may spend a despair result on the initial Resilience check to make the target make another check against the poison at the end of [their] third turn or suffer an additional 5 strain (this ignores soak) as the poison torments [them].
Desert Wrappings: Teedo and other desert cultures often dress in simple wrappings mixed with water reclamation tech, goggles, and other protective add-ons. In addition to offering some relief in hot and arid environments, this style of clothing also tends to conceal the wearer’s features. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Destri-series Laminate Beast Armor: Piccatech's Destri-series beast armor uses modern armor technology to provide unparalleled for working and riding beasts.
Destro-6 Demolition Charge: These shaped detonite charges are favored for sabotage missions and clandestine raids: situations with hardened targets but limited actual fighting.
Detonite Charge: Detonite is a putty explosive with a wide variety of applications, including commercial demolitions, industrial cladding, and entertainment pyrotechnics.
Devaronian Edge: By lacing the blade of a weapon with Devaorian Bloodpoison, a rare gemstone, the wounds caused by this weapon induce small particles into the target’s bloodstream. The gemstone is toxic and causes pain and convulsions in the target. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Dewback: Originating from the Dune Sea on Tatooine, dewbacks are large four-legged reptiles who are well-suited to desert environments.
Digital Lockpick: Some smugglers and thieves find that it pays not only to keep a lockpick in hand in a figurative sense, but also in a more literal fashion.
Diiro: Often carried by Quolas's guards and hunters, the diiro is a common Arbooinian weapon, tailored to the needs of the Sathari.
Dioxis Gas: Used since before the Clone Wars, dioxis can be lethal to most carbon-based life forms within less than one minute following exposure.
Diplomat's Robes: A diplomat's robes—whether Senatorial attire, Ambassadorial dress, or similar attire—clearly indicate that the wearer is a person of importance.
Diplomatic Authorization: Government agents who travel to neutral, allied, or even hostile star systems can reasonably expect to receive special treatment.
Disaster Relief Kit: These compact containers for use in relief efforts when catastrophe strikes inhabited regions, from the largest cities to the most isolated villages.
Disguise Kit: Disguise kits are simple collections of makeup, wigs, hairpieces, prosthetic features, and chromatic iris alternators; some even contain programmable skinweave for altering fingerprints and DNA cloaks that spoof advanced genetic scanners.
Disruptor Pistol: While once dominant across the galaxy's battlefields, destructive disruptor technology has been banned for generations..
Disruptor Rifle: Among the earliest known energy weapons dated by archaeologists, disruptor rifles predate blaster technology, and like disruptor pistols, they can completely disintegrate organic matter.
Dolina Ring Seeds: The swampy world of Dagobah has been erased from galactic charts, but the rare visitors of times part brough away samples of the planet's unique flora and fauna.
Double-Bladed Lightsaber: Also called lightstaves or Sith lightsabers, double-bladed lightsabers date from the mythical Sith Lord Exar Kun of the Qel-Droma Epics.
Double-Bladed Lightsaber Hilt: [A lightsaber hilt] requires a crystal to become a true weapon (otherwise they deal no damage, have no critical rating, and do not have many of their item qualities).
Double-Bladed Vibrosword: Extraordinarily difficult to wield and as dangerous to an untrained user as to their enemy, the double-bladed vibrosword is an uncommon variant of the more familiar vibrosword.
Dragite Gem: Small, dull, and brittle, dragite gems are found in the caves and caverns of the D'olop mountain range on M'haeli, an agriworld in the Expansion Region.
Draxton DF-15 Medium-Yield Fusion Genera: The DF-15 is a small fusion generator designed to power worksites or small buildings. As a maneuver, a character may use a DF-15 to recharge an energy weapon or device that has run out of power or ammunition in the manner of an extra reload/power pack.
Dress Uniform: Equal parts formal attire from a variety of worlds and functional military uniform, an assortment of dress uniforms are worn for ceremonial purposes by Alliance Military personnel.
Droid Brain Defense System: An extremely unusual upgrade generally reserved for powered armor suits, a droid brain defense system uses a full sensor suite to alert wearers to and help them avoid threats.
Droid Brain Detonator: Weapon manufacturers, combat engineers, and sabotage specialists are constantly inventing new triggers and detonators for explosive devices of all kinds, in order to provide for every conceivable situation.
Droid Disruptor: Verpine weaponsmiths enjoy creating specialized weapons, such as the Verpine droid disruptor. When used against organic creatures, the droid disruptor is much less effective. Any organic creature stuck by a droid disruptor does not receive normal damage, but instead simply experiences a brief shock to the system and suffers 2 strain.
Droid Targeting System: Although droids are most commonly thought of as autonomous mobile constructs, the advanced artificial intelligence of droid brains has been used in a variety of other applications for thousands of years.
Dual-Phase Modification: Considered antiquated by the time of the Clone Wars, the dual-phase modification is an artifact of an older, more violent time.
Dueling Lens: Originally invented at the Jedi Enclave on Ossus, this emitter lens focuses the lightsaber’s blade into a more precise, finely tuned form, suited to dueling and controlled strike. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Dueling Pistol: An ornate, long-bodied blaster pistol of impeccable craftsmanship, the Coronet Arms model is typical of dueling blasters, often custom ordered in pairs.
Duraknuckles: Dirty fighters and those looking to protect their fists from the tough hides of certain alien species rely on these metallic knuckles. This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use.
Duskblade: The mysterious Nightsisters use a number of bladed weapons, the most common of which is a deadly short sword.
E-11D Blaster Carbine: The E-11D is the standard-issue longarm of the death troopers. Upgrade the ability of combat checks made when using this weapon at short range once.
E-11s Sniper Rifle: BlasTech's E-11 platform is one of the most successful blaster designs in recent history.
E-22 Blaster Rifle: An uncommon weapon in the Imperial arsenal, BlasTech's E-22 blaster rifle is a medium squad support weapon fielded by numerous Imperial special forces units.
E-5C Heavy Blaster Rifle: The Baktoid Armour Workshop continued to iterate on the E-5 blaster rifle they shamelessly reverse engineered. In a rare display of creativity from the very same corporate entity which stole BlasTech designs, they increased the rate of fire by increasing the spin capacity of the main barrel. Of course, given that this weapon was due for the hands of dispensible B1 model battledroids, it was not reinforced and the safety features were left stripped. This makes this ‘heavy’ rifle particularly light for the firepower available, while dangerous. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
E-5 Blaster Rifle: The E-5 blaster rifle is a copy of a BlasTech design produced by Baktoid Armor Workshop for the Separatist armed forces.
E-5s Sniper Rifle: Despite its name suggesting a relationship to the E-5 blaster rifle, this light sniper rifle has no features in common with the original weapon. It has an extremely long and slim barrel, providing it with excellent long range accuracy. Paired with a high magnification scope, it makes for a great marksman’s weapon. All these features also mean that it makes for a poor weapon in close quarters. The barrel is awkward to use from the hip, and the scope makes it unusable firing down sights. Another limitation is that it’s only capable of only a few shots before needing a reload. It is truly a situational weapon used by dedicated sniper units which provide fire then disengage. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
E-60R Missile Launcher: Another cheap product courtesy of the Baktoid Armor Workshop, the E-60R missile launcher was designed by the Techno Union as an inexpensive and lightweight missile launcher for use by B1-series battle droids. Further cost cutting meant that models were produced without a built-in sighting system. The resulting missile launcher is an accessible and cheap anti-armour weapon, though the price reduction can certainly be felt by untrained users. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
E522 Assassin Droid: The E522 is one of the most feared droids operating in the galaxy today. Skills: Brawl 3, Coercion 4, Computers 2, Gunnery 3, Mechanics 2, Melee 3, Ranged (Light) 4, Vigilance 4. Talents: Adversary 2 (upgrade the difficulty of all combat checks against this target twice), Barrage 2 (add 2 to the damage of all Gunnery, Ranged [Light], and Ranged [Heavy] attacks made at medium or long range), Brace 2 (as a maneuver, remove up to two setback dice added due to environmental conditions from next skill check), Point Blank (add 4 to the damage of all Ranged [Light] and Ranged [Heavy] attacks made at engaged or short range). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Combat Computer (add two boost dice to all combat checks).
E5 Blaster Carbine: Millions of E5 blaster carbines litter the battlefields of the Clone Wars and are still salvaged today. GMs may spend three threat results or a despair result to force the E5 to overheat and damage itself one step (as if struck by a weapon with the Sunder quality).
ED-V8-Series Envoy Droid: Seeing a weakness in the general run of protocol droids, namely the almost universal humanoid appearance of most models, the Rseikharhl Droid Group began the development of a droid who would specialize in assisting negotiators and diplomats working specifically with non-humanoid species.
EE-3 Blaster Carbine: The rugged and reliable EE-3 blaster carbine is one of BlasTech's most popular nonmilitary weapons.
EG-6 Power Droid: Veril Line Systems' EG-6 units are the most common class five droids in the galaxy. Skills (group only): Computers, Mechanics. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: Built-in DF-15 fusion generator, spectrometer, multi-spectral scanner, manipulator arm, power diagnostics package (removes a setback die to checks made to remove system strain from vehicles or starships).
ELG-3A Blaster Pistol: A crossover weapon that occupied a unique niche between holdout blaster and light blaster pistol, the elegant ELG-3A is a SoroSuub Corporation design marketed to politicians and the wealthy, who demand both form and function.
EMP Disruptor: Only the size of a stylus, this miniaturised technology contained a small battery capable of discharging a localised EMP burst that would affect only the closest electronic systems. Originally designed for use by technicians working in tight environments for circuitry repairs, the disruptor has gained popularity among the spec ops groups and nefarious criminal elements of the galaxy to assist in slicing attempts. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
EOD-Mk IV Explosives Disposal Remote: EOD-Mk IV remotes are produced by Industrial Automaton for use in construction, demolitions, and explosive ordnance disposal (EOD) applications.
EVA Powersuit: The extra-vehicular activity (EVA) powersuit is a fully sealed powered spacesuit designed to facilitate external starship repairs in vacuum.
Ealewon A-212 Tracing Console: Tracing consoles are most commonly found with espionage agencies and other sensitive installations, but they could be used by any group concerned about the security of its electronic systems.
Earbud Comlink: Earbud comlinks are tiny, unobstrusive communications devices that fit snugly into an individual's ear. Earbud comlinks have a usable range of long. Noticing that an individual is wearing an earbud comlink requires a Hard difficulty Perception check.
Ebonwood Swaggerstick: Primarily carried as a symbol of authority, a swaggerstick hails to riding traditions of various cultures and is a reasonably effective weapon in the rare occasion it actually gets used. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Echani Foil: These elegant, light and effective weapons were crafted in honor of some of the greatest Echani duelists centuries ago. With the ability to stand up to a Lightsaber, and a honed edge that easily pierces flesh and armor, they are truly weapons of class. Unfortunately, impurities in the cortosis weave has seen few of these weapons survive the test of time. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Electro-Immolation Capsule: A small electric capsule that can be surgically installed into a being, generally in their tooth. If an individual is taken prisoner they can activate the capsule which administers a lethal shock. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Electro-Snare: There are countless devices in the galaxy designed to incapacitate a target or prey animal so that it can be captured alive.
Electro-monocular Scope: Regular optical scopes must be manually focused and are hindered by low light conditions. This low profile Electro-Monocular uses thermal and sonic imaging to produce digital images at up to 15x magnification. It can also pick up sonic vibrations through walls from over a kilometer away and relay them to a connected comm device. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Electro Dart: Fitted with a conductive tip and a small but powerful battery, electro darts are commonly used by Bounty Hunters to incapacitate their targets in a nonlethal manner. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Electrobinoculars: Electrobinoculars are, at their most basic, a pair of macrobinoculars with one or more integrated optical enhancement systems, such as long-range digital zoom, passive light amplification, thermal imaging, ultrasound imaging, and the like.
Electromag-Pulse Disruptor: An electromag-pulse disruptor is a slim stylus used to short out electronic equipment. Add two boost dice to any check the character makes to sabotage electronics or computers. Add a setback die to a character's Perception checks made to find this weapon on a person's body.
Electromagnetic Pulse Launcher: The EMP launcher is a highly specialised form of grenade launcher which was produced for use by the GAR to fight the droid armies of the CIS. Merr-Sonn engineers sought to create a weapon which would specifically destroy droids as effectively as a single shot could. Using similar technology to what is found in droid disablers and ion blasters, the EMP launcher would fire a highly concentrated EMP contained in a stable field which gave it a proton torpedo-like appearance. Once it impacts with a surface, the pulse is released and any electronics at the point of impact receives an overload much greater than from a regular EMP grenade or personal ion weapon. As with other Ion weapons, the EMP launcher can only deal damage to droids and those with considerable cybernetic enhancements. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Electronet: Popular with corporate-funded explorers capturing creature for study, the electronet is a duracord net with interwoven conductive threads capable of delivering a stun charge to anything it touches.
Electronet Launcher: An ideal weapon for subduing most sentient beings, the electronet launcher uses the same concept as a conventional net launcher, but without the tangled mess of a physical net.
Electronic Lock Breaker: Whether stealing rare treasures from a private collector or museum, or securing arms or medical equipment from military bases for an armed resistance, operatives and thieves often need to overcome physical security systems.
Electronic Sighting System: By adding a small electronic device to the top of a blaster or other ranged weapon, a small holographic crosshair is projected a meter out from the weapon's barrel.
Electrostaff: Electrostaffs first saw widespread use during the Clone Wars among Separatist forces, especially among the personal guard droids of General Grievous.
Elysium Model 2 Atmospheric Processor: Gandorthral Atmospherics is a leading provider of atmospheric engineering systems, producing everything from breath masks and hostile environment suits to massive, city-sized terraforming installations.
Emergency Medpac: Emergency medpacs are prevalent wherever the threat of injury is higher than average. Emergency medpacs allow characters to use the Medicine skill to heal others without penalty.
Emergency Repair Patch: Composed of a semi-flexible durasteel disk coated on one side with insulation and edged with auto-activating thermal welds, emergency repair patches are used to keep machinery running until proper repairs can be done.
Energy Bow: The preferred weapon of the Nightsisters of Dathomir, the energy bow is a carbon-alloy bow with a plasma coupler string, a similar technology to the energy binders that link podracing engines.
Energy Buckler: An energy buckler consists of a metallic disk worn on the wielder's wrist. When the wielder is using an energy buckler, the GM can spend any despair result the wielder generates on a Combat check to have the energy buckler run out of power; it ceases to function until the end of the encounter or until recharged.
Energy Dispersion System: This minute metallic wiring is designed to distribute electric shocks across the armor while grounding the user against energy, reducing the potency of such attacks.
Energy Overclock Setting: As deadly and dangerous as they are, vibro weapons can be even deadlier with the inclusion of an overclock setting. This attachment can be applied to any vibro weapon or any other powered Melee or Brawl weapon. Base Modifiers: Once per turn as a maneuver, the wielder may activate the overclock setting to grant the weapon Vicious 3 (or increase the value of its Vicious quality by +3) until the end of the next round. If the wielder generates three threat results or despair on a combat check with the weapon before the end of the next round, the weapon overheats or shorts out and ceases to function entirely until the end of the encounter. Modification Options: 1 Add an automatic advantage result to combat checks with this weapon Mod, 1 Activate attachment as an incidental Mod.
Energy Slingshot: Less a weapon than a tool for mischievous pranksters, the energy slingshot is a wrist-mounted device. Triggering the Disorient quality on this weapon requires one advantage result instead of two advantage results.
Engineer's Hammer: An engineer's hammer is an all-purpose construction and demolitions tool. Using an engineer's hammer adds two boost dice to any checks made to build or demolish structures. When used as a weapon, an engineer's hammer has the profile as mentioned above.
Enhanced Optics Suite: Built into a helmet, this system provides an individual with a host of specialized optical and audio sensors. Base Modifiers: Removes up to two setback dice to Perception, Vigilance, and combat skill checks due to darkness, smoke, or other environmental effects that obscure vision. Modification Options: 1 Skill (Vigilance +1) Mod.
Enhanced XCiter: Technicians, Engineers, and Tinkerers have long known how to modify the gas conversion XCiter in a blaster to yield a more highly energetic—and correspondingly more destructive—payload.
Entrenching Tool: The modest and versatile entrenching tool is one of the most critically important tools in a Warrior's kit. Besides allowing the user to dig holes and trenches, an entrenching tool counts as a small improvised weapon that deals +2 damage, has a critical rating of 4, and does not break due to threat or despair results.
Enviro-suit: At their most basic, enviro-suits are fully sealed, full-body suits made of sturdy materials designed to keep sentients alive in inhospitable places.
Environmental Adaptation Kit: The varied and often hazardous environments found throughout the galaxy have a tendency to hinder and harm even the sturdiest, most well-built pieces of equipment.
Eopie (Cameloid): Eopies are ponderous but surprisingly strong and resilient animals used as both mounts and labor animals on their native Tatooine.
Escape Circuit: Many free droids find the notion of being placed back into servitude intolerable. An escape circuit is a cybernetic implant that can only be installed in droids. A droid installed with an escape circuit can resist a restraining bolt with an Easy difficulty Discipline check instead of the normal difficulty. The droid may spend two advantage results from a check made to resist a restraining bolt to overload the bolt, rendering it entirely useless.
Etaan Crystal: These unusual kyber crystals form in the deep caverns of the Mid Rim world of Etai. Base Modifiers: Installing this crystal changes a lightsaber's base damage to 6 and critical rating to 2, and the lightsaber gains the Breach 1 and Sunder weapon qualities. If the crystal is ever removed, the lightsaber loses these qualities and reverts to its previous base damage and critical rating. In addition, while the Etaan crystal is installed in the lightsaber, if the character wielding the lightsaber uses the Improved Reflect talent to inflict an automatic hit, increase the damage dealt by that hit by 2. Modification Options: 1 Item Quality (Vicious +1) Mod, 2 Damage +1 Mods, 1 Decrease the weapon's critical rating by 1 to a minimum of 1 Mod, 1 Innate Talent (Parry) Mod, 1 Innate Talent (Reflect) Mod.
Exoglove: Common around spaceports on backwater worlds, exogloves mechanically enhance strength, ostensibly for shifting cargo and performing other menial labor.
Expensive Jewelry: People associate wealth with authority, and jewelry is an immediate means to display wealth. A character displaying one or more expensive gems adds an automatic advantage result to Charm and Deception checks, but others add a boost die to checks to recall the character.
Explorer's Knife: Nearly as ubiquitous as combat knives, explorer's knives are indispensable tools for professional foresters. An explorer's knife grants a boost die to all Survival checks.
Explosive Dart: For the targets that are not required to be taken alive, some bounty hunters resort to explosive darts. They also make for an excellent way to clear small groups of targets. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Explosive Implant: Slavers and slaveholders do not always keep slaves locked up in chains while they carry out their duties. A corresponding handheld device similar to a droid caller can locate the implanted target with a Simple (—) Computers check. With the press of a button, the implant can explode, instantly killing its victim. A (once upgraded) Daunting difficulty Medicine or Mechanics check is required remove the implant. However, a threat result alerts the transmitter of the tampering, and a despair result triggers the device.
Explosive Micro-Rocket: Explosive micro-rockets rely on a chepatite impact explosive to deliver a powerful kinetic blast of energy on impact.
Explosive Ordnance Disposal (EOD) Kit: Disarming and disposing of explosive compounds, devices, and military-grade ordnance is a tricky business. An EOD kit adds two automatic advantage results on any checks made to detect and disarm or otherwise neutralize explosive compounds and devices.
Explosive Shackles: A particularly intimidating and malicious method of security slaves and prisoners, explosive shackles have small but lethal charges built into them to deter escape attempts.
Explosives Belt: A tool of saboteurs and infiltrators, the explosives belt is a small and convenient concealed demolitions kit. Setting the explosive charge hidden in the belt requires an Average difficulty Mechanics check. The charge detonates with the following profile: (Damage 15; Critical 3; Range [Engaged]; Blast 5, Breach 1, Limited Ammo 1). Characters upgrade the difficulty of checks to detect the belt's true nature twice.
Extended Hilt: The idea behind the extended hilt modification is to increase the lightsaber's striking and cutting power by using the leverage of the longer handle and a two-handed grip to help focus the wielder's strength into the force of the blow.
Extra Reload: Althoug blaster weapons generally have large clips, it is a good idea to carry some reloads if those clips run out. Extra reloads allow a character to overcome an "out of ammo" despair result with a ranged weapon. By spending a maneuver, a character can switch out a power pack or reload a weapon back into the action.
FC1 Flechette Launcher: Rebel partisans have used the now-banned Golan Arms FC1 flechette launcher in a number of attacks, most notoriously at Inusagi's Sakoola Blossom Festival, killing hundreds of guests and just-announced Imperial Governor Tophervin.
FS19 Compound Bow: Traditional, projectile-throwing bow weapons are a rarity in the galaxy. The basic ammunition used with the FS19 is Field Sports' Mk. IV "Broadhead" arrow, which consists of a meter-long plasteel shaft with a sharp, bladed point at one end and synthetic, spin-stabilizing fletching on the other. These arrows are cheap to replace; the GM who wishes to set a price could charge 1 credit per arrow. In addition, Field Sports produces a line of other arrows for use with the FS19 that increase the weapon's versatility. [...] [Below are] the profiles for non-standard arrows for the bow.
FWG-5 Flechette Pistol: This flechette pistol from Malaxan Firepower Incorporated utilizes a laser tracking system that guides flechette-filled pellets to targets before exploding in a hail of needle-like debris.
FWG-7 Flechette Smart Carbine: An example of Malaxan Firepower Incorporated's successful FWG line of flechette weapons, the FWG-7 provides all the power and versatility of a flechette weapon in a lightweight, easy-to-handle package.
Fabricators' Protective Gear: Worn by welders, machinists, tinkerers, and mechanics throughout the galaxy, fabricators' protective gear is a catchall term for a varied collection of heavy padded clothing.
Fabritech CN-15 Camouflage Netting: This sophisticated and versatile camouflage is used by military personnel to conceal encampments, supply caches, vehicles and other high-value assets.
Fabritech CN-21 Camouflage Paint: This thick paint comes in small tubes holding five applications and is intended to camouflage exposed skin, but can be used on everything from weapons to personal gear.
Fac L-120 Modular Motor Pool: A combination garage, workshop, fabrication facility, and shelter, the L-120 modular motor pool is designed to hold ground vehicles and their support equipment.
False Voice Transmitter: For years, a multitude of criminal and resistance organizations have utilized homemade devices known as false voice transmitters, or FVTs, to fool the Empire.
Falsified Credentials: Whether for relatively tame reasons such as traveling incognito, or more nefarious reasons such as government infiltration, the demand for fake credentials has remained strong.
Fambaa: The enormous fambaa are have been famously domesticated by Gungans for thousands of years on Naboo, but have long been exported to many other worlds including Onderon.
FasClean Atmospheric Reactive Damper: Typically found on heavily populated, highly advanced worlds such as Coruscant, the FasClean Atmospheric Reactive Damper (ARD) produced by Gandorthral Atmospherics is one of the most important environmental engineering devices produced.
Fear Stick: The fear stick is only the size of a stylus or a hypodermic injector and injects a potent neurologic compound in a target's system.
Felebreck Droid Disabler: Produced by Felebreck Electrical Defense Systems, the Droid Disabler is a small but powerful ionization pistol intended to damage, rather than merely incapacitate, droids.
Fibra-line: Fibra-line is synthetic rope manufactured in small, thin spools. The rope is fairly durable, consisting of three large fibrous "cables" that are tightlywoven together. These filaments are usually between six and eight centimetres in diameter. While fibra-line may seem flimsy, it is actually capable of supporting up to 750 kilograms of weight. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Field Central Nervous System Emergency S: Only the size of a heavy blaster pistol, this bulky hand-held device is a miniaturised version of common medical equipment found in medbays across the galaxy. It delivers a specially calibrated electric shock to the organic body, stimulating the central nervous system and awakening a downed individual. Even entirely untrained individuals can usually revive a squadmate with this device, as long they weren’t outright killed. It was developed long before the Clone Wars, and was often given to spec ops groups that lacked dedicated medics or were expected to face continuous combat with little time to address wounds. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Field Ration Pack: Ration packs are typically bland, flavorless affairs that contain an entire meal packed with necessary nutrients and vitamins in a small, easily portable bag or box.
Field Sports Model 77 Air Rifle: Typically marketed to hobbyists and hunters, air rifles are lightweight, practical weapons used for target shooting and hunting.
Filed Front Sight: A common modification to holdout blasters, along with removing their serial numbers, is filing down the weapon's front sight.
Findsman Shockprod: Findsmen shockprods are meter-long metal-alloy polearms with a forked end tipped with stun electrodes. When a character wielding a Findsman shockprod makes a Seek, Sense, or Farsight Force power check, they may add one force point to the results. This weapon requires 2 hands to wield.
Fingerprint Masque: For those who are serious about keeping their identity unknown, fingerprint masques are essential. Fingerprint masques can fool basic fingerprint readers with nothing more than an Easy difficulty Cool or Deception check, while more advanced scanners require an Average or Hard difficulty check, at the GM's discretion. If the biometric scanner operator has reason to be suspicious of a character wearing a fingerprint masque, it may require an opposed social skill check vs. Computers against the operator. A character must make a Daunting difficulty Perception check to actually notice the physical presence of a fingerprint masque on an individual's fingers.
Fire Paste: This versatile chemical putty is used to ignite and sustain emergency fires. Corellian Chemical Corporation's fire paste can be used to ignite nearly any flammable object. Without a fuel source, it can burn for roughly an hour with the same intensity as a small campfire. It can burn in any atmosphere, but not underwater or in vacuum.
Firkraan Crystal: Firkraan crystals have a unique way of channeling the power of a lightsaber, modifying the output energy blade, making it highly effective against droids. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Fissrit: Fissrit, also known as fire worms, are many-legged arthropods measuring just under a third of a meter in length. Skills (group only): Brawl, Perception, Stealth. Talents: None. Abilities: Scorching Touch (when an opponent ends its turn engaged with the fissrit, the opponent suffers 1 wound and 1 strain), Silhouette 0. Equipment: Heated underbelly (Brawl; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Engaged]; Burn 1).
Flame Projector: Flame projectors come in a variety of shapes and sizes, but generally include a rifle-like emitter connected to a number of canisters.
Flameout: Flameout is an extremely strong alcoholic beverage that, when mixed correctly, is known for producing sensations similar to a scalding of the tongue and afreezing of the throat. The drink is very popular in spaceport cantinas from Coruscant to Bakura. Jedi Master Bantis Keeg is reportedly a champion flameout mixer aboard Starlight Beacon. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Flare Jacket: Originally designed by a high-end clothing manufacturer for making literally flashy entrances, the flare jacket caught the attention of organizations that saw potential military use and contracted Ayelixe/Krongbing Textiles to produce a more intense version for a personal defense coat. Models include: Ayelixe/Krongbing Personal Defense Coat, Brennkeyes Syndicate flare jacket.
Flash Charge: Flash charges are fired from a cylindrical launcher like a flare and explode into a brilliant burst a few seconds later. The flash temporarily blinds anyone caught unaware in medium range and overwhelms all electro, macro, and optical magnification devices, negating any advantages they provide. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Flashstick: Wielded almost exclusively by the Drall constabulary, the flashstick is a nonlethal weapon used to subdue criminals and other undesirables.
Flechette Micro-Rocket: Micro-rockets with flechette payloads are packed with razor-sharp needles that can tear through unarmored targets.
Flex-5 Detonite Tape: Flex-5 is a Merr-Sonn Munitions demolitions device made from highly processed detonite for precision demolition of small or lightly armored objects with minimal collateral damage.
FlexPoly Bacta Suit: Requiring much less volume than the average bacta tank while allowing for marginally more modesty of the patient, the Zaltin Corp’s FlexyPoly Bacta suit is a somewhat niche medical marvel. A series of pumps and tubes connects this transparent suit with a sizable device that measures telemetry wirelessly while delivering a patented synthetic bacta to circulate around the patient’s body. Unfortunately, medical technology has not progressed to the point of vital life-saving equipment and stylish streetwear being one and the same. The patient will still be moored to a single location while the suit is in use, lest the tubing be ripped from the ports, spilling precious bacta onto the floor. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Flickerphase Blade: Considered dishonorable and unsporting by most Jedi, so-called flickerphase modifications were never widely adopted. Base Modifiers: Decrease the lightsaber's damage by 2 and add the Inaccurate 2 quality (or increase the existing quality by 1). When attacking with this lightsaber, targets cannot use the Parry incidental to reduce the damage of the attack. Modification Options: 1 Item Quality (Inaccurate –1) Mod, 1 Damage +1 Mod.
Foamcast: Foamcast is the commonly used term for a type of quick-setting aerosol foam found in construction and building maintenance.
Food Preparation System: This dubiously named field stove, more commonly known to military personnel as a "hot plate," is little more than a collapsible tray with integrated circuitry for preparing field rations and other food products.
Foot Speeder: These curious contraptions combine the swiftness of a speeder car with an easy of storage and simple construction that makes keeping and maintaining them much less expensive than doing so for an actual vehicle.
Force-Attuned Resonator: When the Jedi artificer Sokiro Terebin set about reproducing the crystalline, Force-attuned resonators from the blasters she had unearthed at Site-145 on Filata, she first had to solve numerous technical challenges.
Force Pike: Designed by a number of manufacturers as an easy-to-use crowd-control tool for military and law enforcement agencies, the force pike combines the deadliness of a vibro weapon and the utility of a stun weapon in one package.
Forearm Grip: Little more than a vertical handgrip affixed below the barrel of a rifle-sized weapon, the forearm grip is a very popular attachment among mercenaries and professional soldiers across the galaxy.
Forensics Investigation Kit: Essentially specialized tool kits for crime-scene investigations, forensic investigation kits are produced by a number of security companies for use by law enforcement agencies.
Forgemaster's Hammer: The seventh unofficial tenet of Mandalorian culture is disdain for uni-taskers. As such, Mandalorian smithing hammers are effective at both forging and destroying armor. Made of a high Beskar content alloy, these hammers are capable of fending off lightsaber attacks. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Forgery Tools: Regardless of the case or the legitimacy of the reason, quality forgery tools such as carefully crafted inks and implements or complex credential-falsification devices can enhance a crafter's innate talents. Models include: Goseia Heavy Industries Concern Executive Privacy Suite, countless custom-made (and illegal) models.
Formal Council Armor: In rare circumstances, Jedi were called in to treat and societies for which status and associated symbols played a significant factor in negotiations.
Fragmentation Grenade: This common anti-personnel grenade is a shell of hardened alloy or polycarbonate surrounding a wire-wrapped core of detonite or baradium-infused detonite controlled by a simple timer.
Fragmentation Missile: Fragmentation missiles are, for the credits, the best anti-personnel ordnance available on the galactic arms market.
Frangawl Force Powder: Frangawl cultists are masters of dark side techniques, including ones that deal with capturing and corrupting the Living Force in a sentient being.
Fuel-air Bomb: Fuel-air bombs are devastating anti-personnel weapons that use atmospheric oxygen as an oxidizer. A fuel-air bomb's Concussive value increases by one for every additional charge. A despair result on a check to set up this bomb means it immediately detonates, dealing its total damage to everything in its current blast radius. Additional Damage +10.
Fusion Cutter: Fusion cutters are handheld, portable cutting torches found throughout the galaxy in numerous industrial, technical, and artistic operations.
Fusion Lantern: Fusion lanterns are a combination power source, light, and area heater.
Fusion Shunt: A lightsaber is more than just a weapon. It is a symbol, a badge of office—and a tool. Base Modifiers: Activating or deactivating the fusion shunt requires a maneuver. If the lightsaber is used as a weapon while the shunt is active, all combat checks suffer three threat results, but the weapon's Breach quality increases by 1. While the fusion shunt is active, the lightsaber can slice through obstacles with ease, destroying or cutting through barriers and other inanimate targets at twice the normal rate. Exact details are up to GM's discretion, but in general, hardened security doors and reinforced walls should withstand two or three rounds of cutting, with other obstacles being dealt with in a corresponding time frame. Modification Options: None.
Fyr Assault Carbine: Glunok is a relatively obscure Andoan arms manufacturer that offers a wide variety of modern combat slugthrowers. The GM may spend two threat results (or a despair result) to cause a PC's Fyr assault carbine to run out of ammo.
