G-20 Glop Grenade: Merr-Sonn's G-20 glop grenade is a reusable non-lethal anti-personnel grenade that law enforcement uses to capture fleeing suspects and occasionally for riot suppression.
G-2 Retriever Droid: The basic model retriever droid is small and simple, but adept at performing its limited range of tasks. Specifically programmed for release in larger debris fields, it will locate materials of size and composition within the parameters designated by its master. Given the relatively small size of the droid, it is adept at working its way through smaller crevices in breeched hulls, and bringing back smaller materials. Though when released en masse, they can quickly clear debris fields of smaller flotsam, though their small size and limited thrust prevents them from acquiring larger sections of recyclable materials.
G-suit: Most sets of crash gear consist of an assortment of protective wear designed to allow a user to better survive the inevitable collisions and buffeting that are part of every race or mission.
G9-GX Pulse Drill: Designed as a handheld complement to the tried-and-true G2-GE beamdrill, the G9-GX uses a variable-strength, continuous-beam plasma projector to perform all manner of jobs, from cutting to welding and brazing to incinerating waster material and industrial byproducts.
GALAAR-15 Blaster Carbine: The GALAAR-15 is a popular blaster carbine built by Mandalorian arms giant Concordian Crescent Technologies.
GLX Firelance Blaster Rifle: Developed during the Clone Wars, the GLX Firelance is a compact blaster rifle marketed to "freelance law enforcement agents."
GT-Series Construction Droid: These burly-looking class five droids from Veril Line Systems are the latest in a long line of construction droids dating back to the ancient AM-Mk II used in the mid-Republic era.
GV/3-series Guardian Droid: The GV/3 guardian droid, manufactured by Cybot Galactica, is one of the most advanced security droids on the market. Skills: Brawl 3, Perception 3, Ranged (Light) 2, Vigilance 2. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink, and can survive in vacuum or underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Loyalty Imprint (add a boost die to all checks made to defend its designated master), Olfactory Sensor Suite (reduces the difficulty of scent-based Perception checks by 1). Equipment: Retractable blade incisors (Brawl; Damage 5; Critical 3; Range [Engaged]; Ensnare 1), retractable light blaster (Ranged [Light]; Damage 5; Critical 4; Range [Medium]; Stun setting).
GX-8 Water Vaporator: The most popular and well regarded moisture vaporator on the galactic market, Pretormin Environmental's GX-8 has provided fresh, safe water to planetary pioneers for generations.
Gaffi Stick: The Tusken Raiders of Tatooine carry brutal staves made from desert tracti hardwood, krayt dragon horn or bone, or scavenged metal.
Galven Pattern Resequencing: Some operatives would gladly trade a little reliability in exchange for the stopping power necessary to penetrate an opponent's armor.
Gammaplast Armor: Sith Troopers wear an improved version of the armor other stormtroopers wear, with higher quality gammaplast plating kitted out with a number of attachments. Gammaplast armor found elsewhere is almost always made to order. Its sturdy and light construction is highly configurable. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Gandorthral Atmospherics Scout 5 Field G: This eyewear features interchangeable lenses and is designed to protect the soldier's eyes from sun, wind, dust, and other airborne debris.
Gandorthral Portable Survival Shelter: The Portable Survival Shelter by Gandorthral Atmospherics is a marvel of modern materials science and survival technology.
Gank Comm Implant: This peculiar comlink implant interfaces directly with the user's neocortex, allowing the user to communicate silently with other characters who possess similar implants merely by thinking.
Garrote: Deceptively lethal, garrotes are lengths of line, cord, cable, or chain, often with handles at each end. A character who makes a successful Brawl attack when using a garrote against a target can cause the target to become suffocated. Targets may attempt to break free of the garrote with an opposed Brawl check, adding a setback die for each round they have been caught.
Garrote Chrono: Garrote chronos are one of a number of specialized concealed weapons designed for use by field agents. This item provides the wearer with a retractable garrote hidden within a standard chrono. Upgrade the difficulty of checks to detect the concealed garotte twice.
Gauntlets of Marka Ragnos: Crafted from black scales of an unknown creature, these gloves were worn by one of the most powerful Sith of his time. The rage and fury Marka Ragnos imbued into these gauntlets as he slew countless Jedi with his lightsaber can still be felt emanating from them. Their dark presence tempts and corrupts Force users that wear them, slowly drawing them towards the dark side. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Gene-Lock: Some people form a great attachment, even a bond, with their weapon and take steps to ensure that no others can use it. This attachment can be applied to any handheld weapon. This upgrade is most relevant for ranged weapons, but it is possible to gene-lock a vibroblade or similar weapon. In this case, an unauthorized user can still wield the weapon as a basic sword, etc., but any technological components such as a vibro-motor cease to function. Base Modifiers: The weapon functions only in the hands of its authorized owner. Modification Options: 1 Self Destruct Mod (The weapon detonates if an unauthorized user tries to fire it, inflicting one Critical Injury on the user [roll normally to determine the Critical Injury]. The weapon is destroyed if it self-destructs.)
General Purpose Scanner: General purpose scanners are usually small, handheld devices designed to sense and record a variety of data.
Generic Remote: Small wheeled devices, spheres with miniature repulsorlifts, and various other chasses house generic remotes, which can perform basic preprogrammed tasks in response to simple commands from users.
Geonosian Elite Beam Weapon: This exotic and powerful weapon was specifically designed for use by the elites of the Geonosian warrior caste. It would bury into the arm of a Geonosian and drain some of its body fluids to produce the intense beam which could cut through biologicals with ease. While otherwise unusable by other species, they could be found already loaded with the Geonosian fluids, allowing a few shots before the weapon becomes useless. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Geonosian Heavy Blaster Pistol: Game Masters can spend one Despair result to have a heavy blaster pistol to run out of ammo.
Geonosian Sonic Blaster: The weapon used by virtually the whole Geonosian warrior caste, this sonic blaster was designed by Gordarl Weaponsmiths, a subsidiary of Geonosian Industries. A particularly complex weapon which used experimental sonic wave theory based technology, it fired a contained sphere of condensed sonic energy. Upon coming into contact with a physical entity, or energy shield, the sphere would rupture and release the sonic blast on the spot. This could easily cause an instantaneous death by rupturing a target’s internal organs. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Geoscanner: Miners and prospectors depend upon a reliable geoscanner to identify the location of ore deposits. A geoscanner adds a boost die to any Perception, Knowledge (Lore), or Knowledge (Education) checks related to studying the subterranean environment within medium range of the user.
Ghostfire Crystal: These kyber crystals grow in nearly invisible formations on a handful of far-flung Outer Rim worlds. Base Modifiers: Installing this crystal changes a lightsaber's base damage to 6, critical rating to 2, and the lightsaber gains the Breach 1 and Sunder item qualities. If the crystal is ever removed, the lightsaber loses these qualities and reverts to its previous base damage and critical rating. In addition, while the ghostfire crystal is installed in the lightsaber, the wielder may spend four advantage results or a triumph result to prevent the target from using the Parry talent to reduce damage from this attack. Modification Options: 2 Damage +1 Mods, 2 Item Quality (Defensive +1) Mods.
Glitterstim: Glitterstim is a vanishingly rare variety of spice, refined from the webs spun by the energy spiders of Kessel and mined by the unfortunate slaves sent into those pitch-black depths. Restrictions: Glitterstim is illegal on most worlds, and its trade is aggressively monitored and controlled by Imperial authorities.
Glow Rod: Glow rods are small, directional, handheld light sources used in many applications.
Golan Arms DF.11 Anti-Infantry Battery: Golan Arms' DF.11 is one of the more popular anti-infantry artillery weapons on the galactic market today. The DF.11 emplacement is a small, immobile vehicle for game purposes and has the following profile: Crew: 3—One gunner, one technician, and one fire control operator. Weapons: DF.11 anti-infantry battery—this weapon's profile uses personal scale, not planetary scale (Fire Arc All; Damage 15; Critical 2; Range [Long]; Blast 10, Inaccurate 2, Linked 1, Pierce 4, Slow-Firing 1).
Golan Arms Mk.4 Heavy Fragmentation Gren: Sporting a stronger detonite charge and larger surface area than other frag grenades, Golan Arms' heavy fragmentation grenade yields a consequently larger and more powerful blast, with a greater quantity of shrapnel dispersed over a wider area.
Goliath Grenade: A cruel and cowardly weapon, Goliath grenades are unholy concoctions. A variety of dangerous gases including everything from; cohexaloride, gannaheesian IV, ammonia and triaxiphalate are pumped into a tiny fragile grenade. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Gorgo Loaf: An unleavened bread commonly seen throughout the Outer Rim, the thickness and denseness of gorgo loaf makes it ideal for workers needing energy during a long day. While the basic recipe for gorgo loaf is simple, many planets and people add their own twist to the bread, based on the availability of certain ingredients or the significance of certain foodstuffs. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Gorraslug Leather Coat: Gorraslug leather is thick and strong, able to provide some protection from blasters while still appearing like standard clothing of the sturdy variety. It is favored among mercenaries looking for a little style or subtlety without being ostentatious. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Grakks Cybernetic Suite: The Hutts of the Jahibakti clan, and its vassal clans, are known also as Grakks (named after Grakkus the Hutt). They are immediately recogni- sable by their use of cybernetic legs to overcome the weakness of their slug-like bodies. These legs are often custom designs to the preference of the Hutt having them installed, ranging in number and appearance. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Grapnel-Harpoon Launcher: Commonly thought of as tools for climbing or lifting cargo, the weapons division of Pharr Co. instead produces grapnel launchers with the balance and targeting capabilities necessary for combat use, allowing their users to entangle opponents in a binding cord.
Gravity Lock: These hovering, slablike devices are the latest attempts to thwart any would-be ship thieves.. Once firmly clamped onto each leg of a ship's landing gear, gravity locks use tractor beam technology to ensure a ship cannot take off. It has a silhouette of 3 [...], and counts as having a soak value of 15 and a wound threshold of 10. If attacked, two threat results or a despair result may be spent to inflict half of the damage it suffers onto a ship to which it has been attached. Entering the unique key code created when the lock is installed is the usual manner to deactivate the device, but it can be dismantled in a laborious process requiring an upgraded [twice] Formidable difficulty Mechanics check and eight hours of hard work. If the character has direct knowledge of a gravity lock's inner workings, the check is not upgraded and the process takes half the time.
Great Lightsaber: Great lightsabers are oversized lightsabers built for individuals with large, powerful frames. This lightsaber is a great lightsaber hilt containing an unmodded Ilum lightsaber crystal. This crystal occupies two of the weapon's hard points. When installing a lightsaber crystal into a great lightsaber increase the damage of that lightsaber crystal by one.
Great Lightsaber Hilt: [A lightsaber hilt] requires a crystal to become a true weapon (otherwise they deal no damage, have no critical rating, and do not have many of their item qualities).
Grenade Cling: Proper placement of explosives is a key factor in the effectiveness of a device's destructive potential. This attachment can be added to any grenade or explosive device. Base Modifiers: The grenade immediately adheres to its target, and an Average difficulty Athletics check is needed to remove it. The target struck by the grenade cannot avoid the blast or throw the grenade back at the attacker, regardless of any time-delayed detonation. At the GM's discretion, some types of surfaces may resist the adhesion. Modification Options: 2 Increase damage by 1 to stuck target Mods, 2 Increase difficulty to remove adhered grenade once Mods.
Guard Shoto: Similar to the shoto lightsaber, the guard shoto, or lightsaber tonfa, is a lightsaber with its grip set perpendicular to the blade, allowing the wielder to rapidly switch between attack and defense, deflecting bolts and blows one moment and lashing out with circular attacks of blinding speed the next.
Guard Shoto Hilt: [A lightsaber hilt] requires a crystal to become a true weapon (otherwise they deal no damage, have no critical rating, and do not have many of their item qualities).
Guardian Police Droid: GU-series "Guardian" police droids were the primary law enforcement droids operated by the Coruscant Security Force during the final years of the Republic.
Gungan Atlatl: A Gungan warrior could throw one of [their] signature plasma balls like a grenade (and often do), but a Gungan atlatl gives the weapon a greater range and aids in accuracy.
Gungan Electropole: Gungan herders traditionally use electropoles to guide and guard their shaaks. An electropole may be thrown as a ranged weapon. If used in this manner, change the skill to Ranged (Light) and the range of the weapon to short. The weapon also gains the Limited Ammo 1 quality (but can be retrieved and used again).
Gungan Personal Energy Shield: Gungan engineering incorporates a particularly advanced understanding of shielding technologies. The value of the Gungan personal energy shield's Defensive quality is reduced to 0 while the wielder is suffering from the staggered or disoriented condition.
Gungan Plasma Ball: Plasma balls are the predominant ammunition for Gungan weaponry.
Gunjack Spice: Synthesized from a high concentrated admixture of methylpenatox, glucose, and inotropic chemicals, gunjack markedly increases the strength of its user at the cost of reduced motor coordination, diminished mental acuity, and severe long-term health risks.
Gyrostabilizer: A gyrostabilizer, or repulsorlift harness, artificially reduces the weight of a weapon, allowing a user to heft and aim a heavy weapon with greatly decreased difficulty.
Gyrowheel 1.42.08-Series Recycling Droid: A relatively common sight aboard colony ships and in the streets and alleys of countless settlements, Veril Line Systems' Gryowheel series of recycling droid is, in its way, perhaps the most useful and important droid a colony can possess.
H-7 \"Equalizer\" Blaster Pistol: Considered a masterpiece of the blaster pistol design, Coronet Arms' H-7 "Equalizer" is a precision-tuned blaster pistol that is thought of as both a work of art and a functional weapon.
H-series Megafauna Carriage: Piccatech's H-series megafauna carriage is an essential piece of kit for those working with oversized creatures. A beast must have a slihouette of at least 3 and a Brawn of at least 5 to be fitted with a howdah. Basic howdah configurations are readily available for beasts with a tetrapodal or quadrupedal stance, like fambaa or dewback, at the listed price. Howdahs for creatures with more appendages, like the six-legged acklay, can be had for a small additional fee, generally 500 credits. Howdahs can be armored and fitted with one or two Gunnery or vehicle-scale weapons, following the usual restrictions for silhouette [...]. A megafauna carriage can only be equipped to bestial riding animals. In addition to its other effects, using it allows the character to ride the beast without suffering [...] bareback riding penalties [...].
HBt-4 Hunting Blaster: A high-powered rifle for big game hunting, the Herloss HBt-4 hunting blaster is designed for safaris through dense tropical rainforests.
HH-50 Heavy Blaster Pistol: BlasTech's HH-50 is a heavy, double-barreled, large frame blaster pistol designed mainly for hunting large and dangerous animals.
HIC \"Mercy\" Grenade: The "Mercy" grenade is a unique gas-based stun grenade which is typically used by large corporations and by covert agencies via shell corporations.
HNG F-104 Modular Hangar: Similar in construction to the L-120 modular motor pool, the F-104 modular hangar provides ample space for starfighters or airspeeders and all of the equipment needed to maintain and repair these near-priceless military assets.
HNG F-304 Hangar/Motor Pool: These facilities usually take the form of simple buildings with an open floor area; a large, lockable bay door wide enough for vehicles to easily move through; and two smaller entry doors. They can hold a number of vehicles with a combined silhouette of 12 and no single vehicle larger than silhouette 4. Each also includes a full set of mechanics' tools (allowing characters to perform Mechanics checks to heal droids or repair vehicles or starships without penalty), and an oil bath for droids. When used to repair starships or vehicles stored inside, the character repairs 1 additional hull trauma or 2 additional system strain on a successful check. If purchased multiple times, they can be used as separate vehicle storage facilities.
HOB Heavy Repeating Blaster: The HOB, or high-output repeating blaster, is the SoroSuub Corporation's attempt to loosen BlasTech's and Merr-Sonn's strangleholds on the heavy repeater market.
HR-17 Blaster Rifle: Originally developed for use on colony worlds, the HR-17 has become one of the most common blaster rifles for planetary security forces. Although it lacks the range of many other comparable blaster rifles, the HR-17’s relatively light weight, reliability, and ease of maintenance make it an effective weapon in the hands of a capable shooter. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
HSI–280 Hyperwave Signal Interceptor: A sophisticated subspace receiver, the Bothawui Communications Conglomerate HSI-280 hyperwave signal interceptor is designed to covertly listen in on communications sent through the HoloNet or by hypertransceivers.
HT-77 Cold Assault Armor: The HT-77 pattern of clone armor is a specialized suit designed for operation in cold environments, such as polar regions or planets far from the light of their local sun.
HX2 Antipersonnel Mine: The HX2 is a particularly old model of anti-infantry mine produced by Conner Ship Systems. The HX2 followed on from an Old Republic design, the HX mine, but with little innovation. Due to its availability during the High Republic period, there was no reason for the company to innovate in its production. The fairly small load of detonite within this mine made it cheap by the time of the Clone Wars, giving it a blast only somewhat larger than most fragmentation grenades. At least it was fairly small, able to be carried by commando and other specialty units fairly easily. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Hair Trigger: Many pistols are designed to cycle and fire as quickly as the trigger can be depressed. Hair triggers can only be installed on Ranged (Light) pistols that lack the Auto-fire quality. Base Modifiers: Grants the pistol the Linked 1 and Inaccurate 2 item qualities. Modification Options: 1 Item Quality (Inaccurate –1) Mod.
Han Solo's Customized DL-44 Heavy Blaste: A gift from renowned thief Tobias Beckett, Han Solo's blaster has been at his side for years. Overcharged: Once per round as a maneuver, the wielder may set Han Solo's blaster to fire an overcharged shot. Upgrade the difficulty of the next attack with this weapon once to add the Pierce 7 item quality to that attack. If the attack generates two threat results or a despair result, the GM may cause the blaster to run out of ammo. Once per encounter when Han Solo fails a Deception check, he may spend two advantage results to immediately draw his blaster and make a combat check targeting the target of the failed check as an incidental.
Hand Grinder: A hand grinder uses a rotating wheel coated with rough material to polish down spurs and marks on metal surfaces. A hand grinder adds an automatic advantage result to crafting checks that involve shaping large pieces of hardened material. A hand grinder requires two hands to use as a weapon.
Hand Scanner: The term "hand scanner" is a catchall designation used to describe a variety of handheld scanning devices used for specialized purposes.
Hardened: Power armor relies on its energy source to maintain functionality. This attachment can only be applied to power armor. Base Modifiers: Prevents deactivation of power armor by electromagnetic or ion weapons. Modification Options: None.
Hardened Comlink: Larger, sturdier, and more powerful than standard handheld comlinks, these military-grade communication devices are built to withstand the rigors of battlefield use.
Hauling Harness: Beloved by harried quartermasters across the galaxy, hauling harnesses offer convenient means to lift and move heavy loads. Models include: TaggeCo. Mobile Lifting Gear.
Havoc Carbine: Cheaper, lighter and with a faster rate of fire than a standard blaster rifle, the Havoc is a carbine rifle commonly used by local security forces across the galaxy. Its low production cost combined with its usefulness in tight environments makes this the favoured weapon of many urban security officers. However, as a trade off, the Havoc has a shorter range, is less accurate and has far less stopping power than a standard blaster rifle. The Czerka Arms produced blaster features a heavily shrouded long barrel and a compact two-handed grip, and features attachment points for a glowlight and a simple scope. Painted in a matte black, the weapon has an annoying tendency to easily pick up scratches and marks, leaving many older models more chromium than black. Security forces such as Pre-Mor Enforcement use the Havoc Carbine alongside the Arkus pistol, also produced by Czerka Arms.
Healer's Kit: Those who live on the fringes of society, whether by choice or by circumstance, tend to be more hardy and skillful than the typical galactic citizen.
Heartwood Blaster: Weaponsmiths incorporated heartwood, a plant native to Tholoth, into unique weapons. When wielding a heartwood blaster, a character may suffer strain up to their Force rating as a maneuver. If they do, they add an equal number of automatic advantage results to the next combat check they make that turn.
Heating System: Heating systems are installed in armor to protect the wearer against dangerously low temperatures and frigid environments.
Heavy Battle Armor: Heavy battle armor isn't necessarily the domain of commandos, bounty hunters, and assassin droids alone. Heavy battle armor can be eligible to be fully sealed, or it can take the form of a heavy vest and blast helmet.
Heavy Blaster Pistol: Nearly as powerful as most blaster rifles, heavy blaster pistols are large, bulky sidearms that are nearly impossible to conceal.
Heavy Blaster Rifle: Often only issued to one or two infantry members per squad, heavy blaster rifles deal more damage from a greater distance than anything else in a squad's inventory.
Heavy Clothing: The most basic form of protection for many is simple yet durable heavy clothing.
Heavy Repeating Blaster: Heavy repeating blasters are among the heaviest portable weapons deployed on the galaxy's battlefields. Thanks to their size and weight, heavy repeating blaster are nearly impossible to use effectively without being mounted to a tripod [...]. The weapon's cost includes the cost of a tripod and power pack. Setting up a heavy repeater requires the gun crew to spend three actions.
Heavy Robes: Many Jedi wear thick robes, sometimes with ceremonial significance, and sometimes simply to protect themselves against the elements in the course of their work.
Heavy Staff: Any length of solid material can serve as a weapon in capable hands. This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use.
Helmet Mounted Rangefinder: A fairly common piece of equipment for citizens often performing surveillance or scouting, the helmet mounted rangefinder packs all of the functionality of the more common hand-held electrobinoculars into a hands free device. When activated, the rangefinder allows its wearer to zoom in and view distant targets. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Helmet Range Finder: Mandalorians tend to prefer antenna mounted range finders over hand held versions to keep their hands free. These devices provide enhanced magnification, targeting data, and can even track heat signatures through cover. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Herzfall Communications Collar-Amp: The Collar-Amp is a deceptively simple and useful device that is essentially an unobtrusive and very small amplifier. The Collar-Amp can boost the wearer's voice so that [they] can be heard up to long or extreme range (this depends on terrain, environmental conditions, and even weather). For an extra 100 credits, the user can purchase a model that hooks into a sound system or entertainment system which improves sound quality.
Hetzal Honeyfruit: While honeyfruit is produced on thousands of worlds across the galaxy, any true connoisseur of the fruit knows that Hetzal Prime produces the sweetest and juiciest version. Honeyfruit is incredibly versatile and adaptable to a wide variety of dishes. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Hidden Compartment: These are typically very small, easily concealed compartments hidden in places such as the hollowed-out handle of a blaster pistol or the heel of a shoe.
High-Explosive Missile: High-explosive missiles use powerful shaped-charge warheads that give infantry long-range light anti-armor capabilities.
Hilt-masking Kit: Owners of [lightsabers] may have many reasons to want a subtler presence, yet still keep their lightsaber by their side.
Holdout Blaster: Holdout blasters are lightweight, small-framed pistols designed for ease of handling and concealment. Those searching an individual carrying a concealed holdout blaster add a setback die to their Perception checks.
Holo-Megacaster: One of the standard tools for a public speaker, a holo-megacaster is a variation on a standard holocomm system. A character using a holo-megacaster adds a boost die to Presence-based checks to address a crowd, and allows the character's image and voice to be clearly seen and heard at up to extreme range, depending on the settings used.
Holo-Messenger: Small, complex devices about the size of a human hand, holo-messengers are used when a personal touch is needed in the conveyance of a message.
HoloNet Homing Beacon: Bounty hunters and others engaging in a pursuit may need to track their quarry across tremendous distances, including through hyperspace.
Holo Converter: Holo converters are common holo scrambling devices that allow users to alter a holoprojector's outgoing signal. This can be used with any holographic recording/projecting device, and the converter can store four different alter egos in its database. Base Modifiers: Allows a user to create disguises within holographic recording/projecting systems. Modification Options: 2 Add one additional alter ego to the database Mods.
Holocron: A holocron, or holographic chronicle, is an organo-mechanical data storage device capable of storing tremendous amounts of data in a small package.
Hologoggles: Learning is accomplished much more effectively when it integrates dedicated practice time. When purchasing a set of hologoggles, a character selects one non-combat skill. That skill now counts as a career skill as long as the character owns the goggles (however, at the GM's discretion, the goggles must be used for several hours a day for a period of several months to gain this bonus). A character may only receive benefits from one pair of hologoggles at a time; if [they] wish to count a new skill as a career skill, [they] must discard the current set of hologoggles and purchase a new one.
Holographic Costume: One of the more ingenious utilizations of holographic technology comes in the form of wearable tech, commonly used for communications and advertisements. Models include: A/KT "Versatility" Outfit, Plescinia Micro Holo Suit.
Holographic Disguise Matrix: When setting a trap or infiltrating a facility, altering one's appearance grants a huge advantage. When using a holographic disguise matrix, the character can effectively impersonate the visual appearance of whomever the device has previously recorded. This device adds two boost dice to the user's Deception checks to impersonate the target and adds two setback dice to onlookers' Perception checks to detect the imposter.
Holographic Image Disguiser: Also known as holoshrouds, holographic disguisers (HIDs) use a small, self-contained holoprojector to create a holographic disguise for a single person or object.
Holoscanner: Used in spaceports and high-security locations throughout the galaxy, holoscanners can detect the presence of dangerous or illegal items without the need to physically search individuals or cargo containers.
Horse (Equinoid): "Horse" is the generic term in Basic for a wide array of single-hoofed quadruped species found throughout the galaxy. Skills: Athletics 2, Coordination 1. Talents: None. Abilities: Silhouette 2, Domesticable 2 (downgrade the difficulty of checks to train horses by two), Trained Mount 2 (add two boost dice to a rider's Survival checks while mounted on a horse). Equipment: Trampling hooves (Brawl; Damage 8; Critical 4; Range [Engaged]; Knockdown).
Hover Pram: This small pram floats a few feet off the ground and is a secure way to transport infants or small pets. It can be controlled remotely or programmed to automatically follow an individual. It provides moderate protection from the elements and is capable of being pressurized, holding 2 hours of reserve air. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Hull Sealant Canisters: As indicated by their name, hull sealant canisters contain hull sealant. The sealant is under pressure and can be applied to small gaps or cracks in a ship's hull to prevent air loss due to vacuum. Doing so requires a successful Mechanics check, the difficulty of which depends on the size of the hole. Holes larger than three centimeters in diameter also require additional material, such as a piece of metal or ceramic plating that can be secured in place with hull sealant. 1 difficulty die: Tiny but noticeable punctures in the hull. 2 difficulty dice: Breaches in the hull of up to three centimeters in diameter. 3 difficulty dice: Breaches in the hull of up to six centimeters in diameter. 4 difficulty dice: Breaches in the hull up to ten centimeters in diameter. 5 difficulty dice: Hull ruptures up to .5 meters.
Humidity Vest: Many of the sentient species of the galaxy hail from tropical or oceanic planets. As such, their biology is ill suited for the arid environments found on many plants. To compensate for this individuals wear humidity vests to provide passive hydration. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Hunter's Trophy Armor: Bounty hunters who specialize in wild animals and dangerous beasts sometimes make a habit of collecting trophies to show off their accomplishments.
Hunting Barrel: Designed to increase range and accuracy, a hunting barrel can turn any blaster rifle into a dedicated hunting weapon. A hunting barrel can only be used on a blaster rifle. Base Modifiers: Increases weapon's range by one range band. Adds Cumbersome (+2) quality to the weapon, and increases encumbrance by +1. Modification Options: 2 Item Quality (Accurate +1) Mods, 1 Innate Talent (Lethal Blows) Mod.
Hunting Goggles: Hunting goggles are a type of scanner goggles specifically designed to assist professional guides an hunters in tracking their quarry.
Hunting Knife: Sharp blades are useful as tools as they are as weapons. This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use.
Huntsman Vibrospear: The Corporate Sector–based LaserHone effectively created the vibrospear market with its Huntsman model. The Huntsman vibrospear requires two hands to use.
Hurghol: The mountain-dwelling hurghols are surefooted, reptilian climbers whose many-jointed limbs allow them to move across almost any incline or surface.
Hutt Shell Armor: Crafted especially from the Shell Hutts of Circumtore, these massive exoskeletons are fashioned from segmented cylinders of durasteel.
ID9 Seeker Droid: Seekers are small, repulsor-driven, Class II droids used throughout the galaxy as couriers, scouts, and occasionally assassins.
IFTS-4800 Tracking Gun: The IFTS-4800 tracking gun was a handheld radar device utilized by the First Order. It was equipped with an electromonoscope and calibration dials to improve tracking. It can be calibrated to scan for specific ship types for convenient alerts without requiring a full review of the scanning data. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
IG-100 MagnaGuard: Thankfully rare, IG-100 MagnaGuard droids are relics of the Clone Wars, during which they served as elite bodyguards and shock troops for Separatist leaders.
IG-RM-series Enforcer Droid: The IG-RM series is one of the most common offshoots of Holowan Laboratories' IG-series combat droids. Skills: Brawl 3, Coercion 3, Cool 2, Melee 3, Perception 3, Ranged (Heavy) 3, Vigilance 4. Talents: Adversary 1 (upgrade the difficulty of all combat checks against this target once), Body Guard 2 (as a maneuver, suffer 2 strain to upgrade the difficulty of combat checks made against an engaged ally twice). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: DLT-18 heavy blaster rifle (Ranged [Heavy]; Damage 10; Critical 3; Range [Long]; Auto-fire, Cumbersome 3).
ILF–6500 Life–form Scanner: The ILF-6500 is one of the most powerful long-range omnidirectional life-form scanners on the market. Setting up the ILF-6500 requires either an Average difficulty Survival check or a Hard difficulty Education or Computers check to ensure that the device is properly calibrated and located in the best possible area for taking full advantage of its scanning capabilities.
IP-series Enforcer Droid: The IP-series Interests Protection droid, known colloquially as the “Imperial Police” droid, was produced by Cybot Galactica and released in the last weeks of the Clone Wars. Intended to be sold to corporate clients for security purposes, early stocks were quickly bought out by the new Galactic Empire to replace the old GU-series police droids with the more intimidating and ruthless models which did not come pre-loaded with protocol and threat reduction systems. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
IQA-11 Blaster Rifle: Little more than a generic blaster rifle with an extended barrel, the IQA-11 is a no frills and simple rifle sold to working hunters. As a civilian model, it is inexpensive and easy to acquire. This has made it a favourite among bounty hunters and mercenaries interested in a reliable long range weapon. Its design is fairly bland, and save for the give-away long barrel, appears as an unintimidating weapon on a frontier world. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
IR-5 \"Intimidator\" Blaster Pistol: Merr-Sonn's entry into the repeating blaster pistol market is among the larger weapons in its class, and its range is among the best.
Ilum Crystal: The kyber crystals found deep within the caves of the icy planet Ilum were prized by the Jedi as some of the best crystals for constructing a lightsaber. The Rubat crystals found on Phemis function identically, as far as game mechanics are concerned.
Immune Implant: Given the wide variety of life-forms and the frequency with which many travel the galaxy, it's little wonder that the spread and containment of disease is of major concern to medical professionals and travelers alike.
Impact: Derived from the extract of the nysillim plant of Felucia, impact has grown in popularity in recent years among smugglers and other starship pilots.
Imperial Army Canteen: This standard container is constructed from rugged synthetics and housed in a canvas cover available in a variety of uniform specifications and camouflage patterns.
Imperial Army Field Rations: The only features that distinguish field rations from standard ration packs are their awful taste and hyperinfusion of nutrients that allows a soldier to survive on a single ration for up to one week.
Imperial Army Military Pack: This is a standard military-issue backpack used to stow and carry tents, rations, canteens, excavation tools, and all manner of other gear the soldier needs in the field.
Imperial Hazard Trooper Armor: Imperial hazardous environment protective armor, or "hazard trooper armor," is one of the most rugged armor suits in the Stormtrooper Corps.
Imperial Heavy Repeater: A suppression fire and crowd control weapon used by the Imperial Army, the Imperial heavy repeater fires ionized bursts of metallic bolts at an incredibly rapid rate.
Imperial Storm Commando Armor: Storm commandos are among the Empire's most elite troopers, and they utilize the most advanced equipment available. The armor's helmet is equipped with macrobinoculars and scanner goggles, as well as a comlink. While wearing storm commando armor, a character removes a setback die due to darkness, smoke, or similar environmental factors that obscure vision from Perception, Vigilance, and combat checks. Additionally, this armor adds a setback die to other characters' Perception checks to detect the wearer.
Implant Armor: Favored by infiltrators, spies, and other clandestine operatives, implant armor is useful for occasions where obvious armor protection might be impractical.
Implanted Cyberjack: Usually taking the form of a prosthetic finger or other artificial appendage, a cyberjack allows its use to directly interface with a computer system via a universal serial socket.
In-Helmet Scanner: An in-helmet scanner provides the usefulness of a general purpose scanner in a heads-up display splashed across the inside of the user's helmet.
Incendiary Grenade: Highly restricted and banned on most civilized worlds, incendiary grenades are anti-personnel grenades that cause damage with fire and heat rather than with an explosive charge.
Incendiary Micro-Rocket: Micro-rockets with incendiary payloads explode in flames, using a mixture of chemicals that burn very hot and slow enough to ensure they spread to anything flammable nearby.
Incendiary Missile: Incendiary missiles are powerful, frightening weapons that are described by many galactic military analysts as "a war crime in a bottle."
Individual Field Disruptor: An individual field disruptor creates a sphere of disruptive energy around the wearer that interferes with most energy fields and offers some protection to the wearer.
Industrial Automaton FD-series Extinguis: A highly specialized maintenance droid, fire suppression droids perform the singular function of putting out fires. Fire suppression droids can be used aboard any vehicle or starship with a silhouette of 4 or larger. Skills (group only): Athletics. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum or underwater. Immune to poisons and toxins), Firefighter (may make an Average difficulty Athletics check to extinguish a fire; success extinguishes the fire within a number of rounds determined by the GM, and additional successes may reduce the time needed to extinguish the fire. Particularly large or dangerous fires may require more difficult checks at the GM's discretion), Fire Resistant (immune to fire damage or damage from weapons with the Burn quality). Equipment: Fire suppression equipment.
Industrial Automaton Hologlide J57 Cam D: Cam droids, or hovercams, are small, relatively simple droids equipped with repulsorlifts and recording devices. Skills (group only): Perception, Vigilance. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum or underwater. Immune to poisons and toxins), Flyer (the cam droid can fly). Equipment: Holocam.
Industrial Automaton JC Series Pilot Dro: The JC series produced by Industrial Automaton takes all of these qualities and turns them on their head. Skills: Cool 2, Piloting (Planetary) 2, Piloting (Space) 2, Ranged (Light) 1. Talents: Full Throttle (may make a Hard difficulty Piloting check to increase top speed of vehicle by 1 for one round). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink, and can survive indefinitely underwater or in vacuum. Immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: Light pop-up blaster (Ranged [Light]; Damage 5; Range [Short]; Critical 4; Stun Setting).
Industrial Sorter Droid: Though similar to menial labor droids found throughout the galaxy, sorter droids come with a distinct advantage to other models. Passive salvage brings in vast amounts of materials. Some of the debris is actually functional or repairable. Other items are fit for no more than recycling. Sorter droids are able to make assessments of the condition of materials and sort them based on their relative value. Able to stand the tedium of long shifts with little recharge time, sorter droids are found in deep-space passive salvage vessels throughout the galaxy. They become particularly attractive to captains who see no need to environmentally seal their sorting bays.
Industrial Space Salvage Suit: Though many salvagers make do with standard space suits, there are some who look for just a bit more edge in making targeted collection runs. A salvage suit adds a bit more protection as well as a bit more comfort with onboard heating and a rebreather atmosphere scrubber. Further industrial tools are included in these suits to help zero gravity workers get their jobs done. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Infrabinoculars: Infrabinoculars are specialized tools designed to enhance awareness in urban evironments. A character using infrabinoculars can see through up to two meters of wall, possibly allowing surveillance of a whole building at once. Walls made of dense materials or constructed to shield their interiors may impose one or more setback dice on Perception checks. Some models of infrabinoculars can alternate between this infrared sensor package and a long-range view equivalent to that of electrobinoculars.
Insider's Guide: Seasoned travelers can avoid obvious tourist traps and find the establishments frequented by locals, but asking about particularly unusual needs sometimes arouses suspicion. Models include: Vlaso's Guides to the Galaxy.
InstaMist Generator: Though designed for firefighting and agricultural use, Agrierd Intergalactics' InstaMist generator has found a place in the arsenals of some combatants who use it to create ambushes or make quick escapes.
Insulated Firing Mechanisms: One of the most insidious dangers of the battlefield comes not from the foes combatants face, but from the risks of their own gear failing.
Integrated Ascension Gear: Incorporating a grapnel launcher, line, clips, and other climbing and rappelling equipment directly into a set of armor ensures that the user is always prepared to ascend or descend sheet surfaces in a hurry.
Integrated Dart Launcher: A somewhat less eloquent relative of Whistling Birds, these single fire rocket dart launchers are generally only utilized as a last resort. The launcher housings physically take up very little space on a suit of armor and an untrained observer may not even recognize them as armaments. Most Mandalorians opt to install these dumb-fire launchers on their knee pads, but they can be fitted to any solid material. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Integrated Holsters: By mounting their holsters into the framework of their armor, a combatant can ensure no one catches them unarmed. Base Modifiers: Increases encumbrance threshold by 4. Increases encumbrance by 4. The character may holster up to two weapons of encumbrance 3 or lower in the armor. While holstered, these weapons do not count toward the character's encumbrance value. Modification Options: 2 Add a setback die to other characters' Perception checks to find weapons stored in the armor Mods, 3 Holster up to one additional weapon of encumbrance 3 or lower in the armor Mods, 1 Innate Talent (Quick Draw) Mod.
Integrated Illuminator: Operating in darkness is never ideal, but there are several ways to compensate for a lack of night vision. This attachment can be applied to any ranged weapon that could logically benefit from a spotlight. Base Modifiers: Removes up to two setback dice due to darkness on any checks to use this weapon against targets within short range. Modification Options: 1 Increase spotlight range to medium Mod, 2 Item Quality (Disorient +1) Mods.
Integrated Public Address System: For instances when a full-fledged stage set-up is unavailable, various corporations offer a lightweight, portable speaker system that provides the necessary degree of amplification for public address. Models include: Alvaxi "Impetus" PA-wear, TaggeCo. Broadcaster.
Integrated Scanner: There are times when a trooper needs to scan an area for danger as well as keep their weapon at the ready. This attachment can be applied to any Ranged (Heavy) weapon. Base Modifiers: The weapon incorporates a general purpose scanner that the wielder can use while the weapon is drawn. The wielder adds two automatic advantage results to Initiative checks while the weapon is drawn. Modification Options: 2 Remove a setback die from Initiative checks Mods.
Integrated Slicer Gear: Slicers often conduct their work under strict time constrains and less-than-ideal conditions. This attachment can be applied to any armor. Base Modifiers: The wearer counts as having Slicer Gear, which can be controlled simply by gesturing or vocalizing. Adds a setback die to all checks made by others to correctly identify that the character is slicing. Modification Options: 1 Innate Talent (Technical Aptitude) Mod.
Interrogation Droid: The interrogation droids designed by the Imperial Department of Military Research are among the most feared weapons in the Emperor's vast arsenal.
Ion Charger: A modified powercell and exciter attachment which converts a part of the weapon’s output to ionized energy. This attachment is popular with soldiers who find themselves fighting an increased number of droids on the battlefield. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Ion Micro-Rocket: The ion micro-rocket employs an ion-pulse warhead that disrupts electronic equipment within its blast radius. An ion micro-rocket's Sunder quality can only be activated to damage powered equipment, such as sensors, blasters, and vibroblades. It cannot be used to damage items that operate without power, as they have no circuitry to disrupt.
Ion Pike: Used to incapacitate escaped or malfunctioning droids, these slender lengths of metal are flexible enough to slip between armor plating to reach vulnerable power sources and circuit banks, where they release a powerful ion charge.
Ion Shielding: Rarely encounted in body armor and more commonly (and usually illegally) applied directly to a droid chassis, ion shielding provides protection against ionization blasters.
Ion Thruster Gun: Ion thrusters are used in many different engines, from those of airspeeders to the drives of grand starships. Shaped Nozzle: By adding a metal cone to an ion thruster, a Technician can dramatically increase its potency as a weapon, focusing the energy into a sharp beam rather than letting it disperse in a wide arc. Base Modifiers: Removes the Ion quality. Modification Options: 5 item quality (Pierce +1) Mods. Hard Points Required: 0. Price/Rarity: 75/1. Encumbrance: 0.
Ionization Blaster: Ionization blasters are short-ranged weapons used to disable droids and other electronics. [...] Ion blasters have little effect on sentient beings and deal only strain damage to droids, cyborgs, and vehicles as dictated by their Ion quality. Ionization blasters work only on droids and cyborgs as per their Stun Quality. They have no effect on organic beings beyond a brief and painful sting.
Ionizing Emitter Matrix: Few droids can stand up to a lightsaber, but some heavily reinforced droid models have been known to fight through the destruction of their limbs and even some of their core systems to achieve their objectives.
Ionizing Focusing Coils: Dealing with the droid army of the CIS has made advancements in ion technology a high priority for Republic military researchers and contractors, although research in the field has produced mixed results.
J-10 Dual Blaster Cannon: Originally a Separatist design from the Clone Wars, the J-10 is essentially a scaled-up pair of reciprocating E-5 blaster rifles.
J-7b Beamdrill: The standard beamdrill used by Corellia Mining Corporation, the J-7b has been in service since before the Clone Wars.
JT-12C Jetpack: When clone troopers need to quickly deploy in the field, they are often equipped with the JT-12C jetpack. A JT-12C jetpack allows a person to function as a silhouette 1, speed 2, handling 0, system strain threshold 4 vehicle that can only operate in atmosphere and requires Piloting (Planetary) to operate.
Jedi Battle Armor: When the Jedi battled Sith warriors in ancient days, many wore suits of armor to protect themselves from the weapons of their enemies.
Jedi Commander Armor: The traditional robes of the Jedi are appropriate for their work as mediators and peacekeepers, but when leading detachments of clone troopers, many Jedi have opted for additional protection to fit their new role.
Jedi Reconnaissance Armor: In the chaos of a major conflict, many clone troopers fear that the sheer volume of blaster bolts fired from both sides could catch even a Jedi Master unprepared.
Jedi Temple Guard Armor: The Jedi Temple Guard were some of the best fighters the Jedi Order had to offer, sworn to protect the temple to the end.
Jedi Utility Belt: Designed long ago by skilled artisans, the Jedi utility belt has become common gear for many Knights operating in the field.
Jerethian Clamberwolf: The powerful, heavily furred clamberwolves are the unquestioned apex predators of the forests on their homeworld of Jereth VI.
Jet Pack: A jet pack is ideal for fast maneuverability and surprise attacks. A jet pack allows a person to function as a Silhouette 1, Speed 2, Handling 0, System Strain Threshold 3 vehicle that can only operate in atmosphere, and require Piloting (Planetary) to operate.
Ji Rikknit: Cultivated from the ovum sacks of rikknits of New Plympto, a world located about midway between Corellia and Duro along the Corellian Trade Spine, ji rikknit is a highly-addictive narcotic.
Jump Boots: Jump boots are, essentially, a single-use jetpack built into a thick boots and allows a user to leap over tall obstacles and even fly for a very long time.
KO-2 Heavy Stun Pistol: The Palandrix KO-2 heavy stun pistol is a favored weapon of law enforcement agencies throughout the Core Worlds.
KWS Mk I Saberdart: Kaminoan Weaponsmiths, a subsidiary of Kaminoan Armorsmiths Lts., is a small weapons producer based on the water planet of Kamino.
KX-series Enforcer Droid: Arakyd Industries' KX-series enforcer droids are a rarity in the Empire's armed forcers. Skills: Brawl 3, Coercion 4, Computers 3, Cool 3, Perception 3, Ranged (Heavy) 3, Ranged (Light) 3, Vigilance 3. Talents: Adversary 2 (upgrade the difficulty of all combat checks against this target twice), Nobody's Fool 2 (upgrade the difficulty of all Charm, Coercion, and Deception checks against this target twice). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: None.
Kaadu: Sometimes compared to the Cracian thumper or kybuck, kaadu are swift bipedal duck-billed reptavians native to the swamps of Naboo.
Kal: The traditional accompaniement to the beskad, the kal is a short, deadly dagger typically wielded in a user's off hand.
Kamperdine Clothing Specialists Body Glo: A critical negotiation requires the complete attention of everyone taking part. A body glove may be worn under other clothing or armor. It removes a setback die from any checks due to temperatures outside those of the wearer's preferred environment.
Karrak Spice: This rare spice is well known for its properties as a powerful painkiller and for the degenerative toxins that build up in long-term users.
Katarn-class Commando Armor: Clone commandos often accept missions that have a particularly high risk assessment and require extended independent operations.
Kath Hound: The kath hounds of Dantooine are a species of fierce canine pack hunters, averaging about one meter tall at the shoulders.
Kav-Dann Power Armor: Developed by various interests and groups in secret on Nubia following the Empire's rise to prominence, Kav-Dann series power armor is named after a reptilian predator native to the world.
Kelvarek Consolidated Arms KD-30 \"Dissu: A particularly vicious slugthrower, the KD-30 "Dissuader" fires special ammunition filled with a particularly caustic acid capable of eating through armor, flesh, and bone.
Kiavene: Kiavene’s are pike weapons favoured on the remote world of Shili, especially by members of the Togruta Royal Guard. Tall and lightweight, kiavene are expertly balanced and dangerously effective in trained hands. One end of the weapon is bladed, like a spear and the other end holds a tiny blaster unit, allowing the kiavene to be pointed horizontally and used against longer range targets. Due to the thin shaft of the kiavene the ammo capacity for this blaster feature is small and is often used as a last-ditch attempt in an a emergency, This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Kiirium Coating: An obsolete shielding material once processed in abundance in the Si'Klaata Cluster, kiirium was at one time vital to the manufacture of heavy weapons and starships.
Kimber Stone: Kimber stones are a type of kyber crystal produced by violent volcanic activity on the moon of Y'ness in the N'zoth system.
Knockout Grenade: It is not always necessary or even desirable to kill a threat in order to neutralize it. Knockout grenades have no effect on targets wearing breath masks or without respiratory systems.
Kolto Dart: Loaded with the healing liquid from the planet Manaan, these darts have a curative and revitalizing effect, and are occasionally used by battlefield medics. While the application isn’t particularly comfortable for the target, it is effective. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Koromondain SVT-300 Stun Cloak: At first glance, the Koromondain SVT-300 stun cloak appears to be nothing more than a typical thermal cloak with a handsome metallic sheen.
Krayt Dragon Pearl: Highly sought after by jewelers, artisans, and wealthy collectors, krayt dragon pearls are perhaps the rarest of the crystals used in the construction of lightsabers.
Kybuck: The amazingly swift kybuck is native to the Woolwarricca plains on the Wookiee homeworld of Kashyyyk. Skills: Athletics 2, Brawl 1, Survival 1. Talents: None. Abilities: Silhouette 2, Sure-Footed (kybucks suffer no penalties when moving through difficult terrain). Equipment: Horns (Brawl; Damage 6; Critical 5; Range [Engaged]; Disorient 1).
Kyuzo Energy Bow: Designed to be fired from a mount or mobile platform, a Kyuzo energy bow is a massive bow that fires terrifyingly large, charged bolts.
Kyuzo Petars: Designed by the nomadic warrior Kyuzo species, Kyuzo Petars are brass knuckles with wicked curved blades extending from either side as if the wearer were holding a knife in a standard and backhand grip simultaneously.
Kyuzo War Shield: Originally utilitarian in function, the Kyuzo war shield was designed by the inhabitants of the Kwaidan Peaks, as a headpiece to protect travelers from torrential rains during monsoon season in southern Phatrong.
L-X4 Mortar Launcher: A sleek and simple design, the BlasTech Industries L-X4 Mortar Launcher is compact enough to be transported and operated by a single soldier. The owner’s manual insist that only BlasTech brand baradium core thermal shells may be used in the launcher. However many foolhardy operators claim that Thermal Imploders and Jogan fruit seem to fly just as far. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
L70 Acid Projector: Tenloss Syndicates' rifle-sized L70 acid projector, which is banned in virtually all systems, fires a stream of xenoboric acid capable of penetrating virtually any material.
L7 Liquidsilver Light Blaster Pistol: The Liquidsilver is a classic BlasTech design favored by smugglers and outlaws. Add two setback dice to a character's Perception check to notice a L7 Liquidsilver light blaster pistol stashed aboard a starship.
LBR-9 Stun Rifle: The LBR-9 stun rifle is a long-range stun blaster intended as a sniper weapon for law enforcement and intelligence agencies.
LD-1 Target Rifle: Merr-Sonn's LD-1 is the company's latest foray into the highly competitive long-range blaster market.
LJ-40 Concussion Carbine: The LJ-40 has its origins in the Clone Wars, when heavy infantry squads received early shipments of Arakyd Industries LJ-50 concussion rifles.
LJ-50 Concussion Rifle: The LJ-50 concussion rifle is a heavy weapon dating from before the Clone Wars.
LOM-Series Protocol Droid: When Industrial Automaton entered the protocol droid market with the LOM-series, they gambled on an unusual strategy. Skills: Charm 2, Knowledge (Education) 3, Knowledge (Outer Rim) 2, Knowledge (Xenology) 3, Negotiation 2. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), etiquette and protocol (LOM-series protocol droids allow allies to add a boost die to any Negotiation checks or other checks made to negotiate or mediate), insectoid appearance (LOM-series protocol droids allow allies to remove a setback die from Negotiation checks or other checks made to negotiate or mediate when dealing with insectoid species). Equipment: None.
LRD-Series Envoy Droid: Envoy droid fill an obscure but significant niche in the protocol droid market. Skills: Charm 2, Knowledge (Education) 2, Negotiation 2, Perception 2. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), rhetorical mimic (may make Charm, Deception, and Negotiation checks using the characteristics and skills of its master; such checks suffer two setback dice due to the imperfect mimicry, and the droid does not benefit from any relevant talents that its master possesses). Equipment: None.
LS-150 Heavy Accelerated Charge Particle: The LS-150 was the heavy cousin to the ACP Repeater Gun produced by Arakyd Industries. Bringing the name “Trandoshan Chopper” to an entirely new level, the sheer volume of fire coming out of this repeater would easily overcome the usual trouble it had with armoured targets. Given how often it would run out of ammunition and general lack of utility in close quarter combat, the standard model included a basic bayonet. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
LSx-series Slicer Droid: One of the very few dedicated slicer droids on the market, the LSx-series packs an immense amount of computing power into a small package.
Laminate Armor: Suits of laminate armor consist of a number of formfitted plastoid plates designed to protect important areas, worn over a snug, reinforced armorweave body glove.
Lando Calrissian's Cape Armoire: Lando Calrissian has a fondness for capes, and he keeps dozens in a large cape armoire aboard the Millennium Falcon. If a single cape is found, it weighs a base of 1 encumbrance, has a rarity of 6, and its price starts at 900 credits. Game Masters should determine if these statistics are appropriate for the specific cape in question. Dressed for Success: Once per session, the owner of Lando's armoire may make a Hard difficulty Perception check to select a flattering cape. If successful, they can upgrade one Charm check they make during the next encounter while wearing the selected cape. The wearer may spend three advantage results or a triumph result from the Perception checks to upgrade all Charm checks they make during the encounter once, instead. See the table below for a list of some of the capes PCs might find on their travels.
Lasan Chronicles of the Three: At the surface level, Dai Bendu holocrons resemble those produced by the Jedi. When a character studies the Chronicles, they may use Perception, Knowledge (Lore), or Discipline to decipher it. They must then spend an hour studying the Chronicles, succeeding at a Hard difficulty check of the appropriate skill at the end of it. Depending on their chosen skill, they receive a unique bonus that can be used once during the remainder of the session. Knowledge (Lore) grants the Perspicacity of the Child, whose desire to learn outstrips all else. The character may add a boost die to any one Knowledge skill check they make. Perception gives them the Insight of the Fool: in knowing nothing, they see everything. Once when making a combat skill check, they may re-roll one ability die of their choice.
Lector's Outfit: For major addresses, politicians usually rely advanced, large-scale public address systems. Models include: TaggeCo "Confidence" Outfit.
Leia Organa's Diplomatic Wardrobe: Leia Organa of Alderaan faces immense pressure to be both brilliant and elegant at all times. If a single outfit is found, it weighs a base of 1 encumbrance, has a rarity of 6, and its price starts at 1,700 credits. Game Masters should determine if these statistics are appropriate for the specific outfit in questions. Prepared to Lead: Once per session, the owner of Leia Organa's Diplomatic Wardrobe may make a Hard difficulty Knowledge or Perception check to curate an outfit. If successful, select one social skill and add a boost die to all checks the wearer makes during the next encounter. The wearer may spend two advantage results to increase the bonus to two boost dice, or may spend a triumph result to select one additional social skill. See the table below for a selection of Leia's outfits PCs might find on their travels.
Lesai: Lesai is a variety of spice that is not mined, but grown in a fungus found only on certain kinds of lizards from the planets Zebitrope IV and VIII. Restrictions: Lesai is illegal on most worlds with a strong Imperial presence.
Leviathan Power Armor: Designed by the Mon Calamari for use in subaquatic military operations, Leviathan power armor has seen plenty of use within the Rebel Alliance.
Light Blaster Pistol: Light blaster pistols combine the concealability of a holdout blaster with the performance of a standard blaster pistol.
Light Repeating Blaster: Light repeating blasters might be shouldered weapons or hip-fired, but all feature high rates of fire.
Lightbow: A distinctive part of each Guardian of the Whills' spiritual progression on Jedha, lightbows are traditionally constructed by hand upon completion of the seventh duan towards physical perfection.
Lightfoil: Lightfoils are small lightsabers with one-handed hilts that produce slim blades. This lightsaber uses a basic lightfoil hilt containing an unmodded Ilum lightsaber crystal. This crystal occupies two of the weapon's hard points. When installing a lightsaber crystal into a lightfoil, reduce the damage of that lightsaber crystal by one.
Lightfoil Hilt: [A lightsaber hilt] requires a crystal to become a true weapon (otherwise they deal no damage, have no critical rating, and do not have many of their item qualities).
Lightsaber: For a thousand generations, before their ultimate destruction (and that of the Republic they protected) at the hands of Darth Sidious, the Jedi Order kept the peace on countless worlds with their wisdom, their powerful command of the Force, and with their legendary lightsabers.
Lightsaber Maintenance Kit: Designed and manufactured by and for the Jedi Order, the lightsaber maintenance kit is a compact device containing several useful mechanical implements.
Lightsaber Pike: Similar in construction to a force pike or electrostaff, a lightsaber pike consists of a long, sturdy haft typically made of composites or strong alloys.
Lightsaber Pike Hilt: [A lightsaber hilt] requires a crystal to become a true weapon (otherwise they deal no damage, have no critical rating, and do not have many of their item qualities).
Lightweight Frame: This mod reduces both the weight and bulk of ranged weapons to make them easier to handle and conceal. This attachment can be applied to any weapon. Base Modifiers: Reduces a weapon's encumbrance by 1 to a minimum of 1 Mod. Modification Options: 1 Reduce a weapon's encumbrance by 1 to a minimum of 1 Mod.
Lightwhip: Highly modified lightsabers, lightwhips consist of a short lightsaber haft built from alloys or composites laced either cortosis, phrik alloy, or a comparable energy-resistant alloy.
Liquid Detonite Explosive: Through a delicate chemical process, a particularly creative demolitions expert has perfected a method of turning detonite into a liquid form that can be dispensed from an aerosol can. While the process reduces the volatility of the explosive, the liquid form allows for fast and discreet deployment. A small trigger mechanism is required to set off the explosion. These mechanisms can simply be placed on the surface where the liquid has been applied and can usually be activated by timer or remote detonation. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Liquid-Metal Skeleton Key: As a dataspike is to a security console, the Liquid-Metal Skeleton Key is to more traditional locking apparatus. Particularly industrious or successful thieves and infiltrators often come upon these thin, shimmering metal strips. The exact material compound is unknown to most, as they only concern themselves with the effect; unlocking any door they need to. The teeth of this key are in a constant state of flux, up until the moment it needs to solidify to turn tumblers or activate electrical contacts. Once activated, the Skeleton key is a very perfect fit for that door and not much else. The user must then do a small amount of technician’s work to reset the Skeleton for more illicit deeds. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Load-Bearing Gear: Load-bearing gear, or combat webbing, is a system used by soldiers to carry necessary equipment on their persons in easily accessible pouches.
Loaded Chance Cubes: Countless credits have been won and lost on the roll of simple chance cubes. Characters using loaded chance cubes may add up to two boost dice to Deception checks when gambling. Noticing that chance cubes are weighted requires a Formidable difficulty Perception check on the first roll of the dice, but the difficulty decreases by one for each subsequent roll.
Lockbreaker Hand: A lockbreaker hand is a cybernetic hand packed with tiny tools that allow an agent to bypass most common mechanical and electrical locks.
Lockpicking Tools: While most locks in the galaxy rely on an electrical component, there exists locations and cultures without a steady supply of electricity.
Longbow: Much less technically sophisticated than a blaster, a longbow remains a deadly weapon in properly trained hands. A longbow requires a supply of arrows to fire; if the GM needs to set a price, [they] can charge 1 credit per arrow (or a few copper pieces on Weik).
Longeing Whip: Used for guiding riderless beasts and driving teams of creatures, a longeing whip is an essential piece of a beast rider's kit.
Longshot Blaster: This long-barrelled model of blaster rifle can accurately launch bolts much farther than standard varieties, but this range comes at the cost of penetrating power. This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use.
Longsight: Longsight is a rare substance, extracted from caves beneath Aleen's surface. (one dose) A dose of longsight is typically injected directly into the bloodstream as a maneuver. Once injected, the character increases [their] Willpower by 1 until the end of the encounter. However, at the beginning of each of [their] turns until the end of the encounter, [they] suffer 2 strain.
Loronar Corporation Excavator Precision: Designed for and marketed to scholars, historians, and archaeologists, the PTK contains a number of well-crafted hand tools and essential accessories for excavation of ancient sites and general exploration.
Loronar Spray Rifle: The Loronar spray rifle fires a highly pressurized stream of fluid.
Lorrdian Gemstone: Lorrdian gemstones are kyber crystals mined millennia ago by enslaved Lorrdians during the Kanz Disorders. Base Modifiers: Installing this crystal changes a lightsaber's damage to 7, its critical rating to 2, and the lightsaber gains the Breach 1, Defensive 1, and Sunder item qualities. If the crystal is ever removed, the lightsaber loses these qualities and reverts to its previous base damage and critical rating. Modification Options: 1 Item Quality (Defensive +1) Mods, 2 Item Quality (Deflection +1) Mods.
Lorsain Cavalier Pistol: Beautifully hand-crafted by artisan weaponsmiths from the Lorsain House on the planet Caber Callis, these elegant pistols are a sight to behold. The Lorsain Cavalier pistols were originally designed centuries ago as duelling pistols, however when that practice was outlawed, the Lorsain House re-positioned the Cavalier, as a “premier hunting pistol.” While the weapon’s beautiful build ensures the weapon is forever precise, its low capacity and weak stopping power cement it as a highly-sought out collector’s item and less of a day-to-day blaster. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Low-Feedback Scanner: CryonCorp designed the low-feedback scanner specifically for Gotals and members of other species sensitive to the electromagnetic spectrum.
Low-Friction Coating: Low-friction coatings work by lowering the coefficient of friction against solid armor plates. This attachment can only be applied to armor with hard surfaces, such as heavy battle armor, powered armor, and other suits the GM deems appropriate. Base Modifiers: Increases the armor's melee defense by +1. Adds two setback dice to Mechanics checks to modify or maintain the armor. Modification Options: 1 Skill (Coordination +1) Mod.
Lucky Blaster: A blaster being "lucky" is less about its manufacture and more about its behavior. When a characters uses a single maneuver with a Lucky Blaster to Aim, they gain two boost die on their next combat check. This can only occur once per round, and the PC may not use their second maneuver to aim again.
Luke Skywalker's Lightsaber: After losing his lightsaber in a duel with Darth Vader in the bowels of Cloud City, Luke Skywalker retreated to Ben Kenobi's hut in Tatooine's Jundland Wastes.
Luma Flare: Luma flares are rocket-propelled illumination devices commonly used by both militaries and survivalists. As an action, a character may make an Easy difficulty Gunnery check to launch a luma flare into the air to provide light. If [they] succeed and the flare has room to travel into the air, the flare completely illuminated an area out to long range from the location it was fired. If [they] fail or do not have room to fire it, the flare still ignites, but it but does not travel into the air. Instead, it remains at ground level, illuminating an area out to short range. Whether [they] succeed or fail, the flare's illumination continues for five rounds, after which time it burns out. Though luma flares are not designed as weapons, creative characters can nonetheless use them as such.
Luxury Attire: While some leaders project thier power with intimidating armor or weaponry, others take advantage of their station to live in comfort and style. Gold-trimmed robes and cushioned slippers may not intimidate in the same fashion as a dark figure, armored and masked, but the wearer displays their authority in a different fashion by prioritizing their own comfort and flaunting wealth. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
M-300 Hunting Blaster: Offering unparalleled range for a stun rifle, BlasTech's M-300 hunting blaster comes in a small, lightweight, nearly indestructible frame wrapped in weather-sealed polycarbonate.
M-3PO-Series Military Protocol Droid: Technically classified as a protocol droid, the M-3PO-series was never intended to assist in negotiations or cultural exchanges.
M3 Bulwark Blast Shield: Typically carried by riot or military police, or security in particularly rowdy ports, blast shields are as much a weapon as a protective device.
M4-series Messenger Droid: Another of Cybot Galactica's popular and well-liked entries into the droid market, the M4-series is perhaps the finest messenger droid currently available.
M8 Combat Knife: Made for the GAR by Merr-Sonn, the M8 is based on Merr-Sonn's popular Treppus-2 vibroknife.
M9 \"Boomer\" Heavy Pistol: Czerka's "Boomer" is one of the smallest grenade launchers on the market packed into an oversized pistol frame. During combat, GMs may make this weapon run out of ammunition by spending three threat results or a despair result.
MB-7 \"Ranger\" Range Finder: A system incorporating a simple computer and visual sensors, a TaggeCo range finder calculates the distance to any spot in the user's field of vision, feeding precise information to a visor HUD.
MCI–100 Magnacuffs: Produced by Locris Syndicated Securities, MCI-100 magnacuffs are a type of heavy-duty binders designed to restrain unusually powerful or dangerous individuals.
MDD-12 \"Mercantiler\" Datapad: Produced by MerenData to meet strong customer demand, the MDD-12 is a less powerful and less versatile version of the onboard Mercantiler computer system equipped in MerenData's Exchequer series commerce droids.
MGS-171 Hydraulic Door Pry: While cutting lasers and plasma welders are frequent choices for cutting through a ship's blast doors, they are not always the most pragmatic choice. Most blast doors are designed with energy resistance in mind, and if a door maintains a magnetic seal, cutting through is nearly impossible. On such occasions, brute force is a simple and practical solution. Hydraulic door levers are portable devices of heavy equipment designed with two clamp-like diamagnetic wedges. Once the thin wedges are fitted into any crevice in the door, the diamagnetic wedges begin to disrupt the magnetic seal while the hydraulic pressures pry sections of the door apart. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
MI-Series Security Droid: Holowan Mechanicals' imposing MI-series security droids are some of the most efficient and dangerous droids in the galaxy.
MILBAR F-221 Modular Barracks: Modular barracks come in many configurations for easy tailoring to a unit's specific needs. The F-221 model is a medium-sized building made to hold a platoon-sized unit and that unit's equipment. The barracks include a large common area; smaller rooms for offices, lounges, and refreshers; climate control; and an environmental sealing system proof against chemical and biological attacks.
MMD-18 Molecular Dagger: The MMD-18 is a larger, more durable version of Xana Exotic Arms' MSW-9 molecular stiletto. Due to the MMD-18's inherent fragility, any combat checks made that generate either three threat results or a despair result while wielding one shatters the blade, destroying the weapon.
MR-90 Proton Rifle: One of the more powerful rifles in the Merr-Sonn Munitions inventory, the MR-90 proton rifle is a particle beam weapon originally designed for hunting the biggest, most dangerous prey in the galaxy.
MSE-series Maintenance Droid: The ubiquitous MSE-series "mouse droids" are so common in the galaxy's starships and spaceports as to be nearly invisible.
MSW-12 NanoDagger: Another MSW-series mono-molecular blade offering by Xana Exotic Arms, the MSW-12 NanoDagger is a larger, deadlier version of the MSW-9 Molecular Stiletto.
MSW-9 Molecular Stiletto: Produced by Xana Exotic Arms, the MSW-9 is a small, easily concealable blade that is popular among professionals, politicians, and entertainers.
MW-20 Bryar Pistol: A highly accurate sidearm produced by Blastech Industries, the MW-20 Bryar is slightly less common than its older brother, the K-16 Bryar. The large, single-handed pistol features a long and bulky frame built around a slim grip. Unlike the K-16, the MW-20 features a larger middle section to account for the pistol’s unique rotating barrel ability. To prevent the fast-firing weapon from overheating, a rotating middle section could install a new, cold barrel into the front end of the weapon, allowing it to continue its sustained fire. This feature increases the weight and bulk of the weapon, sacrificing some of the portability and lightness that makes the K-16 Bryar so popular. Nevertheless, for someone looking for accuracy and increased stopping power, the MW-20 is hard to beat. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
MWC-35c \"Staccato Lightning\" Repeating: Morellian Weapons Conglomerate MWC-35c heavy repeating blaster cannon is one of the galaxy's premier support weapons. Switching between modes requires a maneuver. If this weapon is ever destroyed—by a weapon with the Sunder quality or through other means determined by the Game Master—it blows up and deals 10 damage to the wearer and other characters within engaged range.
Macrobinoculars: Simpler, less expensive, and often more reliable than electrobinoculars, macrobinoculars are a common sight throughout the galaxy.
