Magna-Lock Safe: The best personal safes on the market are those crafted from multiple bonded layers of high-quality durasteel and equipped with magna-locks.
Magna Clamps: The problem with lifting awkward loads is not only just the weight, but a matter of hand holds. Magna clamps are designed to not only alleviate such problems of cumbersome loads, but also provide easy attachment points for winch lines, and any situation where a handle would be useful. Magna clamps are little more than handles on a powerful electromagnet. Once powered on, the magna clamp affixes securely to the metal object. Switching the clamp off easily releases the object. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Magnetic Weapon Tether: A magnetic weapon tether is a relatively simple modification that consists of a small magnetic device built into the weapon.
Maintenance Droid: The term maintenance droid applies to an extremely broad array of Class Five droids that are created to perform simple repairs and upkeep on different equipment and vehicles.
Mandalorian Armor: Typically, skilled Mandalorian smiths custom design and build suits of armor. All Mandalorian armor suits include an integrated comlink and are vacuum sealed. Beyond that, most conform to the defense, soak, encumbrance, and hard points values presented above, though exceptions exist. The basic profile of Mandalorian armor represents an unmodified suit of armor, but almost no one who wears the armor leaves it unmodified for long. If the PCs are searching for Mandalorian armor to buy, they can look for a suit with specific attachments already installed. In either case, the rarity of the suit increases by 1, or to the rarity of the rarest installed attachment, if that would be higher. The base price of the suit is increased by the cost of the attachments.
Mandalorian Chamber: A relatively recent innovation commonly known as the Mandalorian chamber [...] enables the blaster to simultaneously apply characteristics of both the deadly and stun settings.
Mandalorian Ripper: An energy-propelled slugthrower of Mandalorian make, the ripper is a powerful weapon that fires unguided shrapnel at high speeds. The projectiles are capable of piercing personal shielding and cause deep, grievous wounds to the target. Despite being slugthrowers, these weapons are classified as disruptor weaponry and are illegal to produce or possess on any Republic world. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Manka Cat: A rare example of a feline predator with a pack instinct, manka cats have been found across the mountains and grasslands of a number of the Core Worlds still lightly settled enough to have wilderness areas.
Mantellian Flutterplume: The flutterplumes of Ord Mantell are a species of large, carnivorous avians with powerful, hooked beaks and four sets of razor-sharp talons.
Marcan Herb: A mild narcotic popular among Hutts, Marcan herb can induce a sense of euphoria upon inhalation.
Mark V \"Sand Panther\" Hunting Rifle: Engineers based Coronet Arms' Mark V "Sand Panther" on an ancient rifle once used to hunt Corellian sand panthers.
Marked-Target Firing Lock: In the chaos of a fire fight, shots can go astray, damaging property or bystanders. This attachment can be installed on any ranged weapon that does not possess the Blast or Auto-fire quality. Base Modifiers: The wielder of a weapon with this attachment may designate a target as an incidental while performing the aim maneuver. Once a target has been designated, the weapon cannot be fired at another target. All combat checks made with the weapon to attack the designated target cancel one additional threat result after threat has been used to cancel any advantage results. Only one target can be designated at a time, and the wielder may disengage the lock as a maneuver. Modification Options: 1 Cancel an additional threat result Mod.
Marked Sabacc Deck: A popular game of chance, throughout the galaxy, sabacc is difficult to master and even more difficult to cheat at. Players using a marked sabacc deck gain a boost die to Deception checks when gambling. Noticing that a deck is marked requires a Hard difficulty or Daunting difficulty Perception check, depending on the quality of the deck (a higher quality marked sabacc deck costs 1,000 credits).
Marksman-H Combat Remote: Also referred to as a "seeker droid" or "training remote," the Marksman-H by Industrial Automaton is a versatile little droid that has found many niches, some of them unintended.
Marksman Barrel: Marksman barrels are designed to meet the needs of soldiers for whom long range accuracy is a necessity. This attachment may only be applied on blaster rifles. Base Modifiers: Increases weapon's range by one range band. The weapon gains the Cumbersome 2 quality. Modification Options: 2 Weapon Quality (Accurate +1) Mods, 1 Innate Talent (Sniper Shot) Mod.
Master Lodaka's Lightsaber: In the galaxy at large, if Lothal is known at all, it is as an Imperial world home to a Sienar Fleet Systems production line for TIE fighters.
Mechanic's Utility Suit: Not so much armor as an adaptation applied to the rugged clothing common to Technicians on the go, a utility suit is bedecked with pockets, attachment hooks, and gear straps.
Mechanical Dianoga: Designed for reaching tight spaces in a starship, the mechanical dianoga is a meter-and-a-half-long flexible actuated tube.
Med Nog: While bacta is still far and away the most popular and effective way of treating injuries throughout the galaxy, it is also one of the more expensive ways. For spacers in a pinch, looking for a cheaper way to heal, there is Med nog. Med nog consists of a very small amount of bacta diluted by water and stim-cot, an anti infection medication.When ingested, Med nog can quickly prevent any internal infection from a wound. Unlike pure bacta however, Med nog cannot regrow tissue or help knit broken bones back together. Med nog also tastes very bad, with many people only able to stand a sip or two. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Medical Droid: Medical droids take a variety of forms, from simple attendant droids capable of dressing flesh wounds or diagnosing basic illnesses up to the elaborate and incredibly advanced surgical droids that can be found on military starships and major urban centers throughout the galaxy.
Meditation Focus: Many of the Force and religious traditions throughout the galaxy develop some form of meditation focus, whether it be the prayer beads of the Solaran Hermetic Order, the Cosmic Wheel of the Voss Mystics, or Dagoyan incense.
Medium-range Transceiver: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Portable Medium range (25 light-years) transceiver. May only use transceiver relays (not comlink relays). Interception of transceiver messages are automatically hardened to Hard difficulty.
Medpac: Often described as "a medical frigate you can fit in your pocket," a standard medpac is a larger and better-equipped version of the common emergency medpac.
Medtech Mini-Med: The Mini-Med is less a droid and more a skilled set of surgeon's tools. Skills: None. Talents: None. Abilities: Surgeon's Aid (When a minion group of one or more Mini-Meds performs the Assist maneuver to help a character with a Medicine check, that character counts as having +1 rank in the Surgeon talent per Mini-Med in the group.) Silhouette 0. Equipment: None.
Med–Aid Patch: "Med–aid" is a general term applied to single-use bandages and compresses that have been infused with bacta, antibiotics, coagulants, and other healing agents.
Mem-Stik: Produced in a variety of styles and sizes, mem-stiks are simple, unpowered data storage devices.
Mephite Crystal: Mephite crystals are rare, lustrous kyber crystals found in a handful of places in and around the Adega system. Base Modifiers: Installing this crystal changes the lightsaber's damage to 8, its critical rating to 2, and the lightsaber gains the Breach 1 and Sunder item qualities. Force-sensitive characters using Force powers to sense their surroundings automatically detect a lightsaber using a mephite crystal if it is in range of the power. If the crystal is ever removed, the lightsaber loses these qualities and reverts to its previous base damage and critical rating. Modification Options: 3 Damage +1 Mods, 1 Decrease the weapon's critical rating by 1 to a minimum of 1 Mod, 1 Item Quality (Vicious +1) Mod.
MerenData KLC Series Exchequer Droid: MerenData's KLC Series Exchequer Droid is marketed throughout the galaxy as "The Merchant's Best Friend." Skills: Charm 2, Computers 2, Cool 2, Negotiation 2. Talents: Wheel and Deal 2 (when selling goods legally, gain 20% more credits). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink, and can survive indefinitely underwater or in vacuum. Immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: Mercantile computer with holonet connection (add a boost die to all Negotiation checks while in the Outer Rim and two boost dice to Negotiation checks while in the Core).
Merr-Sonn Model 44 Blaster Pistol: Representative of blaster pistols across the galaxy, the Model 44 is a reliable pistol that is more than adequate for most situations.
Merr-Sonn N-57 Armor: The latest suit in Merr-Sonn's N-series armor for combat engineers has broken with the company's typical policy of selling exclusively to established planetary militaries, and is available for general purchase on the open market.
Micro-Grenade Launcher: This weapon consists of a basic, smoothbore barrel attached to a stock and magazine with an automatic feed-system. This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use.
Micro-Rocket Armor Mounting: Micro-rockets are flexible (if dangerous) weapons favored as secondary arms by many bounty hunters. This attachment can be installed on any armor. Base Modifiers: Enables user to fire one loaded micro-rocket without needing to perform a maneuver to draw. Increases encumbrance by +3. As micro-rockets are Limited Ammo 1 weapons, the launcher has the Limited Ammo 1 quality and thus must be reloaded with a new micro-rocket to be fired again. Modification Options: 5 Mount one additional micro-rocket Mods, 1 Remove a setback die from checks to fire micro-rockets from this attachment Mod.
Micro-Rocket Launcher Pistol: While micro-rockets are most often mounted on armor or on other weapons like blaster rifles, there are weapon frames that only fire micro-rockets.
MicroData P-2 Pocket Attaché: An improvement on MicroData's versatile Pocket Secretary and the more common Versafunction88 datapad, the P-2 Pocket Attaché provides the user with a handheld multipurpose administrative tool and organizer combined with state-of-the-art security and encryption rivaling that of Imperial intelligence agencies.
Micro Detonite Charges: Not as clumsy or random as a baradium detonator, micro detonite charges produce contained high energy explosions. They are easily affixed to most surfaces and can be triggered by a proximity sensor, impact detector, remote activation, or a timer. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Microaxial Starmapper Handheld Navicompu: Microaxial LLC is a relatively small technology firm from the Core Worlds region that specializes in navicomputers and navigational aids.
Microdroid Listener: These tiny spiderlike automata, little more than mobile audio bugs, are barely worth the name "droid." Planting this device without being noticed requires an opposed Skulduggery check vs. Vigilance. Once the bug is in place, a character must make a Daunting difficulty Vigilance check to locate it even when they are specifically searching for one. The microdroid has no self-defense or evasive capabilities, although some manufacturers fit their models with a self-destruct in case of tampering.
Military Field Manual: From comprehensive manuals issued to formal military soldiers to the underground publications used to instruct resistance fighters in guerrilla combat, field manuals can be found wherever troopers are common.
Military Holdout Blaster: Diplomats rarely fare as well as their allies in extended firefights or brawls, but sometimes, a single shot might be sufficient to enable escape from a perilous situation. Models include: Czerka Arms 411, BlasTech C-91.
Military Traumapac: Sending every soldier into battle with a medkit is not feasible due to weight and cost limitations, but SyntheTech/MedTech has optimized its traumapac for militias, mercenaries, and local defense forces.
Mine Detector: Mine detectors are specialized handheld scanners designed to locate buried or concealed anti-personnel and anti-vehicle mines.
Mini-Torpedo, Anti-Personnel: Anti-personnel mini-torpedoes function similarly to fragmentation grenades.
Mini-Torpedo, Armor Piercing: Armor piercing mini-torpedoes are designed to damage single targets, utilizing a shaped explosive charge.
Mini-Torpedo, Ink: These mini-torpedoes contain a reservoir filled with an obscuring black liquid that readily disperses into a large cloud upon detonation.
Mini-Torpedo, Ion: For use primarily against droids and vehicles, ion mini-torpedoes were a popular choice for the Mon Calamari during the Battle of Mon Cala.
Mini-Torpedo, Net: The net mini-torpedo is designed to ensnare targets rather than damage them.
Mini-Torpedo, Stun: Combining a concussive blast with a series of blinding flashes, stun mini-torpedoes are especially effective underwater.
MiniMag Shoulder-Launched Proton Torpedo: The MiniMag PTL is named due to its specific purpose as a launcher for proton torpedoes. This makes it a devastating weapon, and extremely dangerous to use at short range. The warheads used by the MiniMag are half-yield versions of those found on starships, and lack the already-limited homing systems. It was developed by Krupx Munitions—a starship weapons manufacturer—after being commissioned by the late Republic who sought an anti-armour weapon to deal with the increasing presence of heavily armoured droid craft that filled the late Clone Wars. Despite being built for proton torpedoes, the MiniMag is still capable of firing other ordnance. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Miniature Flame Projector: Over the course of their many wars with the Jedi, ancient Mandalorians found flame projectors to be highly effective against lightsaber wielding foes. Though direct conflicts with Jedi are now few and far between, the weapon has remained an essential part of most Mandalorians’ arsenals. The projector itself is generally mounted on a gauntlet, with a remote line connecting to a concealed fuel reservoir. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Missile Tube: Missile tubes are shoulder-fired, portable launchers capable of firing a variety of small but deadly warheads. Additional ammo for a missile tube costs 500 credits per rack of six. Other missiles may be substituted for the standard type, replacing the damage, critical rating, range, and special qualities with that of the alternate missile. Alternate missiles may be purchased for their listed price.
Mk III Flak Vest: The Mk III flak vest from Pacnorval Defense Systems is a basic jacket consisting of light durasteel plates under layered ballistic fabric.
Mk III Katarn-class Commando Armour: Released less than a year following the Battle of Geonosis, the armour was reinforced further to be capable of sustaining heavier ordnance, to the point of partly deflecting laser cannon rounds. In addition to this, the increased internal power sources was able to be rerouted into a proprietary optical camouflage system that was noted as the Night Ops mode. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Mk II \"Hotspot\" Insulated Armor: Flame troopers are among the more specialized units in the Grand Army of the Republic. Mk II "Hotspot" insulated armor comes with a built-in comlink. It adds two boost dice to the wearer's checks to avoid adverse effects from exposure to extreme heat or toxic gases and reduces the damage the wearer takes from fire and hazardous environments by 1. Additionally, when targeting the wearer, attackers must spend one additional advantage result to activate the Burn item quality and treat the quality's rating as one lower.
Mk II \"Steelskin\" Anti-concussive Armo: Some of the heaviest non-powered personal armor on the market, Steelskin armor is illegal on most worlds and sure to draw significant attention in even the roughest of Outer Rim settlements.
Mk II EMP Grenade: Built by SoroSuub specifically for the GAR, the Mk II EMP grenade is the latest word in anti-droid weapons.
Mk II Katarn-class Commando Armour: This improved variant of the original Katarn-class commando armour manufactured by Kaminoan Armoursmiths featured hardened electronics and shielding systems. This was primarily in response to the demonstrated effectiveness of Verpine shatter guns and EMP weaponry against the commandos of Omega Squad. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Mk IV Riot Armor: The Mk IV riot armor is one of Creshaldyne Industries' most popular models. This armor includes a breath mask that allows the user to filter out airborne toxins and gases (but not to breathe in atmospheres without the character's required atmosphere mix). In addition, when staggered or disoriented, the wearer reduces the duration of the effect by one round, to a minimum of one round.
Mk IX Personal Mimetic Concealment Suit: Cyricept's Mk IX Personal Mimetic Concealment Suits use active stealth technologies combined with advanced textiles to provide some of the best personal stealth armor available.
Mk I Katarn-class Commando Armour (homeb: Clone commandos often accept missions that have a particularly high risk assessment and require extended independent operations. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Mk I NightStalker Infiltrator Suit: The Mk I NightStalker is a lightweight, surprisingly durable suit of armor that Bantin Corp recently produced. When wearing a NightStalker suit, the wearer adds an automatic success result to Stealth checks they make and adds an automatic threat result to social skill checks they make.
Mk VIII Gravitic Mortar Launcher: Built by Golan Arms, the Mk VIII launcher is an uncommon weapon. When a gravitic round detonates inside a minefield, it triggers every mine within medium range. Game Masters may use two threat results or a despair result to detonate only a fraction of mines within the gravitic shell's area of effect. Undetonated mines remain active and still pose a threat to Player Characters, their allies, and their vehicles.
Mk. III Modular Backpack: Creshaldyne Industries' Mk. III Modular Backpack is a civilian version of its popular Mk. IV military reconnaissance pack.
Mk. VIII Vibrosaw: Greel Wood Logging Corporation is a small, privately owned logging concern founded solely for the harvesting the of the highly sought-after Greel trees from the third and fourth planets of the Pii System.
Model-1 \"Nova Viper\" Blaster Pistol: The Raider Arms Model-1 "Nova Viper" uses an elegant, long-bodied duelist pistol frame that provides unmatched accuracy.
Model 134a \"Buzzsaw\" Rotary Cannon: Loud, heavy, and dangerous, the Model 134a is among the heaviest portable slugthrowers. The Model 134a can only be fired using the Auto-fire quality. Game Masters may spend three threat results or a despair result to have the weapon overheat and jam. When a Buzzsaw jams, the user must perform a maneuver to clear the jam before it can be fired again.
Model 14 \"Stalker\" Vibrospear: The "Stalker" is Merr-Sonn Munitions' answer to LaserHone's successful Huntsman vibrospear. The GM may spend two threat results from any combat check with this weapon to have it produce a loud noise, spooking nearby animals, alerting foes, and adding two setback dice to the user's next Survival check in that area to hunt or track prey. The Stalker vibrospear requires two hands to wield.
Model 201 Mortar System: The Model 201 mortar is a portable light artillery system used by specialized clone artillery troopers. The weapon consists of three parts—the launcher, a heavy bipod, and the ammunition magazine—and is operated by a specially trained mortar team. A mortar team consists of three clones: one to carry the weapon, one to carry the bipod, and one to carry the ammo. The Model 201 can only be used at medium range or further due to the arc of its shells.
Model 31 Palm Stunner: This high-output stun weapon by Merr-Sonn takes the form of a flat lens small enough to conceal in the palm of a hand. A character can use a palm stunner on an unaware target with an opposed Skulduggery check vs. Resilience or Stealth. A successful check automatically incapacitates a minion or deals 12 damage to the strain threshold of a rival or nemesis. In addition, each success result on the opposed Stealth check increases the damage dealt by one. While Endless Vigil: A Sourcebook for Sentinels shows this as "opposed Stealth versus Resilience check", the Errata (Page 2) corrects this to "opposed Skulduggery versus Stealth check".
Model 38 Sharpshooter's Rifle: One of the best-selling firearms in Czerka's Legacy Arms slugthrower product line, each Model 38 is laser measured to accomodate a shooter's natural stance and includes a custom-fitted stock, trigger, and optic for optimal shooting comfort.
Model 40 Field Repulsor Hoist: The Model 40 field repulsor hoist began life as a field-expedient (aka jury-rigged) vehicle maintenance lift designed by Alliance Army mechanic Lidiah Alexakis.
Model 4 \"Thunderhead\" Scatter Gun: Czerka Arms designed the Model 4 as part of its Legacy Arms line of slugthrower weapons to compete with Adostic Arms' popular AA8 scatter gun.
Model 53 \"Quicktrigger\" Blaster Pistol: Merr-Sonn Munitions' Model 53 "Quicktrigger" blaster pistol is popular across the galactic frontier backwaters thanks to its superior customizability and reliability.
Model 7 Therm-ax: Though similar in appearance to a vibro-ax, the SoroSuub Model 7 Therm-ax operates on very different principles. A therm-ax requires two hands to wield.
Model 80 Blaster Pistol: Merr-Sonn Munitions' Model 80 Blaster Pistol is an old standby for planetary defense forces, militias, and mercenary units.
Model L33 Survey Scanbot: Though some salvagers prefer the thrill and danger of hazarding their way into a derelict or half-destroyed starship in hopes of finding some hidden gem of treasure, others prefer to do the initial evaluation from the safety of their starship. The Survey Scanbot is a simplified, civilian version of a probe droid. The scanbot's features are relatively modest compared to their military or espionage counterparts. Nonetheless, they are well suited to maneuvering into small areas while recording and transmitting their findings. Further, a modest personality matrix allows for easy communication and also some interpretation of data with real-time communication.
Model Q4 Quickfire Holdout Blaster: Touted as a revolution in light blaster design, Merr-Sonn's Q4 Quickfire is one of the more unique holdout blasters on the market today.
Modular Backpack Frame: To ensure each soldier can carry the necessary kit into battle, military organizations utilize a modular load-bearing frame, which accepts up to two rectangular modules to create a pack tailored to a specific combat role.
Molten Magnet: Magma from Sembla has fascinated researchers for centuries.
Mon Cal Defenses Mini-Torpedo Launcher: The Mon Cal Defenses mini-torpedo launcher is a portable weapon designed exclusively for sub-aquatic combat situations. The mini-torpedo launcher can only be used effectively underwater. All combat checks to operate it outside of water have their difficulty upgraded twice. The profile provided with this weapon is for the default torpedoes. Specialized torpedoes can be loaded into the launcher. While these torpedoes are loaded into the launcher. While these torpedoes are loaded ,they replace the damage, critical rating, range, and item qualities of the original weapon with their own. Regardless of what is loaded in the launcher, it maintains the Cumbersome quality.
Mon Calamari Battle Baton: The Mon Calamari battle baton is a simple energy weapon in the shape of a ribbed scepter.
Mon Calamari Coral Pike: The coral pike is an ancient weapon of the Mon Calamari people.
Mon Calamari Energy Lance: Wielded by the Mon Calamari Knights during the Clone Wars, energy lances are designed to be used from atop keelkana mounts.
Mon Calamari Organic Gill: Despite their amphibious nature, the Mon Calamari have long made use of a symbiotic blob to breathe underwater for extended periods of time.
Mon Calamari Spear Blaster: A large blaster rifle of Mon Calamari design, the spear blaster is standard issue among the Mon Calamari Guard.
Mono-molecular Edge: All bladed weapons, even vibro weapons, can benefit from an edged sharpened to mono-molecular thickness and toughened through laser-forging techniques.
Morgukai Cortosis Staff: Relics of the Morgukai, an ancient Nikto warrior society that hunted Jedi and Sith alike, these weapons are exceedingly rare.
Motion Tracker: This sophisticated device incorporates a full suite of onboard sensors and computers to track and predict the motion of a target, then feed that data back to the user through a variety of means, including a helmet HUD, a hardwired feed jacked directly into the brain, or other methods.
Mountaineer Armor: Another offering from Ayelixe/Krongbing Textiles, mountaineer armor is a suit of light, flexible armor designed to assist the wearer in climbing and navigating mountainous terrain.
Multi-Goo Gun: The multi-goo gun, an all-in-one spray injector, has a self-cleaning element that allows Technicians to switch out lubricant, adhesive, or acid cartridges with no loss in function.
Multi-Tool Hand: Multi-tool hands are basic cybernetic hands equipped with a suite of basic tools. A multi-tool hand provides all the benefits of a tool kit.
Multi-optic Sight: A complex and sophisticated piece of equipment, the multi-optic sight is a wonder of modern sensor technology. This attachment can be fitted to any ranged weapon. Base Modifiers: Removes up to two setback dice on any checks to use this weapon due to smoke, darkness, or other environmental conditions that obstruct vision. Modification Options: 2 Skill (Perception) Mods.
Muon Gold: Made from engine lubricant subjected to an intense bombardment of muons, muon gold is a designer spice that provides the user a sense of mental focus and reduces inhibitions.
Musical Instruments: The number of different musical traditions across the galaxy is truly staggering. Anklet-chimes: These small, clear-toned chimes are secured to the performer's ankels with a length of string or chain, tinkling softly as the performer moves. Bandfill: Bandfills are complicated wail instruments featuring several horn bells. Chin-harp: Developed by the Weequay, the chin-harp creates music by striking notes with a small chord hammer. Common Instruments: Most cultures invent some form of horns, drums, and reed instruments as part of their development. Floonorp: Scratch-built musical instruments made out of spare Podracer parts, floonorps are more likely to be assembled than purchased. Kloo horn: Double-reeded wind instruments, kloo horns are commonly used by jatz musicians and create that music's distinctive higher notes.
Mustafarian Lava Flea: Perhaps one of the most unusual mounts in the galaxy, the Mustafarian lava flea is a giant arthropod that evolved for life on the volcanic environment of its homeworld.
N-20 Baradium-Core Code Key Thermal Deto: The N-20 thermal detonator was likely the most mass-produced grenade in the galaxy in recent times. Found on the armour belt of every clone trooper, and later on every storm trooper. It was a much more regulated form of the infamous weapon, with a variable blast intensity and built-in security systems which prevented others from using them after being salvaged from the battle- field. Some enterprising technicians, particularly those who don’t value their life that much, swear they are able to slice through the extreme encryption of the explosives, but you’d be better off finding a black market salesman of regular thermal detonators instead. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
N-4 Noise Grenade: Developed for crowd dispersal and riot control, Merr-Sonn's N-4 noise grenade emits a sharp blast of broad-spectrum sound, causing intense pain to those near the epicenter.
N-S9 Starpath Unit: An extremely valuable and extremely restricted piece of equipment, Starpath units are installed in the bridges of Imperial capital ships at construction. The Starpath uses proprietary Imperial signals and frequencies to coordinate and map the relation of a starship to every Imperial asset, whether it is an installation or a vessel, for nine radial parsecs. These units are invaluable in fleet deployment and assignment, allowing an Imperial captain to see every asset available to them within a specified range. Of course, those with more nefarious needs, would also find a Starpath to be an incredibly useful piece of equipment, allowing them to see every Imperial ship of installation and allowing them to steer well clear of them. Starpath Units can only be found at Imperial shipyards or at high-security factory sites, so getting a hold of one on the black market is incredibly rare.
NX-14 Needler: "Needlers" refer to an obscure series of dart shooters that rely on electromagnetic rail propulsion. A needler removes all setback dice added by a personal deflector shield. Anyone searching an individual carrying a concealed needler adds one setback die to their Perception check to find the weapon.
Naga Sadow's Poison Blade: An ancient Sith sword, forged by the talented alchemist and Dark Lord of the Sith Naga Sadow. The blade itself was known to strike fear in the hearts of any that saw it, due to the painful and often lethal poisons it left within its inflicted wounds. Following Sadow’s death, the blade was interred in a tomb of Korriban, dedicated to his name but not containing his body. Other swords were forged in similar style, though none seem to ever have reached the same level of craftsmanship fearsome reputation as the original. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Nala Tree Frog: Arboreal frogs that live among the nala trees of Ylesia, these amphibians are considered a delicacy among Hutts. (12 frogs)
Nannarium Root: Large blue flowers native to Drall, nannariums are often cultivated for their beauty and sweet aroma when in bloom. (dose) A character under the effects of distilled nannarium root extract may upgrade the ability of any Intelligence or Cunning-related check once, though doing so requires single-minded concentration and causes them to suffer 2 strain. The effects of a single dose of nannarium root lasts for about an hour.
Nano-droids: These microscopic machines typically take the form of spheres, invisible to the naked eye, that are stored in a suspension of electrolytic transfer fluid.
Narglatch: There are many subspecies of narglatch in the galaxy, found in places ranging from the frozen wastes of Orto Plutonia to the swamplands of Naboo.
Needle Gloves: To all appearances, needle gloves look like typical hand coverings; however, with a specific hand gesture, the wearer can extend an ultrathin needle that is otherwise nearly undetectable.
Networked Slicing Uplink: Some slicers make use of a rare and highly illegal set of droid modifications to mask their slicing signatures by routing them through a droid's systems, which fill the signature with junk data from the droid's base-level processes.
Neural Recorder: An individual with a neural recorder installed gains the ability to record and store sensory information with the veracity and recall of and advanced computer system.
Neuro-Saav Cyborg/Droid Interface: Neuro-Saav's cyborg/droid interface is a supplementary cybernetic that must be used in conjunction with a cybernetic brain implant.
Neuromachine Interface: A vehicle control system designed to be integrated directly into the nervous system, this ingenious (if notoriously difficult to use) device allows the user to control a vehicle with a thought, moving it as if it were part of their own body.
Neuronic Lash: The neuronic whip has earned a reputation as the preferred weapon of slavers. Borstel's neuronic lash attempts to mitigate this negative impression through both its appearance and its overall function.
Neuronic Whip: Used by slavers across the galaxy to keep their "merchandise" in line, the neuronic whip is a terrible weapon banned from many worlds.
Neutron Pixie: A powerful hallucinogenic spice, Neutro Pixie typically has a somewhat calming effect on the user. A dose of Neutron Pixie grants the user two automatic advantage results on Agility- and Willpower-related checks until the end of the encounter. Once its effects end, the character suffers an automatic threat result all Brawn, Intellect, and Cunning-related checks for the remainder of the game session due to nausea, muscle aches, weakness and mental torpor.
Nexu: Originally native to the jungles of Cholganna, nexu have been transplanted across the galaxy since they were first discovered to be domesticable.
Night Vision Scope: This attachment uses an optical system similar to a set of electrobinoculars that shows infrared wavelengths of light, effectively allowing the shooter to track heat signatures in dark or concealed areas, or through walls.
Nightstinger Blaster Rifle: Officially classified as a sporting blaster, Xerrol Corporation's Nightstinger was marketed as a powerful, well-balanced rifle for big game hunting, which, while true, omits its ability to fire completely invisible blaster bolts with the flick of a switch.
Nishalorite Stone: Nishalorite stones are found on worlds with particularly strong magnetic fields. Base Modifiers: Installing a nishalorite stone changes a lightsaber's damage to 7 and its critical rating to 3. In addition, the lightsaber gains the Breach 1 and Sunder item qualities. Additionally, a Force-sensitive character with this lightsaber in their possession adds an automatic advantage result to any of their checks made to determine their position, navigate terrain, or find power sources or active technology. If the crystal is ever removed, the lightsaber loses these qualities and reverts to its previous base damage and critical rating. Modification Options: 1 Innate Talent (Planet Mapper) Mod, 2 Item Quality (Disorient +1) Mod, 1 Item Quality (Vicious +1) Mod.
Noble Regalia: Although often inconvenient to wear, severely limiting to movement, and historically archaic, few things command innate respect as much as the traditional attire of a planet's nobility.
Nomad Greatcoat: Long associated with the inhabitants of the Nomad Mountains, this distinctive greatcoat has become popular since its adoption by the military of Corellia as cold weather gear.
Nullicaine: Nullicaine is a staple of field treatment useful for helping soldiers to fight on despite injuries. A character may apply nullicaine to themself or an engaged character by making an Easy difficulty Medicine check and choosing one Easy, Average, or Hard difficulty Critical Injury the target is currently suffering. If the check succeeds, the target immediately suffers 3 strain and ignores the chosen Critical Injury until the end of the encounter. Nullicaine only affects living creatures.
OK-98 Blaster Carbine: A unique SoroSuub design featuring twin power packs to double ammunition capacity, the OK-98 sold poorly even though it performed adequately.
OOM-series Command Droid: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Skills: Discipline 1, Knowledge (Warfare) 2, Leadership 3, Ranged (Heavy) 2, Ranged (Light) 2, Vigilance 2. Talents: Adversary 1 (upgrade the difficulty of combat checks targeting the command droid once), Field Commander (as an action, may make an Average difficulty Leadership check; one ally may immediately suffer 1 strain to perform a free maneuver). Abilities: B-series Commander (if targeting only B- and OOM-series droids, may use Field Commander as a maneuver and the targets don't suffer strain), Command Computer Interface (B- and OOM-series droids within Medium range of the command droid gain a boost die to all checks as long as a central command computer is online), Droid (does not need to breathe, eat or drink, and can survive in vacuum or underwater; immune to poisons and toxins).
OOM-series Pilot Droid: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Skills (group only): Astrogation, Computers, Gunnery, Piloting (Planetary), Piloting (Space), Ranged (Heavy), Ranged (Light). Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat or drink, and can survive in vacuum or underwater; immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment—sold separately: E-5 blaster rifle (Ranged [Heavy]; Damage 9; Critical 3; Range [Long]; Stun Setting).
OOM-series Security Droid: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Skills (group only): Perception, Ranged (Heavy), Ranged (Light), Vigilance. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat or drink, and can survive in vacuum or underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Security Network Programming (for every three security droids in the minion group, add a boost die to Perception and Vigilance checks). Equipment—sold separately: E-5 blaster rifle (Ranged [Heavy]; Damage 9; Critical 3; Range [Long]; Stun Setting).
OR-Series Tech Recycling Station: Tech recycling stations are used to speed up the time-consuming task of sifting through damaged or discarded technology to find items of value.
Obi-Wan Kenobi's Lightsaber: Kenobi's lightsaber features a minimalist, skeletal design with a chromium hilt and emitter and a series of black composite metal ridges along the grip.
Ocular: An ocular is a circular lens that lets the user, on a specific setting, see through walls to pick up heat signatures from the outside. It looks similar in appearance to a slightly larger jewellers loupe. Oculars are often used by spacers checking their hulls for my nocks or other unwanted hangers on. When using an ocular, a character may see through one wall, they can see the vague shape of a heat signature but cannot determine specific species or specific people. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Omniscan 3 Integrated Scanner: Warriors must be aware of their surroundings at all times, ready to respond to any threat. Base Modifiers: While wearing their helmet, the user benefits from a general purpose scanner and adds a boost die to Perception checks made to detect movement or hidden enemies. Modification Options: 1 Skill (Perception +1) Mod.
Optical Camouflage System: Popular with both military scouts and assassins, optical camouflage systems are fragile but effective pieces of modern stealth technology.
Optimized Energy Cell: In a fire fight, there is no such thing as having too many shots left in your blaster. This attachment can applied to any energy weapon. Base Modifiers: When the GM would spend a despair result or a number of threat results to cause the weapon to run out of ammunition, it requires an additional threat to have that effect apply to this weapon. Modification Options: None.
Ordnance Impeller: Over the millennia, tinkerers, weapon designers, and desperate fighters have come up with countless delivery systems for explosives, from missile tubes to combat droids.
Ourlash Scrambling Charge: Originally developed during the final days of the Clone Wars, the Ourlash Corp. developed Scrambling Charge never made it to market during the conflict and instead fell into obscurity during the early days of the Empire. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Ouro Blade: Even for the eccentric designs of ancient Thisspias, the Ouro Blade is a strange weapon. After making a successful combat check with an Ouro Blade, the wielder may spend an advantage result to inflict an automatic hit on the target, doing the attack's base damage, and may do so multiple times.
Overcharge Valve: An overcharge valve is a simple (if dangerous) modification that allows the wielder of a blaster to discharge the entire ammunition reserve in a single, devastating shot.
Overcharged Actuating Module: While some weaponsmiths try to squeeze every last bit of energy form a power cell, others try to cram as much energy as they can into every shot.
Overcharged Power Cell: Those who know what they are doing can sometimes overcharge the power cell of a lightsaber to increase the power of the blade, but these changes make the power cell volatile and potentially unstable.
P-14 Hazardous Industry Suit: Hazardous industry suits allow technical experts and laborers alike to deal with workshop or factory conditions dangerous to most living species. A hazardous industry suit creates an environmental seal when the helmet is worn. and is good for up to eight hours of protection from hostile atmospheres. deadly radiation. or even direct exposure to the void. Finally, the suit's special chemical coating insulates the wearer from burns and shocks.
P-series Destroyer Droid: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Skills: Athletics 2, Cool 2, Coordination 2, Gunnery 2. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat or drink, and can survive in vacuum or underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Fire Sweep (as an action, the droid can make a Hard difficulty Gunnery check to inflict one hit that deals base damage with the blaster cannons on up to two targets within Medium range, plus one additional target per advantage result generated by the check; it can only hit each target once this way), Roller (once per round as a maneuver, the droid can coil up into a ball; in this state, the droid can make an additional Move maneuver each turn without spending strain and may exceed the two maneuver limit; as part of a Move maneuver, the droid can uncoil), Shield Projector (may activate or deactivate as a maneuver, or a...
P2F Hostile Environment Remote: Droids can perform in a wide array of functions for diplomats and negotiators across the galaxy, but sometimes, no matter how hostile the environment, only direct contact with a diplomat's counterparts will do.
PB08 Heavy Blaster Pistol: One of the more popular pistols on the market, Merr-Sonn's PB08 was designed as a direct competitor to BlasTech's DL-44.
PLX-1 Portable Missile Launcher: The PLX-1 was the missile launcher of choice used by the Grand Army of the Republic during the clone wars. Manufactured by Merr-Sonn Munitions, as was the case with most clone ordnance, the PLX-1 was barely considered portable. While ultimately as heavy as the RPS-6 also used by clone troopers, its bulk was excessive for a weapon only capable of holding two missiles in the tube. Users of the weapon called it the “Plex”, and was standard issue to clone troopers with dedicated anti-armour roles. In this role, it excelled at disabling vehicles and starships alike. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
PSF Mk III Power Fence: Power fences are specialized shield emitters designed to provide perimeter protection for civilian and military installations.
PTP Link: A PTP (or point-to-point) link is a multichannel personal communications device capable of transmitting audio signals up to twenty-five kilometers.
PX-7 Heat Sensor: The ChandrilTech PX-7 heat sensor used to detect anomalous variations in temperature nearby. A PX-7 heat sensor adds two boost dice to Perception checks to find heat-emitting objects within short range and adds a boost die to Mechanics checks to remove Critical Hits from starships and Critical Injuries from droids.
Padded Armor: Made of thick, reinforced, wear-resistant textiles woven with energy dispersion mesh, this light armor is one of the most common forms of personal armor protection in the galaxy.
Pain Damper: Pain dampers are a series of nerve implants that allow an individual to tune out extreme pain and fatigue. Pain dampers upgrade the ability of all checks made to resist pain or physical torture once.
Paired Weapons: Warriors have a long history of employing two weapons at one time in a variety of combat engagements. This attachment can only be applied to one-handed weapons. Base Modifiers: Must be applied to two weapons at the same time. Reduces advantage result required to hit with the secondary weapon when two-weapon fighting with this pair of weapons by 1. Modification Options: None.
Palandrix AO14 \"Aranea\" Net Gun: The AO14 "Aranea" is one of Palandrix Personal Protection Gear's more popular non-lethal weapon systems.
Panic Pistol: The Panic Pistol is Oriolanis Defense Systems' compact civilian self-defense slugthrower pistol. While at engaged range, a character wielding a Panic Pistol does not add the +1 difficulty modifier for ranged attacks in melee. However, the GM may spend two threat results or a despair result to damage the weapon one step.
Pannier Modular Cargo Handling System: Designed by Piccatech Ltd., the Pannier system is designed to fit as much cargo on a beast as it can carry in an efficient and secure manner.
Paradour Enterprises Signature Scent Syn: Aromas have a tremendous potential to influence a subject's reaction. Using a signature scent synthesizer prior to a discussion removes two setback dice from the first Charm, Coercion, or Negotiation check made during the encounter.
Parrying Dagger: Typically used in the off hand, the traditional parrying dagger consists of a slim, double-edged blade with a wide guard or a basket hilt for hand protection.
Parrying Vibroblade: The parrying vibroblade is a high-tech version of the parrying dagger.
Passive Foliage Suit: For those operating in the wilds, sometimes the simplest camouflage methods are the best. This attachment can be equipped on any armor that could logically integrate the necessary patches and tie-lines. Base Modifiers: Adds a setback die to any Perception or Vigilance checks made to detect a character in appropriate environments who is wearing this armor. Modification Options: None.
Performer's Attire: Many diplomats perform not only on the political stage, but on actual stages as well. A character wearing performer's attire adds a boost die to checks to perform or otherwise attract attention.
Personal Deflector Shield: Rare, expensive, and difficult to use, personal deflector shields are one of the few types of personal armor capable of withstanding sustained blaster fire.
Personal Stealth Field: The ultimate in personal stealth technology, personal stealth fields are rare and expensive, and well worth the price. Restrictions: Personalized stealth fields are vanishingly rare, and so few governments bother to place restrictions on their use. Some are legitimate artifacts of the past, while others are stolen military technology, and the particulars of their creation dictate their legality. Of course, law enforcement officers tend not to see these fine distinctions when apprehending people using them to commit crimes.
Personal Transponder: Personal transponders are low-powered tracking devices used to locate individuals. Picking up a personal transponder's signal accurately enough to find its exact location within close range on planetary scale requires an Average difficulty Computers check. Increase the difficulty of this check by two for each planetary scale range band beyond close, to a maximum of extreme range at the planetary scale.
Personal Vibrosaw: [S]ome troopers mount a personal vibrosaw on the bracer of their armor. Base Modifiers: The personal vibrosaw can quickly cut through most materials. Exact details are up to the GM's discretion, but a vibrosaw can typically cut a hole large enough to move through in common durasteel doors and walls within two to three rounds, with reinforced doors or titanium plating taking correspondingly longer. A personal vibrosaw can be used as a weapon with the following profile: (Melee; Damage +4; Critical 2; Range [Engaged]; Cumbersome 4, Pierce 2, Sunder, Unwieldy 4, Vicious 2). Modification Options: None.
Phase II ARC Trooper Armor: Advanced Recon Commandos, or ARC troopers, performed the initial field test of Phase II clone trooper armor. Phase II ARC trooper armor includes an integrated comlink with scrambler capable of using all standard Republic communications protocols. The survival pack incorporates emergency rations, a water filtration system, a microfiber line, and a distress beacon.
Phase II Clone Trooper Armor: Throughout the course of the Clone Wars, clone troopers have assumed a broad range of different battlefield roles. Phase II clone trooper armor includes an integrated comlink with scrambler capable of using all standard Republic communications protocols.
Phase I ARC Trooper Armor: The elite ARC troopers within the Grand Army of the Republic receive special gear, including a unique type of reinforced armor, to help them survive their dangerous assignments.
Phase I Clone Trooper Armor: Mass-produced alongside the first batches of clones by the Kaminoans, the first designs of clone armor emphasize function and protection.
Philaxian Phase-Knife: Developed by an obscure cadre of artificers, these blades were once popular on Philaxia before its discovery by the Republic.
Phoenix II Plasma Disruptor: The Phoenix II was manufactured by the Drever Corporation for breaching through all sorts of locked doors. Initially delivered to planetary customs throughout the galaxy, it was quickly bought up by commando units and then the Republic’s SO BDE. As a breaching tool, it was not particularly subtle but was reliable in getting the job done. Some commando units realised that the plasma blast would likely be effective at eliminating droids and improvised the tool as a weapon against them. This eventually led the Drever Corporation to roll out the DN Bolt Caster to fill this newfound niche. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Physician's Kit: Carried by doctors and healers on backwater worlds, physician's kits are bigger and more comprehensive than their medpac cousins.
Physiological Enhancement System: These advanced, custom-tailored systems provide medical diagnostics, ergonomic adjustments, and powered support systems tuned to the wearer's motor functions.
Pioneer Armor: Designed with the combat engineer in mind, pioneer armor consists of rugged, reinforced jacket and trousers, sturdy boots with plasteel toe caps, gloves, and a modified engineer's helmet.
Pioneer Squad Tool Kit: A pioneer squad tool kit is a collection of gear essential for land-clearing and building-construction efforts. The tools and equipment included in a pioneer squad tool kit adds two boost dice on checks made to clear land, build structures, set up obstacles, set up or disable explosives, or engage in other construction or engineering tasks in the field.
Pistol Grip: A pistol grip replaces the stock or other grip on a long gun with one like that of a pistol, allowing it to be fired one-handed.
Pistol Hilt: While the Jedi of old disdained blasters, modern Force users tend to need every advantage they can get. This attachment can only be applied to lightsabers. The user must spend a maneuver to switch from one weapon mode to the other (from lightsaber function to pistol function, for example). Base Modifiers: When the lightsaber is not ignited, the wielder may fire the pistol using the following profile: (Ranged [Light]; Damage 6; Critical 3; Range [Short]; Stun Damage). Modification Options: None.
Pit Crew Coveralls: Working as a mechanic in a pit crew, whether fixing lowly groundcars on a dusty Outer Rim backwater or maintaining elaborate podracers in a high-end racing circuit, is a dirty job.
Plasma Charge: Plasma charges emit high-temperature plasma shockwaves, though they don't approach the disintegrating heat of baradium. Plasma charges have the Burn 1 quality, and the quality increases by one for every additional charge. Additional Damage +6.
Plasma Grenade: Plasma grenades appropriate blaster technology to create a sphere of blaster energy.
Plasma Missile: When primed, a store of high-energy gas within the plasma warhead is converted into a highly volatile plasma state.
Plasma Protein Replicator: Replenishing an individual's vital bodily fluids from an external source can reduce the recovery time from particularly traumatic injuries.
Poison Dart: Effectively just a tranquilizer dart loaded with a more dangerous compound, the poison dart is a favorite among Imperial agents and bounty hunters going for a quiet kill. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Poison Gas Grenade: Poison gas grenades store a concentrated gas, most often dioxis, trion, or Fex-M3. Upon impact, the grenade uses several emitters to spray aerosolized neurotoxin, anesthetic, neuroparalytic, or any other poison at the GM's discretion. Poison gas grenades can be loaded with up to 1 dose of neurotoxin, anesthetic, or neuroparalytic or any other poison at the GM's deems suitable. Whenever the wielder successfully attacks a target with the weapon (whether or not [they] deal damage), the target also suffers the effects of being exposed to the poison. Unless a poison gas grenade is used outdoors in a windy environment, the poison gas lingers for two rounds, affecting anyone caught in the cloud. If the grenade is detonated in a closed, poorly ventilated space, such as a small room or narrow starship corridor, the gas lingers for five rounds instead.
Poison Reservoir: Many assassins who use melee weapons choose to improve the lethality of their blades by adding a poison reservoir that releases a toxin through a channel in the blade at the push of a button in the hilt.
Poisoner's Ring: Produced by a wide variety of artisans, poisoners' rings and similar accessories look like the sort of elegant jewelry commonly worn by aristocrats and wealthy folk. Models include: Beladonab concealment jewelry, countless custom models.
Polis Massan Bodysuit: The Polis Massans, also known as Kallidahin, employ unique, tight-fitting bodysuits in their day-to-day lives. [...] All Polis Massan bodysuits include a tiny head-mounted multidirectional glow rod as well as an integrated utility belt (which increases the wearer's encumbrance threshold by one). Though designed with Polis Massan physiology in mind, bodysuits for use by other species have been commissioned.
Polis Massan Voicebox: Polis Massans, being unable to speak, have come to rely on artificial voiceboxes, similar in most respects to droid vocabulators, to communicate with other beings.
Pommel Cap: The pommel cap is a small, weighted knob or similar piece attached to the base of a lightsaber's hilt. This attachment can only be applied to lightsabers or any Melee weapon with a hilt. Base Modifiers: When attached to a lightsaber hilt, this weapon gains the Stun 2 item quality. In addition, a single small item with an encumbrance value of zero may be stored in the pommel cap, with Game Master approval. Modification Options: 2 Item Quality (Stun +1) Mods.
Portable Comlink Relay: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! A small comlink relay that can automatically intercept and relay a high volume of transceiver or comlink messages to other comlinks, transceivers or relay stations within Close range.
Portable Electric Perimeter Fence: Made by a number of manufacturers throughout the galaxy, portable electric perimeter fences use beams of coherent energy to make a protective barrier around encampments, outposts, or any other sensitive areas that require extra security.
Portable Plasma Shield: A small defensive shield can provide a substantial upgrade. Base Modifiers: The small plasma-shield generator attached to the armor's forearm armor and can be activated or deactivated as an incidental. When the shield is active, the user cannot wield a two-handed weapon. The plasma shield cannot be used to attack but grants the Defensive 1 item quality. Modification Options: 2 Item Quality (Defensive +1) Mods, 3 Item Quality (Deflection +1) Mods.
Portable Plasma Shield (weapon): Heavy infantry was fairly vulnerable due to the weight of their weapons and a need to stand out in the open to get the best use out of the spray. Using similar technology as found in energy bucklers or the armour bracer mounted plasma shield, blaster companies and aftermarket modification providers have designed emitters capable of being mounted on a weapon. Due to its distance from the user, it isn’t as effective as a held shield and its positioning prevents the user from aiming as effectively while maintaining the defensive matrix. While the base model is typically only a small torso-sized shield, it can be extended to protect legs and widen it a little. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Portable Power Generator: For most armies, power is just as important as ammunition, food, and bandages. A portable generators can recharge a depleted piece of equipment as a maneuver, or a droid or larger piece of machinery in an hour. Additionally, a portable power generator can allow an engaged character to ignore an "out of ammo" despair result in the same manner as an extra reload; the generator is usually not depleted by such use, except at the GM's discretion.
Portable Shelter: Portable shelters come in a variety of styles and sizes.
Porvir: Porvir are tiny, awkward creatures. Skills (group only): Perception. Talents: None. Abilities: Unthreatening Aura (when declaring an attack against a porvir within short range, the attacker must suffer 3 strain or choose a different action or target), Silhouette 0. Equipment: Teeth and claws (Brawl; Damage 1; Critical 4; Range [Engaged]).
Powered Capacitive Armor: Popular and lightweight, capacitive armor offers moderate protection against most forms of attack whenever worn. Models include: A/KT Tuff-Powered Armor
Powered Entrenching Tool: Essentially a small folding shovel at the end of a short plasteel haft containing a powerful pistoning mechanism, this tool can be used to forcefully dig holes and trenches as well as level areas for construction.
Pretormin Environmental WPR-60: Nearly every known species depends upon a steady supply of water free from contaminants for basic survival. A water purifier allows the user to purify enough water for one person to drink per day.
Pretormin Plastent: Alliance standard issue, this triangular two-meter tent inflates and anchors in minutes, and sleeps up to four. Sleeping in a plastent removes two setback dice from the user's checks to resist hostile temperatures while sleeping, and downgrades the difficulty of any such checks once.
Prosthetic Replacement: For those seeking high-quality, unobtrusive replacements for lost limbs, prosthetic replacements are among the more popular options.
Protocol Droid: When searching the most remote regions in the galaxy, Imperial authorities often make use of probe droids to scout out remote planets and scour distant locations for signs of whatever, or whomever, is being sought.
Proton Grenade: Often used for demolitions work, proton grenades are powerful explosives based on proton torpedo warhead technology. In an explosive device using proton grenades, the second grenade adds +20 damage, the third adds +30, the fourth adds +40, and the fifth adds +50, for a total of 150 damage for five grenades. After the fifth grenade, additional grenades do not add additional damage, but they do increase the blast radius by one range band per additional grenade.
Proximity Detonator: Although certain models of mines include proximity detonators as standard, some individuals desire this feature for grenades and other explosive devices.
Pulse Cannon: The typical pulse cannon features a fixed buttstock and dramatically elongated barrel. As a maneuver, a character wielding a pulse cannon may prime the weapon to expend all of its ammunition in a single, devastating shot. The next time the weapon is fired that encounter, it counts as having the Breach 1 and Vicious 3 qualities. After being discharged this way, the weapon runs out of ammunition.
Punch Dagger: A punch dagger, also known as a push dagger or gimlet knife, is a small, short-bladed knife attached to a T-shaped handle, or "gimlet."
Q-22 Retinal Tracker: Consisting of microsensors implanted directly behind the user's retina and a tiny tracking computer installed at the base of the skull, a retinal tracker aids the user in targeting imminent threats.
Q-4 Borer Droid: Manufactured by Quarren Industrial on the planet Mon Cala, the Q-4 borer is a diminutive cylindrical mining droid .7 meters in length and .2 meters in diameter.
Q-Signal Messenger Drone: The Q-Signal Messenger Drone from Incom is unlike anything on the market today. Skills: Astrogation 1, Perception 2, Piloting (Space) 2, Stealth 2. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: Messenger Drone Vehicle. Hull Type/Class: Drone/Q-Signal. Manufacturer: Incom Corporation. Hyperdrive: Primary: Class 2, Backup: None. Navicomputer: None—implanted Q-Signal Drone. Sensor Range: None. Ship's Complement: None. Encumbrance Capacity: 10. Passenger Capacity: 0. Consumables: None. Price/Rarity: 12,000 credits/5. Customization Hard Points: 1. Weapons: None. Stealthy: The difficulty of checks made to detect this vessel is upgraded twice. If the vessel did not move in the previous round, also add two setback dice to the check.
Q-series Destroyer Droid: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Skills: Athletics 2, Cool 3, Coordination 3, Gunnery 3, Perception 1. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat or drink, and can survive in vacuum or underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Fire Sweep (as an action, the droid can make a Hard difficulty Gunnery check to inflict one hit that deals base damage with the blaster cannons on up to two targets within Medium range, plus one additional target per advantage result generated by the check; it can only hit each target once this way), Roller (once per round as a maneuver, the droid can coil up into a ball; in this state, the droid can make an additional Move maneuver each turn without spending strain and may exceed the two maneuver limit; as part of a Move maneuver, the droid can uncoil), Shield Projector (may activate or deactivate as a...
Q7-series Astromech Droid: The Q7 was the result of a joint venture between Industrial Automaton and Kuat Systems Engineering. Skills: Astrogation 3, Computers 3, Cool 2, Mechanics 2, Piloting (Space) 2. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink, and can survive indefinitely underwater or in vacuum. Immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: On-board repair and diagnostic suite (counts as tool kit). The majority of Q7 astromech droids have long since been reprogrammed to work with other ships and live their lives in more common astromech droid bodies. [...] Q7s with their original programming gain the following bonuses only when linked to an A3 Nimbus, but not when connected to any other ship. Gearhead 1 (remove a setback die from all Mechanics checks, and the credit cost to add mods to attachments decreases by 50%).
Qi'ra's Monlitzer S-195 Blaster Pistol: While she prefers to rely on her hard-won unarmed combat skills, Qi'ra carries a customized Molitzer S-195 double-barreled blaster pistol, a lightweight sporting blaster used by nobles for protection during ceremonial Chandrilan drag hunts.
Quadnoculars: Quadnoculars use rugged imaging systems to magnify far-off objects for their users. This is a beginner game version of this item and thus its rules are likely simplified for ease-of-use.
Quarterstaff: A simple but sturdy weapon most often found among the primitive or poor, a quarterstaff is nonetheless effective and draws little attention compared to more advanced weapons. They might be made of simple wood, salvaged metal, or something of significance to the owner, but should not be underestimated in the hands of a skilled wielder however humble the appearance. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Questioner-9 Interrogation Serum: Interrogation serums are a favored product of the Empire. [...] Interrogators typically inject the serum into a restrained or helpless target, but Questioner-9 can also operate through ingestion by an unsuspecting diner. The serum takes effect in the victim's system after a delay of about five minutes. At this point, the victim upgrades the difficulty of all checks to withhold information or resist manipulation (including resisting Force powers such as Influence) once for the next 24 hours.
Quick Shed: Though putting armor on remains a chore, taking it off—especially in an emergency situation—is expedited with the quick shed attachment.
Quicksilver Baton: The quicksilver baton is compacted within a containment field. When the baton was activated, it extends to full length, boasting spearpoints at either end. The baton’s durable cylinder is made from a collapsible micromesh matrix held in a containment field. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Quillbomb: Thisspiasians once viewed the giant ystervark as both worthy foe and revered delicacy.
R-88 \"Suppressor\" Riot Rifle: A design dating from before the Clone Wars, Merr-Sonn's R-88 "Suppressor" riot rifle is law enforcement's most-used riot weapon throughout the galaxy.
R7-series Astromech Droid: The R7-series is the latest member of Industrial Automaton's highly respected R-series family of astromech droids. Skills: Astrogation 3, Computers 3, Cool 2, Mechanics 3, Piloting (Space) 2. Talents: Galaxy Mapper 1 (remove a setback die from all Astrogation checks, and Astrogation checks take 50% less time), Hold Together (spend one Destiny Point to make a Hold Together incidental immediately after the droid's starship takes damage. The droid's controlling character explains why the apparently serious damage was in fact superficial, and the damage from the attack becomes system strain instead). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink, and can survive indefinitely underwater or in vacuum. Immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: Arc Welder (Melee; Damage 3; Critical –; Range [Engaged]; Stun Damage), built-in repair and diagnostic suite (counts as tool kit).
R82 Jump Boots: Aratech's R82 jump boots are commonly used for sports and leisure activities, but many intrepid explorers and scouts have also found them useful for exploring less accessible areas on newly located worlds.
RA-7 Protocol Droid: The RA-7 series is the Galactic Empire's go-to choice for protocol and espionage droids. Skills: Deception 2, Knowledge (Education) 3, Knowledge (Xenology) 3, Negotiation 3, Perception 4, Skulduggery 3, Stealth 2, Vigilance 3. Talents: Adversary 1 (upgrade the difficulty of all combat checks against this target once), Plausible Deniability 2 (remove two setback dice from Coercion and Deception checks). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Etiquette and Protocol (protocol droids allow allies to add a boost die to all Negotiation checks and other checks made to negotiate or mediate). Equipment: None.
RGL-318 Rotary Grenade Launcher: Grenade launchers are some of the most versatile weapons available on the market. The weapon's listed price does not include ammunition. Grenades for the RGL-318 cost the same as equivalent hand grenades, but many dealers offer a 50-percent discount on cases of six. Any type of grenade can be chambered by the RGL-318, but the grenades must be specifically made to be fired from the grenade launcher. Launcher grenades cannot be thrown. The listed profile represents an RGL-318 loaded with standard frag grenades.
RH-Series Research Droid: RH-series research droids are a small-run series of science droids produced by Cybot Galactica. Skills: Computers 2, Knowledge (Core Worlds) 2, Knowledge (Education) 3, Knowledge (Lore) 2, Knowledge (Xenology) 3. Talents: Natural Scholar (once per session, may reroll any one Knowledge check). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins). Equipment: Built-in science database (adds an automatic advantage result to Knowledge checks and adds two automatic advantage results when assisting with a Knowledge check in addition to the standard assistance benefits..
RLR-331 \"Bulldog\" Rocket Rifle: A relic of the Clone Wars, the RLR-331 "Bulldog" is a rocket-propelled grenade launcher popular with the mercenaries to whom Kelvarek Consolidated Arms caters.
RM-series Military Intelligence Droid: The RM-series is a powerful, highly competent espionage droid from the powerful security firm Meren-Data. Skills: Computers 3, Cool 2, Deception 3, Knowledge (Warfare) 4, Mechanics 4, Perception 4, Ranged (Heavy) 4, Skulduggery 3, Stealth 3, Vigilance 2. Talents: Adversary 2 (upgrade the difficulty of all combat checks against this target twice). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Improvised Explosive Device (once per session, can make an Average difficulty Mechanics check to build an explosive device out of available materials that deals 11 damage plus 1 for every success result on the Mechanics check; can spend a triumph result to increase damage by 2; the GM can spend a despair result to have the explosive device detonate immediately in the user's face).
RMD-20 \"Eye in the Sky\": Designed for the civilian market, the RMD-20 "Eye in the Sky" is a small, spherical remote intended to record footage while staying on the move.
RPC-2 Rocket Launcher: The RPC-2 rocket launcher was a lightweight and downscaled version of the RPS-6 rocket launcher by Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc.. Designed with space for a single rocket, this launcher was carried on the AT-RT used by the ARF troopers of the GAR. The weapon was a simple anti-armour solution and was used extensively in ambushes, taking the opportunity of weak sides and rears to disable CIS armour columns. In so far as it was a lightweight anti-vehicle weapon produced cheaply, the RPC-2 is the GAR’s equivalent to the E-60R. However, it doesn’t suffer as many setbacks in its package and has a quality build for what it seeks to achieve. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
RPS-6 Rocket Launcher: Built by Sienar, the RPS-6 is the GAR's primary infantry-portable shoulder-fired missile launcher. If attacking a target of silhouette 2 or greater, an RPS-6 adds a boost die if the user performs an aim maneuver.
RS01 \"Ripper\" Powersword: Powerswords are large, heavy vibroswords equipped with high-output vibro field generators that allow them to cleave through most materials.
Radiation Shielding: The galaxy contains many perils, and one of the most common is radiation. This attachment can only be applied to armor with hard plates that can be fully sealed, at the GM's discretion. This attachment can only be applied to laminate armor, heavy battle armor, and any other armor that can be fully sealed. Base Modifiers: Adds two boost dice to Resilience checks to resist radiation. Modification Options: 2 Increase armor's soak by +1 against damage caused by radiation Mods.
Rapid-Recharge XCiter: Specially modified gas conversion enablers (XCiters) can allow a blaster pistol or rifle to achieve much higher rates of fire than factory standard, even rivaling those of a true repeating blaster.
Raquor Venom: Extracted from the glandular toxin sacs of Sriluurian dark wolves, raquor venom is a powerful paralytic enzyme inhibitor.
Reactive Plating: Reactive plating is a modification that attaches small explosive panels to the armor's exterior plates. This attachment can only be applied to armor with a soak of 2 or higher. Base Modifiers: Increase the armor's encumbrance by 4. The first time the wearer of this armor is hit by an attack each encounter, reduce the damage [they] suffer by 10; the armor suffers minor damage and loses this benefit until fully repaired. Modification Options: 3 Reduce the armor's encumbrance by 1 Mods.
Rebel Heavy Battle Armor: Most Alliance field tactics focus on hit-and-fade strikes, sabotage, and denial operations, but sometimes the Rebellion has no choice but to stand and fight against Imperial forces.
Reciprocating Quad Blaster Cannon: What is essentially a downscaled version of an anti-starfighter emplacement, the reciprocating quad blaster cannon is the heaviest personal weapon in Merr-Sonn Munitions' entire arsenal.
Redirection Crystal: Redirection crystals are an invention of the Imperial Security Bureau.
Reek: Although reeks are naturally herbivorous and non-aggressive, their fearsome appearance as well as great size and strength have seen them used for combat and blood sport in arenas throughout the galaxy.
Refined Cortosis Gauntlets: Cortosis gauntlets are simply metal or composite gloves with thick forearm plates laced with cortosis ore. When a character armed with a lightsaber makes a combat check targeting an opponent armed with refined cortosis gauntlets, three threat results or a despair result may be spent to cause a lightsaber to short out and deactivate after the combat check has been resolved. When a character armed with refined cortosis gauntlets makes a combat check targeting a character armed with a lightsaber, the attacking character may spend three advantage results or a triumph result to cause the lightsaber to short out and deactivate after the combat check has been resolved. The lightsaber may be reactivated as an incidental, but it may not be reactivated until after the last Initiative slot during the next round.
Reflec Adaptive Skin: Simple and effective, reflec adaptive skin is an advanced polymer that possesses adaptive light-warping and sensor-dampening properties.
Reflec Shadowskin: An advanced, light-absorbing polymer that can be applied to armor, reflec shadowskin coating can enhance the stealth abilities of its wearer; anyone wearing armor covered in this substance can easily blend into the shadows.
Reflect Body Glove: A reflect body glove is a skintight suit that can be worn underneath traditional garments. Add two setback dice to a character's Vigilance and Perception checks to notice the user is wearing armor. After a successful combat check has been resolved against the wearer, the reflect body glove's soak is reduced by one, to a minimum of zero. The suit's soak may be restored to its original value by making an Average difficulty Mechanics check.
Reflective Pads: A high in the sky idea by many sharpshooters who use blasters, these pads have been released by a number of ‘market edge’ blaster companies. Largely a laughing stock, these small corporate entities swear by these reflective pads and assure buyers that they perfectly and fully reflect a blaster bolt with its full power. The issue is that the degree of skill needed to use them simply makes them appear as useless. With enough reflex and cunning, however, a skilled sniper can put these to good use for firing around cover. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Reflex Grip: Through a delicate system of motion sensors and miniaturized motors built into a lightsaber hilt, a craftsperson can alter the lightsaber to respond to adjustments in grip and position with greater responsiveness.
Reinforced Environment Gear: Produced by a number of companies, such a Pretormin Environmental and Gandorthral Atmospherics, reinforced environment gear is essentially improved adverse environment gear.
Reinforced Gauntlets: Additional armor plating on the hands is a valuable upgrade for those who favor heavier armor, as well as for those who do welding jobs on starships and desire to keep all of their fingers.
Reinforced Magnetic Shielding: A delicately tuned magnetic field maintains the coherency of a lightsaber's plasma beam. Base Modifiers: The lightsaber loses the Breach quality and gains the Concussive 1, Knockdown, and Pierce 1 qualities. Modification Options: 1 Item Quality (Concussive +1) Mod, 2 Item Quality (Disorient +1) Mods, 1 Item Quality (Pierce +1) Mod.
Remote DVI Activator (Long Range): Useful for those needing to make a speedy getaway, a remote activation controller (also known as a beckon call) is a small, handheld device that can be worn on the wrist or stowed in a pocket.
Removed Safety Features: By removing or deactivating weapon safeties and other features, a gun can more easily be drawn and readied for combat. This attachment can be applied to any Ranged (Light) or Ranged (Heavy) weapon that could logically have such elements to remove. Base Modifiers: Adds a boost die to the first combat check made with the weapon during an encounter. The Game Master may spend two threat results or a despair result from any combat check to have the weapon misfire, running out of ammunition and inflicting 4 strain on the wielder. Modification Options: None.
Republic Insignia: Clone troopers, Navy officers, and even Jedi who have served with distinction in the various theaters of the Clone Wars may be nominated for awards to recognize their valor and service.
Republic Seal of Service: Republic Seal of Service refers to a number of similar awards granted to those who have proven themselves to be exemplars of the Republic's ideals and paragons of service and duty.
Repulsor-Assisted Lifting: Although more common among factory or warehouse labor crews than in battle, repulsor-assist systems have gained some popularity among bounty hunters who need to haul in heavy catches.
Repulsor Assist Unit: Some models of grappling hook and climbing harness rely on forces other than sheer muscle power for operation. A character using a repulsor assist unit reduces the difficulty to climb by 2 (to a minimum of 0). A repulsor assist unit can also be attached to a cargo to reduce the encumbrance value of the cargo by 7.
Repulsor Clamp: This pneumatic clamp, attached to a repulsor engine, allows Technicians to hold heavy objects in the air as they work on repairs.
Repulsor Fist: Also called a repulse–hand or a enforcer grip, this durasteel weapon cybernetic is typically neither covered in synthskin nor intendeded to appear natural.
Repulsor Gun: Little more than toys for bored technicians, repulsor guns are makeshift devices crafted from a small repulsor engine, a charge capacitor, and a trigger assembly.
Repulsor Pack: While lacking the range and power of a true jetpack, a repulsor pack provides a reliable—and less terrifying—alternative for those new to personal flight devices.
Repulsor Throwing Razor: A peculiar and deadly weapon originating in the gladiatorial arenas of Rodia, the repulsor throwing razor combines ancient and modern technology.
