This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Trailbreaker Poleaxe: LaserHone's Trailbreak is a versatile piece of equipment designed for exploration, search and rescue, and wilderness survival.
Training Lightsaber: Ostensibly non-lethal variants of the standard lightsaber, training lightsabers are low-powered lightsabers used to train Jedi youngling ins lightsaber combat.
Training Lightsaber Emitter: In the days of the Republic, the Jedi allowed their younglings to train with "training sabers," non-lethal versions of their famous energy blades.
Training Stick: The Jedi have used lightsabers for so long that most forget there may have been a time when they had no access to energy weapons.
Training Suit: The typical Jedi training suit features bulky, heavily padded chest, leg, and arm pieces designed to absorb the stunning blows of training lightsabers.
Training Weights: Many Warriors wear weighted bracers or anklets when training. A character wearing one or more training weights adds a setback die to all Brawn- and Agility-based checks, and to all combat checks. If a character wears training weights throughout a game session, increase the amount of XP the character receives for the session by 1. At the GM's discretion, a character wearing training weights may not receive this additional XP if they did not make any checks affected by the weights during the session.
TranLang Holo V Audio/Visual Translator: The Holo V A/V Translator is a small, handheld translating device produced by TranLang. The Holo V grants the scholar or translator a boost die to any Lore or Intellect checks made to decipher or translate an unknown language.
Tripod Mount: Used in static weapon emplacements to augment heavy squad support weapons such as the Imperial Army's E-Web heavy repeating blaster, tripods use a number of different technologies—from self-extending hydraulics to tiny, low-output repulsorlift generators—to stabilize heavy weapons for firing.
Truncheon: Made from a number of organic and inorganic materials such as metal, wood, and bone, truncheons are simple blunt weapons used by law enforcement agencies across the galaxy.
Tuskbeast Pike: Long spears used by the Nikto, tuskbeast pikes were originally developed to hunt the giant mutated predators of Kintan. The tuskbeast pike requires two hands to wield.
Type III \"Berethron\" Personal Modular: Manufactured in the Corellian sector for the Corellian Defense Force and CorSec forces, the Type III "Berethron" personal modular armor system is a relatively lightweight form of armored clothing that allows for considerable mission-specific customization.
Under-barrel Flame Projector: Uncommon but highly effective, under-barrel flame projectors are designed to give infantry an extra punch at close range.
Under-barrel Grapnel Launcher: Often seen among mercenary and special operations forces, under-barrel grapnel launchers provide a quick way to scale sheer surfaces in the midst of a combat situation or during a covert infiltration.
Under-barrel Grenade Launcher: Under-barrel grenade launchers are designed to give single soldiers or squads heavy anti-infantry or light anti-vehicle capabilities in a small package.
Under-barrel Micro-Rocket Rack: Since their inception, micro-rockets have proven to be versatile weapon systems. They are a favorite of many bounty hunters, who tend to find themselves in unexpected situations in which a variety of munitions comes in handy.
Underslung Scattergun: An ungainly attachment, an underslung scattergun is a single-shot slugthrower that can be attached to another weapon. This attachment can only applied to pistol-sized [Ranged (Light)] and rifle-sized [Ranged (Heavy)] weapon. Base Modifiers: Enables weapon to fire using the following profile at the user's choice: (Ranged [Heavy]; Damage 6; Critical 5; Range [Short]; Blast 5, Knockdown, Limited Ammo 1). Adds Cumbersome (+1) to weapon. Modification Options: None. While Fly Casual: A Sourcebook for Smugglers lacks a Critical Rating, the Errata (Page 4) corrects this to 5.
Unstable Kyber Crystal: There are many opportunities between mining and use for a crystal to become unstable. Base Modifiers: Installing this crystal changes a lightsaber's base damage to 6, its critical rating to 2, and the lightsaber gains the Breach 1 and Sunder item qualities. After installing this crystal, a character may spend a single maneuver communing with the crystal and attempting to harness its power. If they do, they increase their current Force rating by one. This increase persists until they use the extra Force point or Force die to fuel a Force talent or Force power. However, until the end of the encounter, the Game Master may spend four threat results or a despair result generated by the character's checks to have the fault cause the lightsaber to sputter and fail. In this case, the lightsaber deactivates and may not be ignited for one hour.
Urban Compass: While constructed around common datapads, urban compasses are more specialized tools. Individuals using an urban compass to find their way around a city remove two setback dice from checks due to unfamiliarity with the city, traffic, or other avoidable travel hazards. Additionally, an urban compass can turn up information on local establishments and customs, granting a boost die to checks to find information about the city's operation, various landmarks of note, and businesses of all types.
Utapaun Monk Cudgel: Ceremonial weapons from the remote Outer Rim world of Utapau, the Utapaun Monk Cudgel is a half metre long, spiked weapon of pure bronzium. Half of the weapon consists of a handle, which has a ribbed portion at the top and series of inset opalescent squares lower down. Each square represents an era of Utapaun history. The rest of the weapon is the solid barrel of the cudgel. Two rows of spikes line this portion of the weapon, and culminate in a pyramidal head. While replicas of these weapons are common throughout the galaxy, original cudgels from the eighth archaic settlement are rare and expensive treasures indeed. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Utility Arm: For some species, it's a mystery how humans and their ilk get by with only two arms. Base Modifiers: Provides the character with an additional mechanical arm that can function as one of the character's normal limbs, although it does not provide the character with additional actions or maneuvers. The utility arm adds a boost die to Mechanics checks and may modify other checks at the GM's discretion. Modification Options: 1 Add a a boost die to Mechanics checks Mod, 1 Spend 1 strain to gain an additional free maneuver per turn (character still may not perform more than two maneuvers) per turn Mod.
Utility Belt: Utility belts let travelers keep valuable tools and items close at hand. Utility belts increase a character's encumbrance threshold by 1.
Uxibeast: Shaggy uxibeasts have been used as beasts of burden on planets across the galaxy for untold years. Skills: Athletics 1, Brawl 1, Resilience 2. Talents: None. Abilities: Beast of Burden 15 (uxibeasts add 15 to their encumbrance threshold), Domesticable 2 (downgrade the difficulty of checks to train uxibeasts twice), Silhouette 3. Equipment: Trample (Brawl; Damage 10; Critical 5; Range [Engaged]; Disorient 2, Inaccurate 1).
V3-28 Jump Bug Mobile Power Unit: Derelict starships are full of sealed bulkheads, unpowered hatches, and deactivated ship systems. Small power mobile generators and energy cells allow salvagers a way around such problems. With variable power output settings, and a number of electrical contact connectors, jump bugs allow options for connecting to individual components of a starship. Once a technician has disconnected the power leads from the ship's inactive power drive, she can connect the jump bug to the leads, set the jump bug's power settings, and reactivate the component. Usually these are small systems including a ship's computer, door servos, a turbolift, or other similar small systems. Being small-output units, jump bugs are unable to power up a ship's main drive, artificial gravity, shields, or major weapon systems. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
VES-700 Pulse Rifle: Often used by urban riot police and bounty hunters, the Corondexx VES-700 pulse rifle has also found a place aboard many of the light freighters roaming the galaxy.
VX \"Sidewinder\" Repeating Blaster: Unlike other repeating blasters, the SoroSuub VX achieves its high rate of fire through the use of five rotating barrels.
VX Hands-Free Weapon System: The Merr-Sonn VX Hands-Free Weapon System allows the modification of armor to mount one or more weapons, so a spacer can keep their hands free for whatever unexpected needs might arise in combat.
VX–A Intelligent Toolbox: Produced by Merr-Sonn, this rigid backpack contains a suite of tools and portable supplies. An intelligent toolbox counts as a tool kit. Additionally, it reduces the time required for repairs, crafting, and other Mechanics-related tasks by 50%. The MX-M counts as a tool kit. Additionally, it reduces the time required for repairs, crafting, and other Mechanics-related tasks by 5.
Vac Attack Mk-127 Concealable Disc Launc: Initially designed by the VacuTech Combine—which ran out of capital and declared bankruptcy during the development of this weapon thanks to Imperial meddling—the Vac Attack is a small projectile launcher worn around the wrist like a chrono.
Vacuum Sealed: While making one's armor void-compatible is hardly standard, it can be extremely useful. This attachment can only be applied to laminate armor, battle armor, (and armor that explicitly states its compatibility with this attachment). Base Modifiers: Allows the user to ignore the effects of vacuum or poisonous atmospheric environments for up to 10 minutes. Modification Options: None.
Varactyl: The Utai species of Utapau has long used varactyls as mounts, favoring the reptavian creatures for their incredible speed, as well as for their ability to defeat osbtacles by climbing using their clinging feet or even gliding over them using their feathered limbs.
Variable Holdout Blaster: A unique, locally produced holdout blaster manufactured on Ord Gimmel, the variable holdout blaster can switch between seven different power levels, potentially putting it on par with military holdout blasters.
Varpeline Crystal: Varpeline crystals are hard, super-dense minerals found on the Outer Rim world of Mearalis II. Base Modifiers: Installing a varpeline crystal changes a lightsaber's base damage to 8 and its critical rating to 3. In addition, the lightsaber gains the Breach 1, Vicious 1, and Sunder item qualities. When spending a triumph result to inflict a Critical Injury, the character may spend an additional triumph result. If they do so, they may automatically inflict the Maimed Critical Injury on their target instead of the Critical Injury they would otherwise have inflicted. Modification Options: 1 Damage +1 Mod, 2 Item Quality (Vicious +1) Mods.
Vergence Starchart: Most starcharts showing the location of legendary vergences are clever (or not so clever) fakes designed to part foolish treasure hunters from their credits.
Veridicator 200: Designed and distributed by Saldalith Manufacturing, the Veridicator is billed as a personal lie-detection device. [...] Only one target can be scanned and monitored at a time, and they must be relatively nearby. Both the user and the target must remain relatively still, though small movements are unlikely to affect the device's readings. A character wearing a Veridicator 200 adds two boost dice to checks to discern whether another character is lying or omitting significant information and adds two boost dice to checks to ascertain another character's mental state.
Verpine Bond Gauntlets: Bond gauntlets are a Verpine invention designed to strengthen and reinforce an individual's forearms, while providing stabilization to hand and arm movements.
Verpine Comlink: The mode of short-wave telecommunication used by the Verpine is undetectable outside their species. With such a comlink, Verpin communications can be intercepted, and direct communication with the Verpine can be established within a range of 100 kilometers. [...] Verpine [may] choose different "channels," which the comlink can be tuned to, allowing for discreet communications even in the midst of the largest Verpine colonies.
Verpine Droid Disintegrator: A more advanced and highly destructive upgrade to the Verpine’s droid disruptor, the disintegrator overloads and melts a majority of a droid’s interior components. The resulting damage is in most cases irreparable. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Verpine Fiber Ultramesh Armor: Fiber ultramesh armor is the standard armor of Verpine colony guards. It is a more advanced version of the Verpine fiber mesh armor, with a tighter weave and sturdier construction.
Verpine Headband: When worn, a Verpine headband administers small electric shocks to the sleep centers of the wearer's brain in an attempt to keep the wearer awake for extended periods of time.
Verpine Heavy Shatter Rifle: The heavy shatter rifle is another variant of the shatter weaponry created by the Verpine. While a character wields a shatter gun, any combat checks they make that generates two threat results or a despair result damages the weapon by one step.
Verpine Shatter Pistol: The shatter pistol is another configuration of shatter weapon and is designed to be light and easy to wield. While a character wields a shatter gun, any combat checks they make that generates two threat results or a despair result damages the weapon by one step.
Verpine Shatter Rifle: Created by the insectoid Verpine, shatter guns use magnetic fields to propel small metal slugs at high speeds unattainable by typical slugthrowers.
Vessel Implant: Vessel implants are small, subdermal data storage implants that couriers and sleeper agents often use. Finding a vessel implant on a character requires a Daunting difficulty Perception check.
Vibro-Pickaxe: Vibro-pickaxes are heavy-duty tools used for excavation and demolition. A vibro-pickaxe requires two hands to use and adds an automatic boost die to checks involving breaking rocks, ground clearing, and similar activities.
Vibro- star: A lethal throwing weapon, vibro-stars operate under the same principle as vibroswords. A small but powerful device within the weapon sending out high-frequency sonic pulses that cause anything attached to the device to be sent into an extremely quick vibration. This vibration turns even a glancing blow into a gaping would, Because of this, vibro-stars are incredibly sharp and able to cut effortlessly through many materials, including bone and metal. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Vibro-ax: The average vibro-ax consists of a broad-bladed ax head mounted to a sturdy pole between one and two meters long. Vibro-axes require two hands to wield.
Vibro-bayonet: Although most fights are resolved with exchanges of blaster fire, a close-range fire fight can quickly turn into a melee.
Vibro-machete: Produced by manufacturers of vibro tools and weapons such as LaserHone and Merr-Sonn, the vibro-machete is one of the most common vibro-augmented blades on the galactic market.
Vibroknife: Little more than combat knives with onboard ultrasonic vibration field generators, vibroknives have evolved from individually crafted custom weapons to mass-produced commodities.
Vibroknucklers: As intimidating as they are lethal, vibroknucklers are worn like brass knuckles.. Add a setback die to any attempts to locate vibroknucklers concealed on an individual's body. Vibroknucklers cannot deal damage to a target's strain threshold.
Vibrorang: Seen in some of the more remote areas of the galaxy, especially in rougher and more aggressive resistance movements, vibrorangs are throwing weapons containing ultrasonic vibroblade technology.
Vibrorapier: A long, light, slender-bladed vibrosword, a vibrorapier is extremely well-balanced and suited for professional artists of the blade.
Vibrosword: While most cultures have moved beyond the use of bladed weapons for general use and protection, there are still some for whom intricate swordplay and the appreciation of a finely crafted blade are ingrained parts of their martial heritage.
Vid–Vox Scrambler: Vid-vox scramblers are small personal signal-jamming generators prized by intelligence agents, corporate officers, military brass, crime bosses, and anyone else interested in keeping their private conversations private.
Viper Probe Droid: The interrogation droids designed by the Imperial Department of Military Research are among the most feared weapons in the Emperor's vast arsenal.
Vision Cubes: Generally a pair of six-sided dice, Vision Cubes are not all that different from Chance Cubes. A character can use a vision cube while performing a check (including a Force power check) to divine the future. If the check results in having at least one uncancelled advantage result, the character adds a success result and Light point to the results as well. If the check results in having at least one uncancelled threat result, the character instead adds a failure result and Dark point to the results. If the check has no threat or advantage results, the vision cubes do nothing—except potentially look impressive.
Vocal Emulator Implant: Also known as a voxscrambler, a vocal emulator implant connects to an individual's vocal cords and allows them to change the tone and tenor of their voice and even impersonate specific individuals.
Vodran Hunting Rifle: These rifles, usually crafted by hand in local Vodran workshops, are commonly found in the hands of skilled dianoga hunters (or occasionally in the stomachs of dianogas).
Voss Warspear: A spear is essentially a blade at the end of a long pole, typically about two meters in length. The Voss warspear is a one-handed weapon, however it cannot be wielded with other weapons. It may, at the GM's discretion, be wielded while using a shield. A spear may be thrown as a ranged weapon. If used in this manner, change the skill to Ranged (Light) and the range of the weapon to short. The weapon also gains the Limited Ammo 1 quality (but if retrieved, it can be used again).
Vrblther: Vrblthers are aggressive predators found across the moon of Nar Shaddaa, although they are believed to have originated on the Hutt homeworld of Varl.
Vurk Meditation Orb: The orbs are perfect obsidian spheres, roughly 15 centimeters across. Studying the opaque sphere allows a Mystic to focus inwards, leaving the world behind. In this state, they see only the connections between beings, hear only the whispers of the Force. This meditation lasts for at least an hour, and for the 24 hours following this meditation, they may suffer 2 strain to add a Force point to the results of the next check to use the Alter or Battle Meditation Force power.
Vutalamine: Vutalamine is a common anti-anxiety drug that BioTech Industries produces and sells throughout the galaxy. A single dose of vue lasts for twenty-four hours, during that time, the character upgrades the ability of any checks made to resist stress or fear once. In addition, the difficulty of Ranged (Light) and Ranged (Heavy) checks made at long or extreme range is reduced by one. At the Game Master's discretion, frequent use of vue can cause impaired judgement and loss of memory, imposing a permanent setback die on Intellect- and Cunning-based checks.
W-series Destroyer Droid: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Skills: Athletics 2, Cool 2, Coordination 2, Gunnery 3. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat or drink, and can survive in vacuum or underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Fire Sweep (as an action, the droid can make a Hard difficulty Gunnery check to inflict one hit that deals base damage with the blaster cannons on up to two targets within Medium range, plus one additional target per advantage result generated by the check; it can only hit each target once this way), Overloaded Power Core (after generating three threat results or a despair result on a check with the blaster cannons, the shield projector may short out for the rest of the encounter), Roller (once per round as a maneuver, the droid can coil up into a ball; in this state, the droid can make an additional Move maneuver eac...
WESTAR-20 Blaster Pistol: The precursor to the WESTAR-34, which itself was a precursor to the modern WESTAR-35 used universally by the Mandalorians during the Clone Wars, this short barreled pistol was able to be heavily customised to the owner’s specifications. As one of Concordian Crescent Technologies earlier entries, it lacked the stun option that the pacifist Mandalorian government would eventually mandate. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
WESTAR-33 Blaster Pistol: While producing the WESTAR-34, they began production on a more experimental entry into their line of pistols. The WESTAR-33 was rather stocky and crude in design, distinguishing it from the early luxurious weapons of Concordian Crescent Technologies. Despite its outward appearance, this weapon was given no less attention by the Mandalorian engineers. Using a special tibanna blend and spin chamber, this pistol would produce a rare purple beam shot. The process of creating this beam when pulling the trigger was so instant that the engineers built-in a fairly light mechanism. This allowed for users to fire at much higher rates than a regular pistol and potentially land multiple killer shots on their target at once. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
WESTAR-34 Blaster Pistol: A luxurious weapon produced by Concordian Crescent Technologies, this blaster was produced with dallorian alloy, making it capable of withstanding the high temperatures of continual firing. This weapon had a short production run and was quickly discontinued when the Mandalorian government mandated stun settings in all weapons oriented for civilian sale. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
WESTAR-35 Blaster Pistol: The latest in the long-running WESTAR line, the WESTAR-35 is the standard-issue sidearm of Mandalorian law enforcement and military officers.
WESTAR-M5 Blaster Rifle: Developed by Concordian Crescent Technologies in an open tender for a lightweight improvement of the DC-15 blaster rifle, the WESTAR-M5 focused on making the weapon considerably lighter and more ergonomic. The weight reduction was achieved by increasing the height of the weapon and cutting its length to one of a carbine. It features an extendable stock for greater accuracy at long range. What resulted in the end was a weapon very similar to the eventual DC-15a produced by BlasTech in the same bidding process. While the Mandalorian company lost the army-wide bid, the WESTAR-M5 was still used by particular commando groups. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
WJ-880 Blinding Helmet: In the days of the former Republic and the Jedi Order, the WJ-990 blinding helmet was a common training aid for a Jedi Padawan, helping the aspirant to learn to sense the Force flowing through all things by cutting out sensory distractions.
WP-19 Incendiary Grenade: Merr-Sonn's WP-19 is a dangerous weapon that, upon detonation, releases flaming adhesive gel that clings to surfaces and merely spreads if wiped or hosed.
WSW Mk III \"Harrier\" Combat Remote: The WSW Mk III "Harrier" is a combat and training remote produced by Cybot Galactica. Skills (group only): Perception, Ranged (Heavy), Ranged (Light), Vigilance. Talents: None. Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat, or drink and can survive in vacuum and underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Flyer. Equipment: Repeating blaster (Ranged [Heavy]; Damage 10; Critical 3; Range [Long]; Auto-fire), stun blaster (Ranged [Light]; Damage 9; Critical 3; Range [Short]; Stun Damage).
WW-41 CryoBan Grenade: Merr-Sonn's CryoBan grenade uses a variety of chemicals also used in the carbon-freezing process to absorb most of the heat in a given area.
WX-series Destroyer Droid: This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook! Skills: Athletics 2, Cool 2, Coordination 2, Perception 1, Ranged (Heavy) 3. Talents: Deadly Accuracy 1 (add 3 damage to a successful hit of a Ranged [Heavy] check), True Aim 1 (upgrade the ability of a check in additional to the normal benefits of taking the Aim maneuver). Abilities: Droid (does not need to breathe, eat or drink, and can survive in vacuum or underwater; immune to poisons and toxins), Roller (once per round as a maneuver, the droid can coil up into a ball; in this state, the droid can make an additional Move maneuver each turn without spending strain and may exceed the two maneuver limit; as part of a Move maneuver, the droid can uncoil). Equipment: Integrated long-barrel heavy blaster rifle (Ranged [Heavy]; Damage 13; Critical 2; Range [Extreme]; Accurate 2, Pierce 2, Slow-Firing 1).
Walking Stick Hilt Disguise: Often, particularly elderly Jedi built their lightsabers into the body of a sturdy walking stick. This attachment can only be applied to lightsabers. Base Modifiers: Unless ignited, this weapon is completely indistinguishable from a regular walking stick, and a character must succeed on a Formidable difficulty Perception check or Vigilance check to notice that it is not. The weapon also gains the Inaccurate 1 item quality. If the weapon already has the Inaccurate quality, its Inaccurate quality increases by 1. Modification Options: 1 Decrease the value of the weapon's Inaccurate quality by 1.
Warde's Lightsaber: Suljo Warde's lightsaber is the weapon of a master swordsman, a blade with which great and terrible deeds were wrought. The crystal has had all of its mods applied, and the hilt has 0 open hard points.
Water Purifier: Water purifiers are made by countless wilderness supply and outfitting companies throughout the galaxy. [...] In ten minutes, a typical purifier can treat enough water to fulfill one sentient's needs for one day of rigorous activity.
Water Reclamation System: Water reclamation systems are popular with scouts and hermits, especially those living or working on arid worlds. This attachment can be applied to any armor that could logically integrate the necessary pouches and tubing. Base Modifiers: Upgrades the ability of any Resilience and Survival checks made in dry or hot environments due to scarcity of water twice. Modification Options: None.
Waterweave: Waterweave is a composite material created out of native Semblan alloys and minerals. Waterweave armor has encumbrance 7. However, while submerged, the armor's encumbrance changes to 0.
Wayfinder: Wayfinders are ancient pieces of technology created by both the Jedi and Sith as a means of navigating challenging stretches of space. They were created through the study of the brains of purrgil, space-faring megafauna who could travel through hyperspace by biological means. Both religions chose to imbue their wayfinders with the Force, requiring a Force-user to activate the wayfinder, so that secrecy and safety was ensured. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Weapon Detection Goggles: A weapon detector is a specialized sensor built into goggles that is able to detect the presence of explosives, the power cells used in blasters, and the chemical propellants used in slugthrowers, as well as many types of melee weapons, such as knives, swords, and stun batons.
Weapon Harness: Designed to allow a single sentient to field even the heaviest portable weapon with no support, weapon harnesses are common among heavy weapon squads.
Weapon Maintenance Kit: Produced by every major galactic arms manufacturer and many general tool and instrument makers, weapon maintenance kits are essential for keeping portable weapons operating under field conditions.
Weapon Signature Reduction: Both the Empire and the galactic security industry use weapon scanners that are capable of detecting a wide variety of weapon components.
Weapon Sling: Slings are lengths of hide or synthetic fabric designed to allow easy handling of long arms like blaster rifles. This attachment can only be used with Ranged (Heavy) weapons. Base Modifiers: Decrease Cumbersome rating by 1. Modification Options: 1 Innate Talent (Quick Draw) Mod.
Weequay Blaster Lance: Long-barreled blaster rifles designed for use in endless desert wastelands, blaster lances favor range and accuracy above all else.
Weighted Head: Bludgeoning weapons, such as truncheons, clubs, and staffs, deal damage through weight and impact. This attachment may only be used on bludgeoning Brawl and Melee weapons. Base Modifiers: Damage +1. Modification Options: 1 Damage +1 Mod, 1 Item Quality (Concussive +1) Mod.
Weik Greatsword: Regardless of technological advances, a simple metal sword remains an effective and reliable weapon. The greatsword requires two hands to wield.
Welding Rod: Technicians need not only the ability to cut metal apart quickly, but the capacity to fuse it back together. A welding rod adds an automatic success result to Mechanics checks to repair droids, vehicles, and starships. The GM may spend a despair result on any check made with a welding rod to inflict a Critical Injury on the user.
Whipcord Thrower: A whipcord thrower quickly deploys a length of high-tensile-strength cable, firing it over a distance in order to secure a target.
Whistling Birds: Whistling Birds are a Mandalorian weapon designed as a defense against multiple adversaries. A user designates targets and activates the weapon all through a helmet HUD, allowing the miniature guided missiles to be fired discreetly. This weapon can only be produced by Mandalorian armor smiths and is generally integrated into a vambrace or gauntlet. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Wilderness Survival Kit: Wilderness survival kits are a necessary precaution for those who regularly travel through inhospitable areas. Most survival kits include a thermal cloak, a multitool or survival knife, a distress beacon, two emergency comlinks, a spool of wire, ration bars, a basic medpac, two respirators, a water jug with filter, a glow rod, fifty meters of high-tensile microfiber line, ten ration packs, and an emergency flare gun.
Wing Commander Armored Flight Suit: The popular Wing Commander armored flight suit by Ayelixe/Krongbing Textiles is the latest advancement in pilot comfort and protection.
Wipe-3 Data-Purge Grenade: SoroSuub's Wipe-3 data-purge grenade erases electronically stored data from all devices within its blast radius with a brief, but intense, electromagnetic pulse.
Wizard Pouch: A wizard's pouch is essentially a bag with a shoulder strap; created for the "mages" of Weik to carry spell components. Wizard pouches increase a character's encumbrance threshold by 1.
Wrist Blaster: Wrist blasters are custom-made self-defense weapons worn by diplomats, espionage agents, businesspeople, and other individuals who need an inconspicuous personal defense weapon.
Wrist Mount: Originally developed to allow species without opposable thumbs to use pistols, a wrist mount ensures that their user always has a weapon ready while keeping their hands free.
X-30 \"Lancer\" Precision Blaster Pistol: SoroSuub Corporation's X-30 target blaster is used for recreational shooting throughout the galaxy.
X-8 Night Sniper Blaster: Highly customizable and discreet, yet powerful, BlasTech Industries' X-8 Night Sniper Blaster had developed a reputation as a reliable and effective self defense weapon for business people who may sometimes need some extra protection. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
X5R Heavy Demolitions Kit: The heavy carbo-plas case of an X5R heavy demolitions kit is filled with precision timers, remote detonators, and other vital tools of the trade for a dedicated explosives expert.
XCiter Overcharge: An XCiter overcharge allows an individual to empty a blaster's energy cell in a single, extremely powerful shot. While typically applied to holdout blasters, this attachment can be applied to any personal blaster weapon. Base Modifiers: As an incidental, shooters may overcharge the XCiter on their blaster. This gives the weapon the Prepare 1 item quality and increases its damage on the next attack by 4. Once it is fired, the weapon runs out of ammo and must be recharged. GMs may spend three threat results or a despair result from any combat check involving an overcharged shot with this weapon to cause the weapon to explode and deal its base damage to the shooter. Modification Options: None.
XL-2 \"Flashfire\" Light Blaster Pistol: The engineers at Talus Ironworks in the Corellian system created the XL-2 by accident when they test-fired a prototype with a faulty focusing aperture.
XLT-014 Labour Droid: Relative to most labour droids, the XLT-014 is a rather new model to the market. Produced by the renowned labour droid manufacturer Publictechnic, it was fitted with repulsorlifts in a humanoid appearance, allowing it to reach high areas while conserving power working on surfaces. Unlike most simple labour droids, the XLT-014 contains somewhat better programmed and makes fewer mistakes. This sometimes develops into a quirk of superiority, where the droid will start to make mistakes due to an overeager attempt to get through work faster. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
XV-09 Chemical Restraint Harness: The XV-09 Pacifier is one of the more uncommon products sold by Xenovet. Using a Pacifier on a wild animal is a temporary fix at best, and should only be done in emergencies. Mounting the harness on a creature requires a Hard difficulty Survival check to ensure the harness is fitted properly and the auto-hypo is situated correctly. The difficulty of this check can be reduced by 1 with a successful Knowledge (Xenology) check. If successful, the XV-09 grants two boost dice to any Survival checks made to control the beast wearing the harness. In addition, creatures that are successfully pacified gain a boost die to any Discipline checks. Game Masters can spend any threat result generated by the initial Survival check to reflect one or more negative side effects caused by the drugs. The exact nature of these side effects is left to the Game Master's discretion.
XV-20 Portable Veterinary Kit: The XV-20 is produced by Xenovet, a biotech firm based on Commenor known for its designer pets and respected line of veterinary equipment.
XV–38 Portable Large Veterinary Kit: Designed for use by veterinarians, beast breeders, or anyone who deals with large and dangerous animals for a living, the PLAV contains enough drugs, medical supplies, specialty tools, and other gear to stabilize wounded creatures in the field until they can be moved.
Xet Industries Model C \"Fiver\" Self-De: The Xet Model C incorporates a five-round internal cylinder magazine.
Yaladai Spice: Yaladai spice is a prized and expensive stimulant, worth many times the value of more common drugs, such as glitterstim, on the black market.
Yarrock: For untold ages, the spice called yarrock has been produced on Iridonia, created by their shamans to grant their warriors a greater clarity of mind and violent purpose. Restrictions: Yarrock is quite rare, and thus few planets explicitly regulate its use, as the only shipments go through black market traders.
Z-6 Jetpack: When a soldier needs to make a swift and unexpected arrival, a jetpack provides one of the quickest and most surprising methods.
Z-6 Rotary Blaster Cannon: The Z-6 rotary blaster cannon is an exceptionally potent heavy squad support weapon built for the GAR by BlasTech. This weapon can only be used with its Auto-fire item quality active. The GM may spend a despair result or two threat results from any combat check made with this weapon to have it overheat. When the weapon overheats, the wielder suffers 2 strain, and the weapon becomes damaged one step, from undamaged to minor, minor to moderate, or moderate to major.
Z-7 Jetpack: The latests in personal propulsion technology, the Mitrinomon Z-7 civilian jetpack includes an expanded fuel reservoir and advanced gyro-stabilizer in place of an integrated missile launcher. The Z-7 can sustain 5 kilometers of continuous flight at a maximum speed of 140 kilometers an hour. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Z2 Stun Baton: Resembling a typical police truncheon, the Z2 by Merr-Sonn packs an extra, often completely unexpected punch in the form of a concentrated stun blast.
Z50 Grenade Launcher: Malaxan Firepower Incorporated's Z50 is the industry standard when it comes to grenade launchers, and many planetary defense forces and mercenaries field the weapon..
Z6 Riot Control Baton: This extendable cudgel integrates a set of conductor contact vanes that can effectively subdue a target.
Zap Rod: Melee weapons usually seen in the hands of local security personnel, zap rods are designed to give an electric shock when one end is touched to an individual. They usually come equipped with a small slider, to change the level of electric shock, everything from a mild buzz to a bolt strong enough to cause semipermanent damage to many species. Cheap and effective, zap rods are often employed in riot control scenarios, though the weapons have been known to react poorly, and even fail completely, in adverse weather. Imperial personnel prefer to use the more expensive, but more reliable electrobaton in their riot control operations. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
Zephyr Stealth Suit: Stealth suits, produced by companies like Ayelix/Krongbing Textils and Creshaldyne, are designed to make a wearer difficult or impossible to detect by visual or technological means.
Zophis Crystal: In ancient days, the Outer Rim world of Zoph was well traveled by the Jedi, and some Padawans would sojourn to the distant world to seek their kyber crystal.
Zygerrian Energy Bow: The Zygerrian Empire is well known for their slaving operations and have developed an array of non-lethal weapons that they may use to take down escaping slaves or capturing new products. The Zygerrian energy bow is one of a handful of these exotic weapons. Pulling back on the energy link ring focuses a contained bolt for release. Unique among these weapons, the Zygerrian version uses an energy frequency tuned in a way that can disrupt biological processes instead of dealing damage. This can be swapped on the fly so that it deals more physical damage instead. This is a homebrew article from a community-produced homebrew sourcebook!
